gmw_00001502.overview: J1145-0340 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1309.2774] that is part of a galaxy cluster [arXiv:1309.2774]. On arcsecond scales, the source appears unresolved [arXiv:1309.2774], but it is not detected in VLBA images at 8 and 15 GHz [arXiv:1309.2774].

sources.summary: The paper gives J1145-0340 an individual BL Lac radio-structure entry, describing its arcsecond or VLBA morphology, flux measurements, gamma-ray association, and source classification.

quotes.quote: J1145-0340. This object is a part of a galaxy cluster (Piranomonte et al. 2007). On arcsecond scales, it appears unresolved with a total flux density of 18.7 mJy in NVSS data. In our VLBA images, the source is not detected above 1.2 mJy beam ^ 1 and 0.9 mJy beam ^ 1 at 8 and 15 GHz, respectively.

gmw_00001503.name: J1156+4238
gmw_00001503.overview: J1156+4238 is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1309.2774]. On arcsecond scales, the source is unresolved, while Very Long Baseline Array observations reveal a point-like radio structure [arXiv:1309.2774].

sources.summary: The paper gives J1156+4238 an individual BL Lac radio-structure entry, describing its arcsecond or VLBA morphology, flux measurements, gamma-ray association, and source classification.

quotes.quote: J1156+4238. On arcsecond scales, this object is unresolved with a total flux density of 15.6 mJy in the NVSS image. In our VLBA data, it shows a point-like structure with a total flux density of 2.6 mJy at 8 GHZ and _ core, tot 1.16. We measure SC for this BL Lac to be 0.17. No gamma-ray emission is found associated to this source.

gmw_00001504.name: J1201-0007
gmw_00001504.overview: J1201-0007 is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1309.2774]. At kiloparsec scales, the source is resolved into a double structure in the north-south direction [arXiv:1309.2774], though it remains undetected in high-frequency VLBA observations [arXiv:1309.2774].

sources.summary: The paper gives J1201-0007 an individual BL Lac radio-structure entry, describing its arcsecond or VLBA morphology, flux measurements, gamma-ray association, and source classification.

quotes.quote: J1201-0007. At kiloparsec-scales, this source is unresolved with a total flux density of 69.5 mJy in the NVSS image. The FIRST image shows that this source is resolved into a double structure in N-S direction with flux density peaks of 8.7 mJy Northern peak) and 17.5 mJy (Southern peak). In our VLBA data, it is undetected at both frequencies above 15.3 mJy beam ^ 1 at 8 GHz and 14.7 mJy beam ^ 1 at 15 GHz in agreement with the resolved arcsecond structure. No gamma-ray emission is found associated with this target.

gmw_00001505.name: J1201-0011
gmw_00001505.overview: J1201-0011 is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1309.2774]. On kiloparsec scales, the source is unresolved in radio observations, and it lacks any associated gamma-ray emission [arXiv:1309.2774].

sources.summary: The paper gives J1201-0011 an individual BL Lac radio-structure entry, describing its arcsecond or VLBA morphology, flux measurements, gamma-ray association, and source classification.

quotes.quote: J1201-0011. On kiloparsec-scales, this source is unresolved with a flux density of 28 mJy in the NVSS image. In our VLBA data, it is undetected at both frequencies above 0.6 mJy beam ^ 1 at 8 GHz and 3 mJy beam ^ 1 at 15 GHz. We measure SC to be 0.22. No gamma-ray emission is found associated to this target.

gmw_00001506.name: J1215+0732
gmw_00001506.overview: J1215+0732 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1309.2774] classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1309.2774]. On kiloparsec scales, it features a diffuse halo, while parsec-scale observations at 8 GHz reveal a jet extending to the east [arXiv:1309.2774].

sources.summary: The paper gives J1215+0732 an individual BL Lac radio-structure entry, describing its arcsecond or VLBA morphology, flux measurements, gamma-ray association, and source classification.

quotes.quote: J1215+0732. On kiloparsec-scales, a diffuse halo is present both in NVSS and FIRST images where a total flux density of 138.8 mJy and 81.8 mJy is revealed in NVSS and FIRST respectively. On parsec-scales, this object shows at 8 GHz a jet extending to the east (in agreement with the observations of Giroletti et al. 2004b, Rector et al. 2003 at 5 GHz) with a total flux density of 36.6 mJy.

gmw_00001507.name: J1217+3007
gmw_00001507.overview: J1217+3007 is a blazar [arXiv:2002.04119] classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2009.09039] that is hosted by a massive elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1701.04305] at a redshift of z = 0.130 [arXiv:2009.09039]. The source features a straight radio jet [arXiv:0711.1158] and exhibits multiwavelength variability, including optical outbursts [arXiv:1203.0490, arXiv:1606.06431] and very-high-energy gamma-ray flares [arXiv:2002.04119, arXiv:2401.11762].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1ES 1215+303 as a bright IBL detected from radio through VHE bands, with uncertain redshift and a VERITAS excess after special analysis.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1215+303 1ES 1215+303, also commonly referred to as ON 325, was first detected in the B2 408 MHz survey conducted with the Bologna Northern Cross telescope. It was detected at X-ray energies by the Einstein observatory. 1ES 1215+303 has been classified as a bright IBL based on a synchrotron peak located at 10 ^ 15.6 Hz . The redshift of the object is listed as z = 0.130 in NED but is uncertain, according to White et al. it is 0.237 .

1ES 1215+303 was first detected in the VHE band during a brief flare over a period of 4-nights by MAGIC in early January 2011. The VHE flux claimed during this flare was 2.0 of the Crab Nebula flux above 250 GeV.

However, since the east wobble position is 1.3 ^ away, it was not used for the analysis of data of 1ES 1215+303. On the other hand, the west wobble position is very close to the source, so this required a special analysis, namely the ring background model .

sources.summary: The paper reports the first MAGIC VHE detection of 1ES 1215+303, describing it as a high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lac/TeV blazar with multiwavelength variability and SSC modeling constraints.

quotes.quote: We study the VHE ray emission from 1ES 1215+303 and its relation to the emissions in other wavelengths. Triggered by an optical outburst, MAGIC observed the source in 2011 January-February for 20.3 hrs. The target was monitored in the optical R-band by the KVA telescope that also performed optical polarization measurements. We triggered target of opportunity observations with the Swift satellite and obtained simultaneous and quasi-simultaneous data from the Fermi Large Area Telescope and from the Mets ahovi radio telescope.

The MAGIC observations of 1ES 1215+303 carried out in 2011 January-February resulted in the first detection of the source at VHE with a statistical significance of 9.4 . Simultaneously, the source was observed in a high optical and X-ray state. In 2010 the source was observed in a lower state in optical, X-ray, and VHE, while the GeV ray flux and the radio flux were comparable in 2010 and 2011. The spectral energy distribution obtained with the 2011 data can be modeled with a simple one zone SSC model, but it requires extreme values for the Doppler factor or the electron energy distribution.

sources.summary: The paper studies 1ES 1215+303 as a TeV blazar with multiwavelength variability, aliases and coordinates, VHE and Fermi detections, a double-humped SED, and a measured redshift.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1215+303 R.A. = 12^h17^m52.0819^s , Dec. = +30^o07^ 00^ 635 , J2000; 2011AJ....142...89P , also known by many other names including Ton 605, ON 325, B2 1215+30 and S3 1215+30, is a blazar detected in the very-high-energy (VHE; 100 GeV) gamma-ray band. Blazars, of which there are, at the time of writing, 72 known to emit VHE radiation http://tevcat.uchicago.edu , are the most numerous sources detected at these energies comprising approximately one third of the VHE sources. 1ES 1215+303 was first discovered at VHE by MAGIC the Major Atmospheric Gamma Imaging Cherenkov; Aleksic12 and has been monitored by the Very Energetic Radiation Imaging Telescope Array (VERITAS) at TeV energies since 2008. The source exhibited one of the most luminous and large-amplitude flares E 90  GeV ever detected from a VHE blazar measured by VERITAS, when, in 2014, the TeV flux reached 2.4 times the Crab Nebula flux with a variability timescale of < 3.6 h Abeysekara17B21215 .

In the high-energy (HE; 100 MeV 500 GeV) gamma-ray band, 1ES 1215+303 has been detected by the Fermi Large Area Telescope (LAT), most recently as 4FGL J1217.9+3007 2019arXiv190210045T . A high-flux state correlated with that detected in the VHE band was observed at these energies during the luminous and isolated gamma-ray flare of 2014 Abeysekara17B21215 . 1ES 1215+303 exhibits a double-humped spectral energy distribution (SED) typical of blazars, with the synchrotron peak between radio and X-ray energies and the high-energy peak at GeV TeV energies. The synchrotron peak frequency of 1ES 1215+303 has been measured to be _ syn >10^ 15 Hz which led to its classification as either an intermediate-frequency-peaked BL Lac In the classification scheme of .

sources.summary: The paper presents 1ES 1215+303 as a confirmed z=0.130 BL Lac/VHE source, deriving HAWC flux and spectral fits while noting source-confusion concerns with PG 1218+304.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1215+303 is one of six ray emitting BL Lac objects located in the Northern part of the Coma Berenices constellation, five of them known to be VHE sources. The redshift of this HBL is now confirmed to be z=0.130 , discarding the early measurement z=0.237 by 1993ApJS...84..109L . The lower value was confirmed through optical spectroscopy at the GTC and, more recently, through the direct identification of a Ly emission line 2017ApJ...837..144P,2019AJ....157...41F . Catalogued as 3FHL J1217.9+3006, this object is well detected up to 500 GeV, and modeled with a power-law of index =2.3 0.1 .

The default HAWC search gave TS=11.36 for K=(4.64 1.38_ stat 0.70_ syst ) 10^ 12 TeV^ 1 cm^ 2 s^ 1 , as the intrinsic normalization. The optimized power-law fit for the intrinsic spectrum is rather softer, dN dE = (3.78 1.36_ stat 0.57_ syst ) 10^ 12 E 1 TeV ^ 3.07 0.37_ stat 0.15_ syst TeV^ 1 cm^ 2 s^ 1 , with TS=12.80 , representing a test statistic increase TS=1.44 . The attenuated photon flux is N_ obs (>0.5 TeV )=(2.53 1.35) 10^ 12 cm^ 2 s^ 1 . The integrated energy flux results in a luminosity per solid angle of L(>0.5 TeV )/ = (3.9 2.7) 10^ 43 erg s^ 1 sr^ 1 , given the luminosity distance of 585 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper presents 1ES 1215+303 as a high-energy-peaking BL Lac with uncertain redshift, a bright host galaxy, previous TeV upper limits, Fermi-LAT variability, and a 2011 MAGIC VHE detection.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1215+303 (also known as ON 325) is a high energy peaking BL Lac object. Two values can be found for its redshift in the literature: z=0.130, and z=0.237 http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ . However, since none of the references given for the redshift show the optical spectra, it is difficult to judge which is the most correct value. Nevertheless, 1ES 1215+303 has a bright host galaxy of magnitude R =16.24 (see e.g. ), which can be used for estimating the redshift. Following the methodology of we derive z=0.13 0.04 (Nilsson, 2011, priv. comm.), favoring the lower redshift value.

1ES 1215+303 was classified as promising candidate TeV blazar in and has been observed several times in VHE rays prior to the observations presented here, providing only upper limits (Whipple: F (>430 GeV )<1.89 10^ 11 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , MAGIC: F (>120 GeV )<3.5 10^ 11 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 ).

In the 2011 data, the preliminary analysis detected a number of excess events of N_ exc (>300 GeV )=110.4 13.6 , corresponding to a significance of 10.15 , which is the first significant detection of VHE rays from 1ES 1215+303 .

sources.summary: The paper uses ten years of Fermi-LAT and VERITAS gamma-ray spectra of the BL Lac blazar 1ES 1215+303 to constrain photon-ALP coupling.

quotes.quote: We present the limits on photon to axionlike particle (ALP) coupling from the 10-year period observations of the TeV BL Lacertae blazar 1ES 1215+303 (with redshift z=0.130 ). The contemporaneous gamma-ray spectra are measured by the collaborations Fermi-LAT and VERITAS with five flux phases from 2008 to 2017, including four low states and one flare. Using these flux phases, we show the spectral energy distributions (SEDs) under the null/ALP hypotheses and set the combined limit on ALP.

The source 1ES 1215+303 ( R.A.=12^h17^m52.0819^s , Dec.=+30^ 07'00''635 , J2000), also known as other names, e.g. , Ton 605, ON 325, B2 1215+30, and S3 1215+30, is classified as an intermediate-frequency-peaked BL Lac (IBL) or a high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lac (HBL) object, which was first discovered at VHE by the MAGIC telescopes . The redshift of this source was measured to be z=0.1305 0.0030 , and we take z=0.130 in this work. Recently, its long-term contemporaneous gamma-ray spectra are measured by the collaborations Fermi-LAT and the Very Energetic Radiation Imaging Telescope Array System (VERITAS) with several low state and flare flux phases from 2008 to 2017 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 1215+303 as having a large core-to-lobe ratio, a straight VLBI jet, and polarization detected in the core and inner jet in 1998.

quotes.quote: 1215+303 The 1.5 GHz VLA image of LaurentMuehleisen93 shows an unresolved core with a hint of extended emission towards the southwest. The core-to-lobe ratio is 14 , the largest in the HEAO-1 sample. Our 1993 image shows a short jet in PA 135 , and our 1998 image reveals a well-defined straight jet in this same position angle (Fig.  fig:1215 ). Polarization was detected only at the latter epoch, in both the core and inner jet. The inferred jet B field is aligned with the jet.

sources.summary: The paper resolves conflicting redshift claims for 1ES 1215+303 by detecting [O II] and [O III] emission at the low redshift value.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1215+303 : bade1998 reported a redshift z = 0.130 for this target, but no information about the detected lines are given. On the contrary white2000 showed an optical spectrum claiming a redshift of 0.237, although it appears featurless from their figure. A more recent spectrum (SNR = 60) obtained by ricci2015 was also found featurless. The target was clearly resolved in HST exposures ( scarpa2000 ) revealing a massive elliptical host galaxy, suggesting the source is at low redshift. Given these different redshift values, we secured a high quality optical spectrum (SNR 300) in which we detect two emission lines: [ OII ] 3727 , [ OIII ] 5007 at z = 0.131 (see also Table tab:line ) confirming the low redshift previously reported.

sources.summary: The paper gives J1217+3007 an individual BL Lac radio-structure entry, describing its arcsecond or VLBA morphology, flux measurements, gamma-ray association, and source classification.

quotes.quote: J1217+3007. This object is especially variable in the optical and polarized in optical and radio bands (Kollgaard et al. 1992, Fan Lin 1999, Giroletti et al. 2004). NVSS data reveal a point-like structure with a total flux density of 588 mJy.

sources.summary: The paper describes ON 325 as a VHE BL Lac source in its monitoring sample and reports its optical/radio flux level, variability trends, and correlation behavior.

quotes.quote: ON 325 : The average brightness in both bands are above the median and light curves show clear outbursts. Visually, the light curves seem to show an anti-correlation (see Fig. Fig:lc18 ) instead of a correlation with the radio flux density increasing when the optical is decreasing. Our analysis finds no common trend and DCF reveals no significant peaks and is therefore in agreement with this visual impression. Interestingly, at the time of the ray flare observed by Fermi LAT around MJD 54700 fermibright there was a major decaying outburst in the radio (gap in the optical light curve), while during the Very High Energy ray detection by MAGIC around MJD 55570 Alek12a , there was an major outburst in the optical, but not in the radio.

sources.summary: The paper gives 1ES 1215+303 an individual blazar variability note, summarizing TeV-band detections, GeV or hard-X-ray behavior, and variability classification.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1215+303 : The long-term VHE flux variability behavior of this source was recently studied by 2020ApJ...891..170V using VERITAS observations and significant flux variations were detected see also 2012A&A...544A.142A . The TeV band light curve has reveled several ray flaring episodes which were also found by the Bayesian block algorithm. The high activity was reflected in the GeV band light curve. Based on the estimated parameter, this object can be considered partially variable.

sources.summary: The paper summarizes long-term VERITAS and multiwavelength monitoring of the blazar 1ES 1215+303, including a 175.8-hour exposure and a synchrotron-peak shift during its 2017 flare.

quotes.quote: A high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lac (HBL) 1ES 1215+303 ( z =0.13, ) was extensively studied in a MWL context using long-term data from radio to ray energies. a VERITAS exposure of 175.8 h was used in this study, which includes regular monitoring observations since December 2008. The synchrotron peak frequency from a low state to the 2017 flaring state was observed to be shifted from infrared to soft X-ray .

gmw_00001508.name: J1221+0821
gmw_00001508.overview: J1221+0821 is a non-LAT BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1309.2774]. It exhibits a point-like kiloparsec structure with a total flux density of 178 mJy, but appears unresolved in 8 GHz VLBA imaging [arXiv:1309.2774].

sources.summary: The paper gives J1221+0821 an individual BL Lac radio-structure entry, describing its arcsecond or VLBA morphology, flux measurements, gamma-ray association, and source classification.

quotes.quote: J1221+0821. This source has a point-like kiloparsec structure with a total flux density of 178 mJy from NVSS maps. In our 8 GHz VLBA image, it appears unresolved with a total flux density of 17.3 mJy, while at 15 GHz it is not detected above 14.7 mJy beam ^ 1 . SC is 0.1. It is a non LAT BL Lac.

gmw_00001509.name: J1231+6414
gmw_00001509.overview: J1231+6414 is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1309.2774]. On kiloparsec scales, it exhibits an almost unresolved core with faint halo-like emission, while parsec-scale observations reveal a one-sided jet pointing to the north-west [arXiv:1309.2774].

sources.summary: The paper gives J1231+6414 an individual BL Lac radio-structure entry, describing its arcsecond or VLBA morphology, flux measurements, gamma-ray association, and source classification.

quotes.quote: J1231+6414. The kiloparsec-scale structure shows an almost unresolved core with a total flux density of 59.3 mJy in the NVSS (see also Perlman 1994). A faint emission from a halo-like feature is also observed. On parsec-scales at 8 GHz this source shows a one-sided jet pointing north-west. A similar structure has been found by Giroletti et al.

gmw_00001510.name: J1253+0326
gmw_00001510.overview: J1253+0326 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1309.2774] that exhibits an unresolved morphology in NVSS and FIRST radio images [arXiv:1309.2774]. On parsec scales, it displays a one-sided structure at 8 GHz and a point-like structure at 15 GHz [arXiv:1309.2774].

sources.summary: The paper gives J1253+0326 an individual BL Lac radio-structure entry, describing its arcsecond or VLBA morphology, flux measurements, gamma-ray association, and source classification.

quotes.quote: J1253+0326. This BL Lac shows an unresolved morphology both in NVSS and FIRST images with a total flux density of 107 and 79.2 mJy, respectively. On parsec-scales, at 8 GHz it appears one-sided with a core flux density of 29.5 mJy and a jet flux density of 7 mJy, while at 15 GHz it has a point-like structure with a total flux density of 27.1 mJy. SC is 0.34. No gamma-ray emission is found associated to this target.

gmw_00001511.name: J1257+2412
gmw_00001511.overview: J1257+2412 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1309.2774] classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1309.2774]. It is an extremely weak radio source that is only marginally resolved with the Very Large Array [arXiv:1309.2774].

sources.summary: The paper gives J1257+2412 an individual BL Lac radio-structure entry, describing its arcsecond or VLBA morphology, flux measurements, gamma-ray association, and source classification.

quotes.quote: J1257+2412. This extremely weak object is only marginally resolved with the VLA with a total flux density of 13.1 mJy in NVSS data. It was barely detected by the VLBA ( 3 mJy core) at 5 GHz (Giroletti et al. 2004b). In our VLBA images at 8 and 15 GHz, no emission is revealed above 0.9 mJy beam ^ 1 at both frequencies.

gmw_00001512.name: J1341+3959
gmw_00001512.overview: J1341+3959 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1205.4939, arXiv:1309.2774] that exhibits a compact, point-like core structure in radio observations [arXiv:1205.4939, arXiv:1309.2774]. The source is a gamma-ray emitter that has shown a decrease in flux by a factor of three and a softer-when-brighter spectral trend [arXiv:1809.01173].

sources.summary: The paper finds that RXS J1341+3959 has an accurate VLBI/MERLIN position offset from NED and appears compact in both EVN and MERLIN imaging.

quotes.quote: Our results show that the accurate position for RXS J1341+3959 and RXS J1410+6100 are significantly different from the positions provided by NED, with position difference larger than 10^ '' . The accurate positions for these two sources can only be derived from our MERLIN data (see Table table_position ), which however, are much closer to that from the Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty Centimeters (FIRST) 1.4-GHz radio catalogue bec95 , with a position difference less than 0^ '' .3 and 0^ '' .5 , respectively.

The total flux at FIRST 1.4 GHz for this source is around 39.9 mJy. Both the EVN and MERLIN images show a compact core, which is presented in Fig. J . The image parameters are shown in Table table_img .

sources.summary: The paper gives J1341+3959 an individual BL Lac radio-structure entry, describing its arcsecond or VLBA morphology, flux measurements, gamma-ray association, and source classification.

quotes.quote: J1341+3959. This source is unresolved both in NVSS and FIRST maps with a total flux density of 85.6 mJy and 57.9 mJy, respectively. At parsec-scales, it shows a point-like structure with a core flux density of 6.3 mJy at 8 GHz and 5.8 mJy at 15 GHz. SC is 0.07. It does not have a counterpart in the 2 LAC.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1341+3959 as a gamma-ray flux outlier, with flux down by a factor of three, a possible photon-index change, softer-when-brighter behavior, and LD classification.

quotes.quote: The most prominent outliers are: J1419+5423, whose flux has increased by 60 , and J1341+3959, whose flux decreased by a factor of three when considering 8.5 years of LAT data.

Only for J1341+3959 there is a hint of change of the photon index, from _ 3FGL = 2.54 0.18 to _ 8.5-yr = 2.16 0.12. In general, variations in ray flux and in photon index are not correlated. Neither softer-when-brighter nor harder-when-brighter trends are found, as shown by the comparison between the ray flux ratio and the photon index variation presented in Fig. fig:phvsflulum2 , with the exception of J1341+3959 that shows a softer-when-brighter trend.
Among the 23 LAT-detected sources, 20 are classified as DD, and 3 as LD (J0847 + 1133, J1341 + 3959, and J1534 + 3715).

gmw_00001513.name: UGC 3995
gmw_00001513.overview: UGC 3995 is an overlapping pair of galaxies [arXiv:1505.03949] whose centers are separated by approximately 30 arcseconds [arXiv:astro-ph/0703552]. The smaller component B is positioned in front of the spiral disk of component A [arXiv:astro-ph/0703552], and the pair is likely undergoing an interaction [arXiv:1505.03949].

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 3995A+B as a tide-quiet close-pair example, citing separated centers, unperturbed photometry, symmetric kinematics, and small optical and H I velocity differences.

quotes.quote: They studied the close pair UGC 3995A+B (CPG 140, in Karachentsev 1972, 1987), whose POSS-II B band image is shown in Fig. cpg140 . Galaxy centers are separated by approximately 30 , and the smaller component B is seen in front of the spiral disk of component A. Marziani et al. proceeded to broadband photometry decomposition and found unperturbed individual solutions for both galaxies. Optical spectroscopy reveals symmetrical kinematics relative to the galaxy centers. That is, the rotation-curve amplitudes are the same on the receding and approaching sides of both galaxies.

The H i global profile is symmetrical as well. The optical velocity difference is 20 km s ^ 1 (internal error of 18 km s ^ 1 ), and the H i velocity difference is 1 km s ^ 1 , from Sulentic Arp . These observations suggest that the pair is a CP III, a ``tide-quiet'' close pair.

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 3995 as an occulting-galaxy dust template and notes that the overlapping pair is likely interacting, affecting its extinction distribution.

quotes.quote: UGC 3995 This pair of galaxies has been long known to be overlapping and interacting e.g., Marziani99 and we have analyzed the extinction properties of both CALIFA DR1 IFU data and archival WFPC2 F606W imaging in . Interactions Occulting galaxies are often interacting and the redshift difference for two of our templates (UGC 3995 and 2MASX) are such that an ongoing interaction cannot be ruled out.
It is very likely in the case of UGC 3995. In the case of UGC 3995, one could contribute this to the ongoing interaction.

gmw_00001514.name: ESO 507-G025
gmw_00001514.overview: ESO 507-G025 is an early-type brightest group galaxy that exhibits non-regular rotation [arXiv:2206.13215]. It hosts a weak active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2208.14115], a nuclear rotating disk [arXiv:2201.07838], a misaligned ring structure [arXiv:2201.07838], and gas suggesting an outflow [arXiv:2208.14115].

sources.summary: ESO 507-25 has a nuclear rotating disk, a misaligned ring with a smooth velocity gradient, two southwest ionized-gas tails, and likely molecular disk signatures.

quotes.quote: From double-horned HI and CO profiles, osullivan18b reported that some sources (e.g., NGC 924, NGC 940 and ESO 507-25) likely host molecular disks.

ESO 507-25 -- This galaxy shows a nuclear rotating disk centered on the galaxy, with a velocity gradient from + 150 to 250 km s ^ 1 from the SE to the NW extending along 2.2 kpc ( 10.2 ). A ring structure is found misaligned with the inner disk, which exhibits a smooth velocity gradient from 260 to + 210 km s ^ 1 from the SE to the NW across 15 kpc of extension. Also, two tails of ionized gas are extending out of the ring to the SW moving at 160 km s ^ 1 and 90 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses ESO0507-G025 as a type-ii example, finding weak-AGN indications, two independent rotating gas structures, bicone-shaped nuclear velocity dispersion, and AGN/LINER gas suggesting an outflow.

quotes.quote: Only in ESO0507-G025 and NGC 677 do we see clear indications of AGN activity in the nuclear region from the WHAN diagram, with the EW(H ) 3--6 indicating a weak AGN).

In Figure fig:ESO0507_velocity we see an example of these velocity fields for the galaxy ESO0507-G025. Clearly, this galaxy has two independent velocity structures or rotating discs, while the velocity dispersion in the nuclear region shows a bicone-shaped structure which increases in velocity dispersion with distance from the centre. The gas in these bicone structures is located in the AGN/LINERs region of the BPT diagram (see Figures fig:example_figure and fig:figures_BPT_int ) suggesting an outflow perpendicular to the observer.

sources.summary: The paper discusses ESO 507-G025 as a brightest-group early-type galaxy, using MUSE kinematics and multiwavelength context to assess rotation, shape, gas, or activity.

quotes.quote: An example of a spectrum extracted from a central bin in ESO 507-G025 is shown in Figure fig:Spectra . We show the rest of the maps ( , h_ 3 , h_ 4 , and ) for ESO 507-G025 in Appendix OtherKINmaps as an example, and the maps for the other 17 BGEs are included as supplementary information. Non-regular rotation (NRR) is the most common characteristic of the Krajnovic2018 M3G BCGs, whereas we find it for only 8/18 BGEs: ESO 507-G025, NGC 193, NGC 410, NGC 677, NGC 777, NGC 1060, NGC 5846, as well as NGC 4261 (which shows non-regular rotation around the major axis, as reported and discussed by Davies1986 ).

gmw_00001515.name: NGC 410
gmw_00001515.overview: NGC 410 is a giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1412.6556, arXiv:2111.02430] that serves as the central dominant galaxy of the LGG 18 group [arXiv:2111.02430]. It exhibits a weak hint of coherent stellar rotation [arXiv:2201.07838], a stream of ionized gas [arXiv:2201.07838], and a northeastern radio emission extension [arXiv:2111.02430].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 410 as a Type-I shell galaxy candidate, using SDSS/Galfit photometry and visual shell counts to estimate MOND asymptotic circular and shell-expansion velocities.

quotes.quote: In this section, we roughly estimate the asymptotic circular and shell expansion velocities for several real Type-I shell galaxies assuming MOND gravity. The shells in galaxies NGC 3923 (e.g., prieur88 ) and NGC 7600 (e.g., turnbull ) have been known for a long time. In spite of their prominence, the shells in NGC 410 and NGC 3099 have not been reported yet. For obtaining the surface density profile of NGC 3923, we fitted the profile to its near-infrared image taken by the Spitzer space telescope. We used the same data, M/L ratio, and the galaxy distance from Earth as in bil13 .

For NGC 7600, NGC 410, and NGC 3099, we started from their SDSS (Sloan Digital Sky Survey, see, e.g., sdssdr10 ) images in the g , , and i bands. We fitted each of them by the one-component profile in Galfit Penggalfit . The integrated magnitudes in the g , , and i bands, and the corresponding color indices were determined from these data. The approximate M/L ratios were obtained from the color indices using the tables of sdssml . We assumed a constant M/L across the whole volume of the galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 410 as a giant elliptical CDG with point-source radio emission and a lower-frequency northeastern extension spanning about 10 kpc.

quotes.quote: NGC 410* Another galaxy from our VLA A configuration project is a giant elliptical NGC 410 (Figure fig:chandra_vla_ps_a ), which is the CDG of the LGG 18 group kolokythas2018 . \ The radio emission with a point source-like morphology was previously observed with the VLA at various frequencies condon1998,filho2002 . The more recent study by at lower frequencies (610 MHz) with the GMRT found a small extension of the radio emission to north-east with a total extent 10 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 410 lacks clear stellar rotation but has a weak coherent rotation hint and hosts a smooth NE-SW ionized-gas stream.

quotes.quote: Contrary to the random motions of the stellar component in BCGs e.g., hamer16 , the kinematics of the stellar component lack clear net stellar rotation in only 4 sources of the sample (NGC 193, NGC 410, NGC 1060, and NGC 5846). Particularly in NGC 5846, there is a very weak hint of coherent stellar rotation in the inner region of the galaxy (West approaching, East receding), as well as in NGC 410 (Northwest receding, and Southeast approaching).
NGC 410 -- hosts a stream of ionized gas with a smooth gradient going from NE to SW with a velocity from + 50 to 400 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 410 as a brightest-group early-type galaxy, using MUSE kinematics and multiwavelength context to assess rotation, shape, gas, or activity.

quotes.quote: We have three objects, NGC 410, NGC 777 and NGC 1060, in common with the MASSIVE sample Ma2014 and we compare some of our derived properties in Appendix Section:comparison . Non-regular rotation (NRR) is the most common characteristic of the Krajnovic2018 M3G BCGs, whereas we find it for only 8/18 BGEs: ESO 507-G025, NGC 193, NGC 410, NGC 677, NGC 777, NGC 1060, NGC 5846, as well as NGC 4261 (which shows non-regular rotation around the major axis, as reported and discussed by Davies1986 ). We can see that the six least luminous BGEs are fast rotators, and the three most luminous BGEs are slow rotators (NGC 410, NGC 777, and NGC 1060), and an overlap between the two classes over a broad range of luminosities in between.

gmw_00001516.name: NGC 584
gmw_00001516.overview: NGC 584 is a fast-rotating early-type galaxy [arXiv:1902.10175] and the brightest member of a small galaxy cluster [arXiv:1712.05833]. It hosts a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:1902.10175] and exhibits a highly perturbed, clumpy ionized-gas distribution with radial filaments [arXiv:2201.07838].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 584 as one of six nearby fast-rotating early-type galaxies with new SINFONI-based dynamical modeling and reports a Schwarzschild black-hole mass of (1.3 0.5) 10^8 M_.

quotes.quote: We add six new black hole mass (M _ BH ) measurements of nearby fast rotating early-type galaxies to the known black hole mass sample, namely NGC 584, NGC 2784, NGC 3640, NGC 4570, NGC 4281 and NGC 7049. Our target galaxies have effective velocity dispersions ( _ e ) between 170 and 245 km s ^ 1 , and thus this work provides additional insight into the black hole properties of intermediate-mass early-type galaxies. We combine high-resolution adaptive-optics SINFONI data with large-scale MUSE, VIMOS and SAURON data from ATLAS ^ 3D to derive two-dimensional stellar kinematics maps. We then build both Jeans Anisotropic Models and axisymmetric Schwarzschild models to measure the central black hole masses. Our Schwarzschild models provide black hole masses of (1.3 0.5) 10^8 M_ for NGC 584, (1.0 0.6) 10^8 M_ for NGC 2784, (7.7 5) 10^7 M_ for NGC 3640, (5.4 0.8) 10^8 M_ for NGC 4281, (6.8 2.0) 10^7 M_ for NGC 4570 and (3.2 0.8) 10^8 M_ for NGC 7049 at 3 confidence level, which are consistent with recent M _ BH _ e scaling relations.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 0584 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: This is the brightest member of a small cluster containing mostly E galaxies 1961AJ.....66..562V . It is classified as a E4 in the NED catalog, but seems to contain a disk structure 1994A&AS..105..481M . Our PN kinematic data show that the galaxy halo rotates with at least 50 along the photometric major axis, and has a velocity dispersion of 100 . The galaxy has central ellipticity 0.4 which falls to 0.1 at 150'' 5R_e from whereon increases again.

sources.summary: NGC 584 shows clear stellar rotation, a gas velocity field mildly dominated by rotation, and a perturbed clumpy ionized-gas distribution with radial filaments.

quotes.quote: The stellar velocity fields reveal that most of the sources in our sample (10/18 sources, e.g., NGC 940, NGC 924, NGC 1453, NGC 4169, NGC 978, NGC 584, NGC 1587, NGC 4008, NGC 7619 and NGC 6658) show a clear large-scale rotation pattern in their stellar light.

Note that 3/18 of the extended disk-like sources also have elongated structures arising from the main disk, and one source (NGC 584) shows a smooth velocity field but a highly disturbed gas distribution.

NGC 584 -- has a velocity field slightly dominated by rotation, with a velocity gradient from + 150 to 300 km s ^ 1 in the WE direction along with 5 kpc (50 ) in extension. However, the distribution of the ionized gas is quite perturbed and clumpy, with a few radial filaments.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 584 as a brightest-group early-type galaxy, using MUSE kinematics and multiwavelength context to assess rotation, shape, gas, or activity.

quotes.quote: With the exception of NGC 5846 (0.8 R_ e ), all our maps cover at least 1.5 R_ e . We indicate close companions or objects in the line-of-sight with a black solid line or ellipse (for NGC 5846 where these areas fall within the region where the effective parameters were measured, these areas were masked during the measurements). Various kinematic features are evident upon visual inspection of the V and kinematic maps: Rotation Despite being massive early-type galaxies, all our BGEs show some level of rotation, as the MUSE data quality is such that even very little net rotation can be detected (e.g. \ NGC 193, NGC 5846).

gmw_00001517.name: NGC 677
gmw_00001517.overview: NGC 677 is a brightest-group early-type galaxy [arXiv:2206.13215] characterized by low ellipticity [arXiv:1403.4627] and weak, non-regular stellar rotation [arXiv:2201.07838, arXiv:2206.13215]. The galaxy hosts a chaotic gas velocity field [arXiv:2201.07838] and a complex system of filamentary and clumpy structures [arXiv:2201.07838, arXiv:2206.13215].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 677 as a difficult fast-rotator case with minimal large-radius rotation, low ellipticity, and a rising outer dispersion profile along the minor axis.

quotes.quote: However, the two most massive galaxies in our sample, NGC 677 and UGC 4051, do show significant, albeit small ( 5 above _ R_e ) rises in dispersion.

There are three remaining MC FRs (NGC 677, shown in the top of Figure Fig:KinematicsSetGalb , NGC 3837 and IC 301), and these are the most difficult to classify. While technically they satisfy the FR criterion, their _R values are borderline between FRs and SRs, and they do not rotate at all beyond 1.5-2 R_e . From Figure Fig:LambdaTransition , we see that all of these are low ellipticity galaxies, which never rotate particularly fast in the sense that (R_e) 0.15 . NGC 677 exemplifies this class. NGC 677 also has an interesting outer dispersion profile that appears to start to rise again at around 3 R_e (10 kpc) on both sides of the galaxy along the minor axis, as has been seen in a few central galaxies see e.g.

sources.summary: NGC 677 is described as a filamentary system with complex central clumps, overlapping spiral-like southwest filaments, faint northeast clumps, weak stellar rotation, and a chaotic gas velocity field.

quotes.quote: Another 3 sources exhibit weaker signatures of stellar rotation (NGC 777, NGC 677, and NGC 4261) in their stellar velocity fields.

Clear examples of very chaotic velocity fields can be seen in NGC 677, NGC 4008, and NGC 5846. In some cases, velocity gradients along the filaments are detected, but the gas is quite disturbed on average.

NGC 677 -- host complex set of filaments, with several clumpy structures at the center. A set of 5.6 kpc (15 ) overlapping filaments is found at southwest of the center that appears to be spiraling moving with a velocity of 180 km s ^ 1 and 270 km s ^ 1 , and to the southeast with velocities of 150 km s ^ 1 . A faint 9.5 kpc(25 ) clumpy structures are seen at northeast of the galaxy center with similar velocities.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 677 as a brightest-group early-type galaxy, using MUSE kinematics and multiwavelength context to assess rotation, shape, gas, or activity.

quotes.quote: Non-regular rotation (NRR) is the most common characteristic of the Krajnovic2018 M3G BCGs, whereas we find it for only 8/18 BGEs: ESO 507-G025, NGC 193, NGC 410, NGC 677, NGC 777, NGC 1060, NGC 5846, as well as NGC 4261 (which shows non-regular rotation around the major axis, as reported and discussed by Davies1986 ). Here 3 out of the 18 BGEs (NGC 193, NGC 677, and NGC 1060) show > 75 (``minor axis" or ``prolate-like" rotation). Studying the thermodynamical properties of the X-ray atmospheres, Olivares2022 find that the filamentary structures and compact disks (the morphology of the ionised gas) are found in systems with small central entropy values, short t_ cool /t_ ff and t_ cool /t_ eddy ratios (NGC 193, NGC 410, NGC 677, NGC 777, NGC 978, NGC 1060, NGC 1587, NGC 4008, NGC 4261, NGC 5846, NGC 7619).

gmw_00001518.name: NGC 777
gmw_00001518.overview: NGC 777 is a giant elliptical galaxy and the brightest member of the LGG 42 group [arXiv:2111.02430, arXiv:2206.13215]. It exhibits weak stellar rotation [arXiv:2201.07838], central ionized-gas rotation [arXiv:2201.07838], clumpy filaments [arXiv:2201.07838], and compact radio emission [arXiv:2111.02430].

sources.summary: The paper used NGC 777 as a completeness-test field, modeling radial recovery fractions and sky-brightness dependence to set completeness corrections for the full sample.

quotes.quote: In order to accurately count GCs in all areas of our images, we used the DOLPHOT artificial-star test function to perform an extensive completeness study on three of the fields. We created twelve radial zones centered on each galaxy---circular zones for NGC 1016, and elliptical zones of constant eccentricity for NGC 57 and NGC 777 as seen in the top of Figure fig:zones . We added 3500 artificial stars to each zone, and determined the artificial star recovery fraction for each zone as a function of magnitude. We then modeled the recovery fraction in each zone as a sigmoid curve harris2016sigmoid : f_ comp = 1 1+e^ (m-m_0)

After determining a constant for both filters, we found m_0 for each of the twelve zones and compared it to background brightness. The relation is well fitted with an exponential curve (see Figure fig:m_0_sky for the results from NGC 777). We found that all three of our test galaxies produced similar relations in both filters (Figure fig:all_m_0_sky ), as expected since the filters and exposure times were essentially identical for all the fields, so we adopted the average exponential parameters for m_0 as functions of local sky brightness for our entire sample of galaxies: m_ 0, F 475 X = -0.05497 _ F475X ^ 0.6024 + 30.17 m_ 0, F 110 W = -0.003423 _ F110W ^ 0.6570 + 27.37 This sky brightness-dependent completeness relation allowed us to more accurately characterize completeness for GCs throughout our images, regardless of how close they were to the centers of their host galaxies.

sources.summary: NGC 777 shows weak stellar rotation, central ionized-gas rotation, NW and NE clumpy filaments, and a possible X-ray cavity projected near the NE H filament.

quotes.quote: Another 3 sources exhibit weaker signatures of stellar rotation (NGC 777, NGC 677, and NGC 4261) in their stellar velocity fields.

The shallow X-ray Chandra observations of NGC 777 reveal the presence of a potential X-ray cavity on the hot atmosphere detected by panagoulia14b , located in projection near the NE H filament (see Fig.  fig:chandra ). The 4.85 GHz VLA and 610 MHz GMRT observations show no indication of jets kolokythas18 . However, ho01 found a slightly resolved radio emission at 5 GHz, which appears to be aligned with the direction of the SE-NW ionized filaments.

NGC 777 -- there is indication of rotation along the SE--NW axis at the center, with a velocity gradient between 90 km s ^ 1 to 250  km s ^ 1 . The peak is followed by a 7.5 kpc (21 ) NW clumpy filament moving at about 170 km s ^ 1 , and a NE filament of 1.8 kpc (5 ) on size filament moving with a velocity of 270 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper reports compact radio emission from NGC 777, a giant elliptical CDG, in new VLA A data and non-detection in archival C data.

quotes.quote: NGC 777* Compact radio emission is detected in our new VLA A configuration total intensity image with a resolution of 1.5 arcsec for NGC 777 (Figure fig:chandra_vla_a h), the giant elliptical galaxy and the CDG of LGG 42 group. This compact morphology was also detected by with GMRT. \ Additionally, we also imaged the archival VLA C configuration data, where no radio emission was detected at the angular resolution of 3.2 arcsec, possibly due to the flux density being very close to the sensitivity limit of the VLA observations (see Section sec:radio_central ).

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 777 as a brightest-group early-type galaxy, using MUSE kinematics and multiwavelength context to assess rotation, shape, gas, or activity.

quotes.quote: We have three objects, NGC 410, NGC 777 and NGC 1060, in common with the MASSIVE sample Ma2014 and we compare some of our derived properties in Appendix Section:comparison . Non-regular rotation (NRR) is the most common characteristic of the Krajnovic2018 M3G BCGs, whereas we find it for only 8/18 BGEs: ESO 507-G025, NGC 193, NGC 410, NGC 677, NGC 777, NGC 1060, NGC 5846, as well as NGC 4261 (which shows non-regular rotation around the major axis, as reported and discussed by Davies1986 ). We can see that the six least luminous BGEs are fast rotators, and the three most luminous BGEs are slow rotators (NGC 410, NGC 777, and NGC 1060), and an overlap between the two classes over a broad range of luminosities in between.

gmw_00001519.name: NGC 924
gmw_00001519.overview: NGC 924 is a brightest-group early-type galaxy classified as a fast, regular rotator [arXiv:2206.13215]. It features a complex rotating gas disk surrounded by a clumpy outer ring and inflowing or outflowing gas clumps [arXiv:2201.07838].

sources.summary: NGC 924 contains a complex rotating gas disk with ordered inner velocities, a clumpy outer ring, and inflowing or outflowing clumps around the disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 924 -- NGC 924 shows a more complex kinematics. Ordered velocity structures are found along the inner 9 kpc ( 30 ) of the galaxy with a velocity range from 260 to + 110 km s ^ 1 from SE to NW. Around the rotating disk, a clumpy outer ring of gas is moving from + 170 to 280 km s ^ 1 located at 7.5 kpc (25 ) of the central disk. A few clumps located SE and NW of the disk are inflowing/outflowing of the inner disk with a velocity of 270 km s ^ 1 and + 120 km s ^ 1 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 924 as a brightest-group early-type galaxy, using MUSE kinematics and multiwavelength context to assess rotation, shape, gas, or activity.

quotes.quote: We find the majority (10/18) of our BGEs to be regular rotators (RR): NGC 584, NGC 924, NGC 940, NGC 978, NGC 1453, NGC 1587, NGC 4008, NGC 4169, NGC 6658, and NGC 7619. Point-like morphology in X-ray for the hot gas component is found in three of our BGEs with H rings (NGC 924, NGC 940, and NGC 4169), whereas ESO 507-G025 has no hot gas component, potentially indicating that the cold gas is more likely to be the result of gas-rich mergers or tidal interactions instead of cooling from a hot IGrM. Of the overlap with this study, two of these star-forming BGEs are fast rotators (NGC 924 and NGC 940) and one is a slow rotator (ESO 507-G025) that can also be classified as a fast rotator when inclination is taken into account.

gmw_00001520.name: NGC 940
gmw_00001520.overview: NGC 940 is an early-type galaxy classified as a brightest group galaxy and a regular rotator [arXiv:2206.13215]. It hosts a smooth, 11-kiloparsec rotating ionized-gas disk, a smaller inner nuclear disk, and a co-moving cold molecular gas disk [arXiv:2201.07838].

sources.summary: NGC 940 has a smooth 11 kpc rotating ionized-gas disk, a smaller inner nuclear disk, double-horned gas profiles, and a co-moving cold molecular disk.

quotes.quote: From double-horned HI and CO profiles, osullivan18b reported that some sources (e.g., NGC 924, NGC 940 and ESO 507-25) likely host molecular disks.

NGC 940 -- A smooth rotating disk of gas is detected centered on the AGN in NGC 940, with the gas moving from 300 to + 170 km s ^ 1 from NW to SE across 11 kpc. An inner nuclear disk of 2.5 kpc (7 ) is located in the inner region of the galaxy with a slightly different PA that the main extended rotating disk.

Our preliminary NOrthern Extended Millimeter Array (NOEMA) observations of NGC 940 reveal a cold molecular gas disk (Olivares et al. in prep), which appears to be co-spatial and co-moving with the optical emission lines in a rotating disk structure.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 940 as a brightest-group early-type galaxy, using MUSE kinematics and multiwavelength context to assess rotation, shape, gas, or activity.

quotes.quote: We find the majority (10/18) of our BGEs to be regular rotators (RR): NGC 584, NGC 924, NGC 940, NGC 978, NGC 1453, NGC 1587, NGC 4008, NGC 4169, NGC 6658, and NGC 7619. Here, we find that all of the h_ 3 versus V/ anti-correlation slopes of our fast rotating BGEs are around -0.1 as expected, with the steepest being NGC 940 (--0.15) and NGC 7619 (--0.16). NGC 940 has _ e 0.6 , therefore NGC 7619 (with a fairly low _ e 0.14 ) is the only BGE that can potentially be similar to the Class D galaxies in Naab2014 .

gmw_00001521.name: NGC 978
gmw_00001521.overview: NGC 978 is a brightest-group early-type galaxy classified as a regular rotator [arXiv:2206.13215]. It features a compact ionized-gas distribution with small tails [arXiv:2201.07838], which likely condensed from the cooling of its intragroup medium [arXiv:2201.07838, arXiv:2206.13215].

sources.summary: NGC 978 has a compact ionized-gas distribution, a smooth 3.6 kpc velocity gradient, small disk-following tails, and evidence for condensation from its intragroup medium.

quotes.quote: NGC 978 -- The gas distribution in NGC 978 is a very compact. NGC 978 has a smooth velocity gradient from 210 to + 180 km s ^ 1 in the EW direction along 3.6 kpc ( 11 ). Small tails of ionized gas are detected to the SW and NE of the central disk following the kinematics of the disk.

In the compact rotating disk systems, NGC 978 and NGC 4261, both low central entropy values, K_0 and the cooling time to the eddy turnover time ratios, t_ cool /t_ eddy , suggest that the warm ionized gas condensed through the cooling of the IGrM.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 978 as a brightest-group early-type galaxy, using MUSE kinematics and multiwavelength context to assess rotation, shape, gas, or activity.

quotes.quote: PA _ phot from the 2MASS XSC agrees with our MUSE white-light images (obtained by adding the MUSE cube along the wavelength axis) as shown in Figure fig:Flux , except for NGC 978 where 2MASS gives 115 (white solid line), but we find PA _ phot 75 (white dashed line). We find the majority (10/18) of our BGEs to be regular rotators (RR): NGC 584, NGC 924, NGC 940, NGC 978, NGC 1453, NGC 1587, NGC 4008, NGC 4169, NGC 6658, and NGC 7619. Studying the thermodynamical properties of the X-ray atmospheres, Olivares2022 find that the filamentary structures and compact disks (the morphology of the ionised gas) are found in systems with small central entropy values, short t_ cool /t_ ff and t_ cool /t_ eddy ratios (NGC 193, NGC 410, NGC 677, NGC 777, NGC 978, NGC 1060, NGC 1587, NGC 4008, NGC 4261, NGC 5846, NGC 7619).

gmw_00001522.name: NGC 1060
gmw_00001522.overview: NGC 1060 is a luminous, slow-rotating early-type galaxy [arXiv:2206.13215] that lacks clear net stellar rotation [arXiv:2201.07838]. The galaxy hosts a small-scale radio jet and features clumpy ionized filaments with smooth velocity gradients [arXiv:2201.07838].

sources.summary: NGC 1060 lacks clear net stellar rotation, has a clumpy ionized filament with a smooth velocity gradient, and hosts a small-scale jet possibly linked to filament formation.

quotes.quote: Contrary to the random motions of the stellar component in BCGs e.g., hamer16 , the kinematics of the stellar component lack clear net stellar rotation in only 4 sources of the sample (NGC 193, NGC 410, NGC 1060, and NGC 5846).

NGC 1060 hosts a small-scale jet, although the low spatial resolution of XMM-Newton observations are insufficient to resolve any X-ray cavities at the scale of the jets kolokythas18 . Nevertheless, the distribution of the SE filament seems to be spatially linked to one of the radio lobes, raising the possibility that AGN outburst could have triggered the formation of the cold gas and produced the smooth velocity gradient in the SE clumpy filament (see Fig.  fig:nii_maps_examples_app ).

NGC 1060 -- Similarly, NGC 1060 have a smooth velocity gradient along its filament length. This clumpy ionized filament is moving to the EW with a velocity from + 180 km s ^ 1 at the peak of the emission to 130 km s ^ 1 , followed by another NE clumpy filament moving from 180 to + 40 km s ^ 1 along 3.5 kpc ( 10 ).

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 1060 as a brightest-group early-type galaxy, using MUSE kinematics and multiwavelength context to assess rotation, shape, gas, or activity.

quotes.quote: We have three objects, NGC 410, NGC 777 and NGC 1060, in common with the MASSIVE sample Ma2014 and we compare some of our derived properties in Appendix Section:comparison . Non-regular rotation (NRR) is the most common characteristic of the Krajnovic2018 M3G BCGs, whereas we find it for only 8/18 BGEs: ESO 507-G025, NGC 193, NGC 410, NGC 677, NGC 777, NGC 1060, NGC 5846, as well as NGC 4261 (which shows non-regular rotation around the major axis, as reported and discussed by Davies1986 ). We can see that the six least luminous BGEs are fast rotators, and the three most luminous BGEs are slow rotators (NGC 410, NGC 777, and NGC 1060), and an overlap between the two classes over a broad range of luminosities in between.

gmw_00001523.name: NGC 1453
gmw_00001523.overview: NGC 1453 is a massive, fast-rotating elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2005.00542, arXiv:2111.06904] that serves as the brightest member of its galaxy group [arXiv:2206.13215]. It features a large, externally accreted warm ionized gas disk [arXiv:1904.08929, arXiv:1701.08772] and a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:1904.08929, arXiv:2111.06904].

sources.summary: The MASSIVE survey paper uses NGC 1453 as its regular fast-rotator JAM case, inferring a nonzero central black hole, varying anisotropy, and mass model parameters.

quotes.quote: Here we apply the computationally simple method of Jeans Anisotropic Modeling (JAM; cappellari2008 ) to illustrate how the two datasets can be used to infer the central black hole mass, the stellar mass-to-light ratio and the dark matter halo content of the regular fast rotator NGC 1453 in our sample. We choose NGC 1453 due to its regular photometric properties and almost purely elliptical isophotes (Fig. fig:mge ; Goullaudetal2018 ). In addition to being a regular fast-rotator, showed that the photometric and kinematic position angles of NGC 1453 are almost perfectly aligned, suggesting that the galaxy may be modeled as an axisymmetric system. As an interesting note, NGC 1453 also contains a large ( R 20'' ) warm ionized gas disc that is rotating almost perpendicularly to the stellar kinematic axis, suggesting that the gas accreted through external processes Pandyaetal2017 .

Using the GMOS and Mitchell stellar kinematics, we applied the Jeans modeling method to measure the mass distributions in NGC 1453, the most regular fast rotator in our sample. To partially account for spatial variations in the stellar velocity anisotropy, we allow the anisotropy parameter _ z in the inner and outer parts of the galaxy to be different. The JAM results show that our kinematics point towards a nonzero central black hole mass and a spatially varying velocity anisotropy. The best-fit mass model (with 3 errors) has a black hole mass of 3.29 0.75 10^ 9 M_ , a stellar mass-to-light ratio of = 2.06 0.13 \ _ (in WFC3 F110W band), and a circular velocity of V_c = 364 134 \ for the dark matter halo.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1453 to test axisymmetrized triaxial Schwarzschild modeling, showing that orbit-library choices and higher Gauss-Hermite moments affect the inferred supermassive black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: We then performed proper axisymmetric orbit modeling using the revised code to obtain a new \ measurement for the massive elliptical galaxy NGC 1453, a fast rotator in the MASSIVE survey Maetal2014 well suited for axisymmetric orbit modeling. Similar to Ahnetal2018 , we had encountered difficulties in constraining \ in NGC 1453 when we used the original code with comparable settings.

We use the massive elliptical galaxy NGC 1453 reported in Liepoldetal2020 to illustrate the effects of the modifications described thus far. In Liepoldetal2020 , we demonstrated that using more than 4 Gauss-Hermite moments was essential for obtaining robust constraints on the model LOSVDs. Below we examine the effects in both the 4-moment and 12-moment cases, with the latter being our chosen configuration.

For NGC 1453, we found the shape of the ^2 contours for \ to vary significantly as we went through the step-by-step axisymmetrization procedure described in this paper (Figure figure:versions ). As we described in Liepoldetal2020 , the orbit models favored no black holes when we used the original code with typical (near) axisymmetric parameters in the literature and four Gauss-Hermite moments to constrain the stellar LOSVDs. In contrast, we obtained a well constrained non-zero \ using our final axisymmetrized code including all the changes described in Sections sec:axi and sec:minor_changes .

sources.summary: The paper applies triaxial orbit modeling to NGC 1453, a fast-rotating massive elliptical, deriving its intrinsic shape while finding its SMBH and mass parameters consistent with axisymmetric results.

quotes.quote: We apply the updated code and search method to NGC 1453, a fast-rotating massive elliptical galaxy. A full 6D parameter search finds p=b/a=0.933^ +0.014 _ 0.015 and q=/a=0.779 0.012 for the intrinsic axis ratios and T=0.33 0.06 for the triaxiality parameter. Despite the deviations from axisymmetry, the best-fit SMBH mass, stellar mass-to-light ratio, and dark matter enclosed mass for NGC 1453 are consistent with the axisymmetric results.

Due to the regular isophotes of NGC 1453 (Figure 5 of Liepoldetal2020 ), we use the same PA for all MGE components and do not model isophotal twists. This is a common simplifying assumption e.g., vandenBoschdeZeeuw2010,Walshetal2012,FeldmeierKrause2017 and it enables us to explore the galaxy's shape using the new scheme outlined in Section sec:new_params . For the distance to NGC 1453, we adopt our new determination of 51.0 Mpc from the MASSIVE-WFC3 project Goullaudetal2018 using the surface-brightness fluctuation technique Jensenetal2021 .
Thus, despite being a fast rotator with regular isophotal and kinematic features, NGC 1453 is best fit by a triaxial model.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1453 as the closest MASSIVE comparison: a similarly fast, regular rotator whose triaxial models fit non-axisymmetric kinematics and indicate a slightly less flattened shape than NGC 2693.

quotes.quote: The only other MASSIVE galaxy that exhibits similarly fast and regular rotation is NGC 1453. NGC 1453 is recently studied both in the axisymmetric limit Liepoldetal2020 and with fully triaxial orbit modeling Quennevilleetal2021b . The triaxial models are found to better recover the input kinematics while also fitting the non-axisymmetric features present in NGC 1453. Given the similarities between NGC 2693 and NGC 1453, we turn to NGC 2693 in this paper.

NGC 1453, the other MASSIVE galaxy for which we have performed triaxial orbit modeling Quennevilleetal2021b , has best-fit shape parameters of (p, q) = (0.93, 0.78) , indicating a slightly less flattened shape than NGC 2693 and the mean MASSIVE galaxy. Additionally, the intrinsic shapes of NGC 2693 and NGC 1453 are consistent with the distribution of shapes of fast rotators found in the IllustrisTNG50 and IllustrisTNG100 simulations Pulsoni2020 , where the mean axis ratios are (p,q) (0.9,0.52) and the dispersion is 0.15 for the most massive fast rotating elliptical galaxies.

sources.summary: NGC 1453 has a 21 kpc smooth rotating gas disk, an inner compact disk with a different position angle, NW extended structures, and cooling-compatible intragroup gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 1453 -- Similarly, NGC 1453 shows a smooth velocity gradient from + 300 to 360 km s ^ 1 from North to South across 21 kpc. An inner compact disk of 3 kpc (9.5 ) of diameter, with a slightly different PA from that of the major disk, is found at the very center of the galaxy. Extended structures are seen in NGC 1453 located 9 kpc (30 ) at the NW the center of the galaxy, moving at + 250 km s ^ 1 .
Only NGC 1453 has a detected IGrM, which appears to be cooling according to the criteria discussed above.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1453 as an extreme external-accretion example whose warm ionized gas is strongly misaligned with the stars, making stellar mass loss an unlikely gas source.

quotes.quote: An extreme example of external accretion is the MASSIVE fast rotator NGC 1453 in which the warm ionized gas is kinematically misaligned with respect to the stars by 98 deg. buson93 and zeilinger96 also found that the warm ionized gas is misaligned relative to the stars in NGC 1453, though with a larger misalignment angle of 128 deg. It is unlikely that stellar mass loss from the old stellar population is the primary source of the warm ionized medium in NGC 1453 because such recycled gas would tend to remain in the potential well of the stars, leading to photometric and kinematic alignment between the stars and gas.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 1453 as a brightest-group early-type galaxy, using MUSE kinematics and multiwavelength context to assess rotation, shape, gas, or activity.

quotes.quote: We find the majority (10/18) of our BGEs to be regular rotators (RR): NGC 584, NGC 924, NGC 940, NGC 978, NGC 1453, NGC 1587, NGC 4008, NGC 4169, NGC 6658, and NGC 7619. NGC 1453: Even though this target is hosted by an X-ray bright group, the NCC status of the group makes is unlikely that the cold gas originated from IGrM cooling. NGC 1587: Similar to NGC 1453, this is hosted by an X-ray bright but NCC group, making it unlikely that the cold gas originated from IGrM cooling.

gmw_00001524.name: NGC 1587
gmw_00001524.overview: NGC 1587 is an early-type, regular rotator galaxy [arXiv:2206.13215] that is the brighter elliptical of a galaxy pair [arXiv:1103.1364]. It exhibits tidal interactions with its elliptical neighbor [arXiv:2201.07838] and features spiral-shaped ionized gas filaments that appear to be inflowing toward its center [arXiv:2201.07838].

sources.summary: NGC 1587 has rotating or inflowing spiral ionized filaments and shows tidal interaction with an elliptical neighbor; the authors suggest colder gas may have been acquired through that interaction.

quotes.quote: The set of optical filaments in NGC 1587 appears spiral inflowing into the central galaxy, indicating signs of tidal stripping (see Fig  fig:optical , left panel).

NGC 1587 shows signs of tidal interaction with its elliptical neighbor. Accordingly, we suspect that at least a fraction, if not all, of the colder gas detected in this source, may have been acquired through a galaxy interaction. Still, based on the condensation criteria, the IGrM is also compatible with gas cooling.

NGC 1587 -- shows indications of rotation, but this is probably due to several thin filaments that are inflowing or outflowing toward the center of the galaxy. In this source, the ionized gas is moving from 280 to + 80 km s ^ 1 from NW to SE across 11 kpc (44 ) in a spiral form.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1587 as the brighter elliptical of a pair, with a compact radio component, amorphous halo-like emission, and no detected emission from NGC 1588.

quotes.quote: NGC 1587 NGC 1587 is the brighter of the pair of elliptical galaxies NGC 1587/88, which is part of a poor group with an unusually low velocity dispersion 100 km s ^ 1 (Mulchaey et al. 2003, Osmond Ponman 2004). The 610 MHz radio image of NGC 1587 is shown in Fig. fig:ngc1587 at full resolution (left) and slightly lower resolution (right), overlaid on the Chandra and POSS-2 images, respectively. The source has a compact, bright component, coincident with the galaxy optical peak. This component appears surrounded by amorphous, low-brightness emission that encompasses a 5 kpc radius region around the center. A fainter structure extends for 10 kpc north-west of the galaxy.

NGC 1587: Similar to NGC 1453, this is hosted by an X-ray bright but NCC group, making it unlikely that the cold gas originated from IGrM cooling.

gmw_00001525.name: NGC 4008
gmw_00001525.overview: NGC 4008 is a brightest-group early-type galaxy [arXiv:2206.13215] reclassified as an SA0 lenticular galaxy due to its extended stellar disc [arXiv:2204.13408]. The galaxy exhibits a clear stellar rotation pattern [arXiv:2201.07838, arXiv:2206.13215], chaotic ionized-gas kinematics [arXiv:2201.07838], and three faint, nearly parallel northeast filaments [arXiv:2201.07838].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies NGC 4008 from E5 to SA0, citing its velocity dispersion, ellipticity and surface-brightness profiles, extended disc, and bulge-to-disc transition.

quotes.quote: We present one such `elliptical galaxy', NGC 4008, to illustrate why we reclassified it as an S0 galaxy instead of an E galaxy. NGC 4008 was classified as an `E5' in RC3. Figure  fig:NGC4008img shows the galaxy, our model, and our residual image of NGC 4008. The galaxy has a central stellar velocity dispersion of _0 208 - 240 km s^ 1 Faber1989,Simien2002 . In its ellipticity profile ( , middle panel in Figure  fig:NGC4008profile ), one can see that the ellipticity reaches 0.4 , and the surface brightness profile ( , top panel in Figure  fig:NGC4008profile ) has a smooth exponential decline from R_ eq 20 .

This decomposition also aligns with Simien2002 , who show that at R_ eq > 10 , NGC 4008 transitions from bulge-dominant to disc-dominant. The presence of an extended disc means NGC 4008 is not an `E5' but an `SA0' galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC 4008 has a clear stellar rotation pattern, chaotic ionized-gas kinematics, and three faint nearly parallel northeast filaments with measured sizes and velocities.

quotes.quote: The stellar velocity fields reveal that most of the sources in our sample (10/18 sources, e.g., NGC 940, NGC 924, NGC 1453, NGC 4169, NGC 978, NGC 584, NGC 1587, NGC 4008, NGC 7619 and NGC 6658) show a clear large-scale rotation pattern in their stellar light.

Clear examples of very chaotic velocity fields can be seen in NGC 677, NGC 4008, and NGC 5846. In some cases, velocity gradients along the filaments are detected, but the gas is quite disturbed on average.

NGC 4008 -- reveal three faint almost parallel NE filaments. The southern filament has a size of (9 ) and a velocity of 80 km s ^ 1 , the middle (3.5 ) and velocity of 20 km s ^ 1 , finally the northern filament has a length (5 ) and a velocity of 100  km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4008 as a brightest-group early-type galaxy, using MUSE kinematics and multiwavelength context to assess rotation, shape, gas, or activity.

quotes.quote: We find the majority (10/18) of our BGEs to be regular rotators (RR): NGC 584, NGC 924, NGC 940, NGC 978, NGC 1453, NGC 1587, NGC 4008, NGC 4169, NGC 6658, and NGC 7619. Studying the thermodynamical properties of the X-ray atmospheres, Olivares2022 find that the filamentary structures and compact disks (the morphology of the ionised gas) are found in systems with small central entropy values, short t_ cool /t_ ff and t_ cool /t_ eddy ratios (NGC 193, NGC 410, NGC 677, NGC 777, NGC 978, NGC 1060, NGC 1587, NGC 4008, NGC 4261, NGC 5846, NGC 7619). NGC 4008: No cold gas was detected in this BGE.

gmw_00001526.name: NGC 4169
gmw_00001526.overview: NGC 4169 is an early-type galaxy classified as a brightest group galaxy and a regular rotator [arXiv:2206.13215]. The galaxy's ionized gas counter-rotates relative to its stellar component and exhibits an extended velocity field with clumpy and southwestern structures [arXiv:2201.07838].

sources.summary: NGC 4169 has an extended gas velocity field with clumpy and southwest structures, and its ionized gas counter-rotates relative to the stellar component.

quotes.quote: NGC 4169 -- The velocity field of NGC 4169 is moving from + 150 to 280 km s ^ 1 going from SE to NW across 4.8 kpc of extension, with some clumpy structure at 3 kpc (15 ) of the SE center. It also hosts some extended structures located 2.8 kpc (13 ) to the SW of the galaxy center, moving at + 30 km s ^ 1 .
Note also that in NGC 4169, the gas (PA = 143 ) is counter-rotating with respect to the stars (PA = 332.9 ), with a PA of 189 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4169 as a brightest-group early-type galaxy, using MUSE kinematics and multiwavelength context to assess rotation, shape, gas, or activity.

quotes.quote: We find the majority (10/18) of our BGEs to be regular rotators (RR): NGC 584, NGC 924, NGC 940, NGC 978, NGC 1453, NGC 1587, NGC 4008, NGC 4169, NGC 6658, and NGC 7619. Point-like morphology in X-ray for the hot gas component is found in three of our BGEs with H rings (NGC 924, NGC 940, and NGC 4169), whereas ESO 507-G025 has no hot gas component, potentially indicating that the cold gas is more likely to be the result of gas-rich mergers or tidal interactions instead of cooling from a hot IGrM. The rest of our fast and slow rotators are not star forming according to their classification (although NGC 4169 and 6658 are unknown as their FUV photometry is unavailable).

gmw_00001527.name: NGC 5846
gmw_00001527.overview: NGC 5846 is a massive, slowly rotating giant elliptical galaxy that serves as the dominant member of its galaxy group [arXiv:1103.1364, arXiv:1601.02597]. The galaxy hosts a low-power active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1103.1364, arXiv:1305.2225] surrounded by complex X-ray cavities [arXiv:1108.5229, arXiv:1411.6040], filamentary dust [arXiv:1411.6040], and molecular gas [arXiv:1711.10630].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5846 as a group-brightest galaxy with modeled dynamics, an unusual GC system, new Subaru photometry, 195 confirmed GCs, and blue-GC rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 5846 is the brightest member of a galaxy group. The dynamics of NGC 5846 has been modelled within 1 \ ( Cappellari07 ; Kronawitter ). Recently, Das derived the mass of this galaxy up to 11 \ exploiting X-ray and PNe observations. An HST /WFPC2 analysis of the NGC 5846 GC system was given in Forbes97B and then revisited by Chies-Santos06 .

In addition to the classic bimodality, they also discovered that the GC system is better aligned with the galaxy's minor axis than its major axis and that this galaxy has an unusually low specific frequency compared to similar dominant ellipticals in groups or clusters. In this work we present new Subaru photometry in gri filters, with the g band in moderate seeing conditions ( 1 arcsec). Although the red peak is not clearly visible (Figure SpectroPhoto_Matrix ), KMM returned a high probability for the colour distribution to be bimodal with a colour split at (g-i)=0.95 . We spectroscopically confirm 195 GCs over 6 DEIMOS masks.

sources.summary: NGC 5846 is treated as a non-variable LINER: separate XMM-Newton and Chandra fits show little X-ray flux variation, though aperture contamination prevents joint analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 5846 We used two observations from XMM Newton (January and August 2001) and other two observations from Chandra (May 2000 and June 2007). The analysis was firstly made separately due to different apertures (see Table obs ). HR Variations of 1 and 6 were obtained for XMM Newton and Chandra data, respectively (from Column 8 in Table obs ). In the case of Chandra observations, we only made the analysis up to 3 keV, due to low count-rate at hard energies. In this case SMF0 was used, resulting in a non-variable source when fitting a MEPL model (Table bestfit and Figure bestfig ) with flux variations below 1 in a period of 7 years (Table lumincorr and Figure luminfig ).

The XMM Newton data did not show variability (the best fit model is MEPL, see Table bestfit and Figure bestfig ), and flux variations below 1 on the soft and hard energies for a period of 7 months (Table lumincorr ). The contribution to the 0.5-10.0 keV band emission from the 25 aperture Chandra data was 73 from the annular region, what avoided the joint use of Chandra and XMM Newton data for this object. No short time scale variability was found in the analysis of the light curves (see Table estcurvas and Figure lightcurves5846 ), since measures were below 2 from the average.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5846 as the optically brightest group galaxy with nuclear dust and disturbed X-ray gas, whose downward profile and bright outer halo fit a double-Sersic model.

quotes.quote: NGC 5846 NGC 5846 is the optically most luminous galaxy in the center of its group. HST images show the presence of a ``parabolic'' dust lane around the nucleus of the galaxy. X-ray observations Trinchieri02 show a disturbed hot gas morphology on arcsecond scales. Haynes91 found that the three dimensional distribution of galaxies around NGC 5846 is a prolate cloud, pointing toward the Virgo cluster.

We found that this model converges to a best-fit solution, with physically meaningful value, for only two galaxies, NGC 5044 and NGC 5846. In these cases the fit yields very small S e rsic indices ( n_ 2 0.7 and n_ 2 0.4 , respectively) for the outer components. Specifically, we can distinguish the profiles of NGC 1399, NGC 5044 and NGC 5846 which show a downward inflection, from those of NGC 3923, NGC 4365 and NGC 4472, which instead have an upward inflection. For NGC 1399 and NGC 5846 the outer halo component becomes even brighter than the inner component at R R_ tr , where the relative contribution of the outer halo with respect to the total galaxy light ( f_ h ) reaches 60 and 55 respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5846 as a massive group early-type galaxy with conflicting E/S0 classifications, little rotation, flat spheroid-like kinematics, and likely repeated late minor mergers.

quotes.quote: NGC 5846 is a massive, bright early-type galaxy belonging to a galaxy group, which is located in the constellation Virgo. This galaxy has conflicting morphology, being classified as an E0-1 in RC2 and S0 in RSA Michard1994 . Early works have identified very little to no rotation from stellar Emsellem2004,Foster2016 and GC kinematic Pota2013 studies of this galaxy.

NGC 5846 does not show any stellar rotation within 1 2 R_ e , as previously shown by ATLAS ^ 3D within 0.5 R_ e . No net rotation is also seen for the PNe, whose rotation velocity profile is constant at 50 kms^ 1 out to 5 R_ e . The red GCs also show roughly constant rotation velocity profile out to 6 R_ e , which is overall consistent with that of the PNe with a small offset in rotation. On the other hand, the blue GCs show rotation velocity, peaking at 200 kms^ 1 , between 1.5 2.5 R_ e , which decreases out to large radii, i.e. 4 R_ e , beyond which it is consistent with both that of the PNe and red GCs.
These mergers were likely minor and occurred more than once in the case of NGC 821 and NGC 5846, therefore enhancing the velocity dispersion of the kinematic tracers at all radii.

sources.summary: NGC5846 UDG1 is presented as a nearby UDG with discrepant but rich GC counts, HST-resolved candidates, and spectroscopic confirmation of many GCs.

quotes.quote: Another UDG for which there have been different estimates in the literature for its number of GCs is NGC5846 UDG1 in the NGC 5846 group. It was identified as a UDG with a system of GCs in the deep ground-based imaging of . A crude estimate of the total number of GCs from this imaging was 45. At 25 Mpc, this UDG is sufficiently nearby for GCs to be partially resolved with HST. From HST imaging, derived a total of 26 6 GCs (which they refer to as MATLAS-2019).
Subsequently, with deeper HST imaging, derived 54 9 GCs.

Fortunately, NGC5846 UDG1 is sufficiently close to obtain spectra of its GC candidates. confirmed 11 individual GCs, two GCs those spectra were combined together using VLT/MUSE data. A preliminary analysis of Keck/KCWI data has confirmed the GCs observed by (except for one not in the KCWI field-of-view) and added another dozen (Haacke, L. 2023, priv. comm.).

sources.summary: The paper revisits NGC 5846's globular cluster system, finding new central candidates, measured cluster sizes, X-ray counterparts, and a luminous red-cluster X-ray subpopulation.

quotes.quote: NGC 5846 is a giant elliptical galaxy with a previously well studied globular cluster system (GCS), known to have a bimodal colour distribution with a remarkably high red fraction. Here we revisit the central galaxy regions searching for new globular cluster (GC) candidates, and measure magnitudes, colours and sizes for them.

We detect two dozen previously unknown GC candidates in the central regions. Reliable sizes are obtained for about 60 GCs; their typical effective radii are in the range 3-5 pc. The largest clusters are located in the central regions. We find 7 X-ray counterparts to GCs, most of them in the central region.
They are among the most luminous X-ray sources in NGC 5846. They are also optically luminous, compact and belong to the red subpopulation.

sources.summary: The paper finds ring-like metallicity enhancements, relaxed thermodynamic maps, entropy elongation, core pressure disturbance, and central radio evidence for an AGN in NGC 5846.

quotes.quote: NGC 5846, z=0.0063 This system reveals a number of ring-like structures in the image, which we identify with metalicity enhancements. No optical counterpart for this structure is seen in the DSS2 images, consistent with the idea of metalicity enhancements. Although an optical image reveals a small galaxy 0.6^ south to NGC 5846 (NGC 5846a, velocity difference 500 km/s), it is not demarcated on the entropy map. This rules out a significant gas halo for this object and so it should have in any case a negligible impact on the X-ray appearance.

The two dimensional spectral mapping of the properties of the X-ray emission from NGC 5846 is done at the effective 6--10 kpc resolution, within the central 50 kpc. As the spectroscopic analysis shows (see Fig. f:sprats ), entropy, pressure, temperature and iron abundance all appear relaxed with only a slight displacement between the central peak and the center of the large-scale emission. The large-scale elongation of the entropy is confirmed and an associated elongation in the high-element abundance zone is found. In addition, some disturbance in the pressure map is observed in the core.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC5846's diffuse X-ray corona, deriving its temperature, mass, gas fraction, abundance gradients, classification, distance, and X-ray luminosity from ROSAT and ASCA data.

quotes.quote: In this paper we analyze the diffuse X-ray coronae surrounding the elliptical galaxy NGC5846, combining measurements from two observatories, ROSAT and ASCA. We map the gas temperature distribution and find a central cool region within an approximately isothermal gas halo extending to a radius of about 50 kpc, and evidence for a temperature decrease at larger radii. With a radially falling temperature profile, the total mass converges to 9.6 1.0 10^ 12 at 230 kpc radius.

This corresponds to a total mass to blue light ratio of 53 5 / . As in other early type galaxies, the gas mass is only a few percent of the total mass.
