quotes.quote: Such scenario was indeed fostered by an initial claim of large redshift ( z 1.246 ) for the TeV blazar PKS 0447-489 , whose gamma-ray spectrum was measured up to 1 TeV . The VHE spectrum was found consistent with secondary emission , and provided a case study for such possibility. However, recently it has been shown that the redshift estimate is most likely incorrect . The absorption feature seen at 6280 \ is not a redshifted Mg II line (as initially thought) but most likely originates in Earth's atmosphere, being detected in the spectra of multiple standard stars in the same observing runs . The redshift of PKS 0447-489 remains thus uncertain.

sources.summary: The paper models PKS 0447-439 as an HBL whose SED fits observations in both EED models, while accepting only the PLLP-derived EBL-density result.

quotes.quote: 5. PKS 0447-439 It is an HBL, we set '_ min =100 and t_ v,min =24 hr to build the SEDs qin2018b . We can find that the modeling SEDs favor the observation both in two EEDs. However, as shown in the right panel of the Fig.8, the EBL density is lower than that given by galaxy counts in BPL EEDs and should be discarded. The EBL density obtained in the PLLP model, shown in the right panel of Fig.8, is about 6-20 n W m ^ 2 sr ^ 1 slightly higher than that givem by .

sources.summary: The paper gives PKS 0447 439 an individual blazar variability note, summarizing TeV-band detections, GeV or hard-X-ray behavior, and variability classification.

quotes.quote: PKS 0447 439 : The TeV emission from this high-synchrotron peaked blazar was first detected with the High Energy Stereoscopic System (H.E.S.S.) during 2009-2010 2013A&A...552A.118H consistent with the low-activity detection in the TeV band light curve (Figure fig:1 ). The source has shown moderate variability in the TeV band. It exhibited its brightest TeV flare in the time bin MJD 59002 59092. This activity was also reflected in the GeV band light curve.

sources.summary: The review says PKS 0447 439 was discovered at VHE, had contemporaneous multiwavelength SED modelling with an SSC model, and received an upper redshift limit.

quotes.quote: Among these, the high-frequency-peaked BL Lac object (HBL) PKS 0447 439 was discovered at VHE with at the 15.2 CL in 13.5 h of observations . Multi-wavelength observations with , Swift , ATOM and ROTSE were used to draw a contemporaneous spectral energy distribution of this source, which was modelled using an SSC model. The redshift of this object being uncertain , the modelling of multi-wavelength data was used to derive an upper limit on its redshift of z<0.59 .

gmw_00001532.name: 1ES 0502+675
gmw_00001532.overview: 1ES 0502+675 is a high-frequency peaked BL Lacertae blazar [arXiv:1110.0040, arXiv:2306.06170] with an uncertain redshift [arXiv:1110.0040] that exhibits TeV flux variability at a confidence level greater than 99 percent [arXiv:2401.11762]. The active galactic nucleus has been observed in a moderately flaring state [arXiv:2201.02508] and has shown a possible intra-night fading event [arXiv:2306.06170].

sources.summary: The paper says 1ES 0502+675 was observed after Fermi Bright Source List motivation, detected strongly by VERITAS, showed steady soft VHE flux, and retained uncertain redshift after deeper MMT spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0502+675 observations were motivated by the flux and spectrum reported in the Fermi-LAT Bright Source List for 0FGL J0507.9+6739 . This HBL was observed by VERITAS for 30 h of quality-selected live time between September 2009 and January 2010. An excess of VHE rays (VER J0507+676), corresponding to a statistical significance of 11 , is observed from the direction of the blazar. The VHE flux is constant within the observed statistics, with an average value of 6 Crab above 300 GeV, and the time-averaged VERITAS photon index is = 3.92 0.35 . Although the catalog redshift of this blazar is 0.341, possibly explaining the softness of the observed spectrum, the value is from a private communication and the original MMT spectrum could not be located. Following the VERITAS detection, a 10 times more sensitive observation was performed with the same spectrograph at the MMT. No significant absorption/emission lines were observed and hence the redshift is uncertain.

sources.summary: The paper studies the blazar 1ES 0502+675 with Fermi-LAT Pass 8 data, finding a 4 sigma 1 GeV hardening during a moderate flare and interpreting it with one-zone SSC emission.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage The ray spectral feature of the blazar 1ES 0502+675 is investigated by using Fermi Large Area Telescope (Fermi-LAT) Pass 8 data (between 100 MeV and 300 GeV) covering from 2008 August to 2021 April. A significant ( 4 ) hardening at 1 GeV is found in the ray spectrum during a moderately flaring state (MJD 55050-55350). The photon index below and above the break energy is _1=2.36 0.31 and _2=1.33 0.11 , respectively. In the rest of the observations, the ray spectrum can be described by a power-law form with the photon index of 1.6 . In the frame of a one-zone synchrotron self-Compton (SSC) model, the spectral hardening is interpreted as the transition between the synchrotron component and the SSC component.

sources.summary: J0507+6737 is the only TeV-HBL in the sample with a possible intra-night fading event and reported mildly superluminal motion; even accepting it leaves a very low duty cycle.

quotes.quote: One possible exception is the session on 2021-10-10, during which a hint of gradual fading by 2.5 over 3 hours was noticed for the z = 0.314 blazar J0507+6737. The fading was observed relative to both comparison stars which themselves remained steady throughout that session, as did the PSF (Fig. fig:all_dlc_J0507_PV ). Interestingly, this is the only blazar in our sample for which 2018ApJ...853...68P have reported a (mildly) superluminal motion at a high confidence level ( _ app = 2.23 0.44c, i.e., 5.1 , see Table tab:sample_table ). Even taking, conservatively, this possible INOV detection as confirmed (despite its formal classification being `non-variable', see online Table S2), the INOV duty cycle for our sample would still be only 4 .

sources.summary: The paper gives 1ES 0502+675 an individual blazar variability note, summarizing TeV-band detections, GeV or hard-X-ray behavior, and variability classification.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0502+675 : A 2.5-year long TeV flaring event was observed by the LAT during the initial years of its operation, and the source was also detected with VERITAS in this time-period Figure fig:1 ; 2009ATel.2301....1O . Due to large uncertainties in both TeV and GeV bands data, however, the results of the analysis were inconclusive. The source went into quiescence after this flare and was sporadically detected in the TeV band at a flux level comparable to its 15 years averaged value as also confirmed by the Bayesian block analysis. Overall, significant TeV flux variability at > 99 confidence level was detected from this blazar.

gmw_00001533.name: RX J0543.9 5532
gmw_00001533.overview: RX J0543.9 5532 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1402.5464] classified as a high-frequency peaked blazar [arXiv:1501.07115]. The galaxy has a redshift of z=0.237 [arXiv:1402.5464] and exhibits periods of enhanced GeV-band activity alongside a quiescent TeV-band state [arXiv:2401.11762].

sources.summary: The paper estimates J0543 5532 at z=0.374 from imaging, contrasts later spectroscopy at z=0.237, and concludes its host is substantially fainter than the standard-candle assumption.

quotes.quote: Our estimates and lower bounds are generally in agreement with other redshifts estimates, although our z=0.374 estimate for J0543 5532 implies a significantly sub-luminous host.

For the five sources with host detection, two (J0316+0904 and J0543 5532) have imaging redshift estimates consistent with the spectra-derived bounds in that paper; the low significance detection of J1023 4336 is also consistent.

However, since completing this study pita13 have published new high quality VLT/X-shooter spectra of a number of blazars, including J0543 5532, measured here. For this source, they infer z=0.237 based on a weak Ca II H/K doublet and a Na I absorption line. This redshift, near the lower bound of shaw13a is well below our imaging estimate, implying a host that is substantially fainter than our standard candle assumption. At this z our measured host flux implies an absolute magnitude M_R = -21.67^ +0.01+0.20 _ 0.01-0.17 , 0.8 mag ( 1.7 ) away from our assumed standard candle luminosity. The host absolute magnitude is consistent with, but more accurate than, the spectroscopically estimated value in pita13 .

sources.summary: The paper models 054357.3-553206 as an HBL and likely IceCube event 19 counterpart, finding a hybrid SED resembling PG 1553+113 with neutrino peak flux below gamma rays.

quotes.quote: 054357.3-553206 \ is the second HBL source that has not yet been detected in TeV energies and the most probable counterpart of IceCube event 19 according to PR14. The hybrid SED obtained with our model is shown in Fig. rxs and shares many features with the model SED of PG 1553+113. According to the discussion in Sect. pg1553 , we can infer that the observed ray emission originates only partially from photohadronic processes, since the model derived neutrino peak flux is lower than the ray one. In fact, we find that =0.1 , same as in the case of PG 1553+113.

sources.summary: The paper gives RX J0543.9 5532 an individual blazar variability note, summarizing TeV-band detections, GeV or hard-X-ray behavior, and variability classification.

quotes.quote: RX J0543.9 5532 : 2014MNRAS.445.4345B reported the first VHE detection of this source using the LAT data above 100 GeV though it was not detected with the Cherenkov telescopes 2014A&A...564A...9H . The GeV and TeV band light curves indicated that the object was in quiescence most of the time since the LAT operation began in 2008. According to the Bayesian block analysis, the enhanced GeV band activity was identified starting with the time bin MJD 58642 58732 and the source was also detected in the TeV band. However, no significant TeV flux variability was identified as revealed by the analysis.

gmw_00001534.name: PKS 1424+240
gmw_00001534.overview: PKS 1424+240 is a distant active galaxy [arXiv:2305.07086] and BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1412.1544, arXiv:1701.04305] located at a redshift of approximately 0.6045 [arXiv:2401.07911]. It is a bright gamma-ray blazar [arXiv:2401.07911] and a candidate source of IceCube neutrino emission [arXiv:2202.04363, arXiv:2211.09972].

sources.summary: The paper constrains blazar PKS 1424+240 to z >= 0.6035, uses HST/COS observations, and shows its VHE spectrum probes unusually high EBL gamma-ray opacity.

quotes.quote: We apply this technique to the VHE-detected blazar PKS 1424+240, constraining the blazar to z 0.6035 , making it the most distant VHE-detected blazar thus far. PKS 1424 + 240 was observed under a Hubble Space Telescope (HST) program (12612, PI: Stocke) which uses flaring blazars as probes of intervening, weak IGM absorption. PKS 1424 + 240 is one of 200 objects monitored by a network of automated telescopes: the Katzman Automatic Imaging Telescope (KAIT, ), the ``NF/ Observatory" neely and the Small and Moderate-Aperture Remote Telescope System (SMARTS, ). Optical photometry in April of 2012 indicated that PKS 1424 + 240 was sufficiently bright to trigger a five-orbit HST/COS observation. The blazar was observed on 19 April 2012, with the medium resolution ( v 18 km s^ 1 ), far-UV gratings G130M ( 1135< <1450 , 6.4 ksec) and G160M ( 1400< <1795 , 7.9 ksec).

PKS 1424+240 can now be included in the study, expanding the limited opacity parameter space available. Before the determination of the redshift lower limit of PKS 1424+240, the highest sampled gamma-ray optical depth probed was associated with the detection of 3C 279 during an elevated state, probing a ( E =475 GeV , z=0.536)=3.2 , 3.9 or 4.3 when estimated with the models, respectively. Now, with PKS 1424+240, the highest opacity sampled is between 4.1 and 5.3 (if estimated with the and models, respectively.) We show the highest opacity sampled by the VHE detection of 3C 279 compared to the opacity probed with the detection of PKS 1424+240 in Figure 2, as derived for the model.

sources.summary: The paper models PKS 1424+240 as a distant BL Lac blazar whose hard TeV spectrum can be explained by lepto-hadronic jet emission at redshift 0.6-0.75.

quotes.quote: We investigate the formation of TeV spectrum of a distant blazar PKS 1424+240 residing at a redshift z 0.6 in two scenarios in the frame of a lepto-hadronic jet model taking both the uncertainties of the extragalactic background light (EBL) and its redshift into account.

The results show that in the first scenario the 68 upper limit of its redshift within which this scenario can explain the VERITAS TeV spectrum in 2009 well is 0.75 ; in the second scenario, this upper limit of the redshift becomes 1.03 . However, the second scenario can be excluded because it requires an unreasonable photon field around the jet with a luminosity of 10^ 43 \ erg \ s^ 1 . In conclusion, the jet model can explain its TeV spectrum with a low EBL density if 0.6<z<0.75 .

PKS 1424+240 is classified as a BL Lac Object (BL Lacs) because of its weak emission-line feature. Very high energy (VHE) gamma-rays from PKS 1424+240 have been detected by VERITAS Acciari2010,Archambault14 and MAGIC Aleks2014 . The intrinsic TeV emission from a blazar is expected to be attenuated through interaction with the ultraviolet-infrared photons of extragalactic background light (EBL). This attenuation depends on the distance from the source to the Earth and the EBL density. However, as for many BL Lacs, the redshift of PKS 1424+240 is still uncertain. Furniss13 presented a firm redshift lower limit of z 0.6035 for PKS 1424+240 according to the observations of Ly and Ly absorption.

sources.summary: The paper presents fast optical photometry and polarimetry of PKS 1424+240, finding nearly constant total flux, stable optical polarization behavior, and evidence for high intrinsic optical luminosity.

quotes.quote: Short timescale (from tens of seconds to a couple of minutes) optical linear polarimetry and photometry for two blazars, BL Lacertae and PKS 1424+240 , was carried out with the PAOLO polarimeter at the 3.6 m Telescopio Nazionale Galileo. Several hours of almost continuous observations were obtained for both sources. Our intense monitoring allowed us to draw strongly different scenarios for BL Lacertae and PKS 1424+240 , with the former characterized by intense variability on time-scales from hours to a few minutes and the latter practically constant in total flux.

PKS 1424+240 is also a BL Lac object and its redshift is still uncertain. Fur13 report a lower limit at z 0.6 , which can make it one of most luminous objects in its class. Its host galaxy was possibly detected by MR10 at typically a few percent of the nuclear emission, although Sca00 reported much fainter limits.

In general, looking at historical data, the polarization degree of PKS 1424+240 seems to be almost constant ( 4 ) below a given optical flux (likely 9.0 mJy, based on the refereed studies). The optical position angle seems to be quite stable and aligned with the kinematic direction of the radio jet and with the radio polarization position angle.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG1424+240 as an HSP BL Lac and distant VHE blazar; uGMRT imaging shows a polarized core and lobes in a double-lobed morphology.

quotes.quote: PG1424+240 a.k.a. OQ +240 This source was identified as a blazar by Impey1988 . It was originally classified as a white dwarf in the PG sample but Fleming1993 used ROSAT All Sky Survey (RASS) to identify PG 1424+240 as a BL Lac object. It was first detected at VHE by VERITAS Acciari2010 and subsequently verified by MAGIC Aleksic2014 .

The VLA image of this BL Lac shows a collinear jet/lobe structure with roughly co-linear jets extending to the north, PA of 10 , and south PA of 175 Rector2003 . Our 650 MHz uGMRT image (Figure fig6:PG1424 ) shows a morphology resembling a double-lobed Seyfert galaxy e.g., see Gallimore2006,Das2021 . The core and lobes are both polarized. The spectral index image shows both the core to be relatively flat and then the spectra steepens towards the lobes. We find that the magnetic field is parallel to the lobe directions and also parallel to the VLBI jet direction in the core.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1424+240 as a blazar with unknown redshift, reports its VERITAS VHE discovery, and summarizes its spectrum and multiwavelength SED modeling.

quotes.quote: PKS 1424+240 was detected as a radio source and was classified as a blazar from optical polarisation studies . Non-thermal X-rays were later reported from this source . The redshift of PKS 1424+240 is unknown. A lower limit on the redshift of z > 0.06 was measured by and limit of z < 0.67 was obtained by , both assuming a minimum luminosity of the host galaxy. PKS 1424+240 was observed by VERITAS for about 37.3 hours between 2009 February 19 and June 21, 65 of the data having been taken with 3 telescopes.
The analysis of the data resulted in the discovery of PKS 1424+240. The statistical significance of the observed excess of gamma-ray events is 8.5 standard deviations .

sources.summary: The paper argues that PKS 1424+240 is very likely a member of a newly found z=0.6010 galaxy group, fixing the blazar redshift near 0.6035.

quotes.quote: PKS 1424+240 is a BL-Lac blazar with unknown redshift that was detected at high-energy gamma rays by Fermi-LAT with a hard spectrum. At very high energy (VHE), it was first detected by VERITAS and later confirmed by MAGIC. Its spectral energy distribution is highly attenuated at VHE gamma rays, which is coherent with distant sources. Several estimations enabled the redshift to be constrained to the range 0.6 < z < 1.3 .

This implies that the possible membership of PKS 1424+240 in the group found at z=0.6010 has an occurrence probability of 98 . Moreover, if the most reliable redshift lower limit determined by is taken into account, i.e. z > 0.6 , the probability for our observations detecting a group with eight or more members by chance is practically zero, which would increase the probability of PKS 1424+240 being a member of the newly identified group to nearly 100 .

In summary, we propose that the group of eight or more members found near PKS 1424+240 at z = 0.6010 0.003 is the group that is hosting this blazar, and that very likely the true redshift of PKS 1424+240 is z = 0.6035 .

sources.summary: The paper models PKS 1424+240 as a luminous high-frequency blazar whose SED resists one-zone SSC fits but can be described by multi-zone, EIC, hadronic, or UHECR scenarios.

quotes.quote: \ is a luminous high-frequency blazar, with a peak luminosity similar to the one from 3C 279, but with a peak frequency similar to Markarian 421 (in its low state); it is currently the most distant VHE blazar detected in a non-flaring state, and thus a unique source of VHE photons. This work presents a systematic modeling attempt of the emission from this source, comparing the results from different blazar emission scenarios in order to investigate the emission mechanism(s) at work in this peculiar blazar. We first investigated Synchrotron Self Compton (SSC) models including both the standard one-zone and multi-zone SSC models as well as a single-zone SSC model with contributions from external photons from the dusty torus in an External Inverse-Compton (EIC) model. We find that the standard one-zone SSC model (which usually satisfactorily describes the SED of HBLs) cannot describe the SED of PKS 1424+240, unless we adopt values of > 250 and we consider a systematic bias in the ray spectrum observed with Cherenkov telescopes.

We next investigated hadronic emission scenarios, including both ray emission from hadrons at the source as well as secondary emission from UHECR line-of-sight interactions. For the source emission models, no reasonable solution was found for a pure proton-synchrotron model; however good solutions were found for proton-synchrotron emission at lower energies with synchrotron emission from secondary leptons producing the VHE emission. Thus we show a hadronic scenario can provide a good description of the data, and naturally predicts a hardening at TeV energies.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS1424+240 as an HSP BL Lac, discusses previous redshift constraints and host-galaxy redshift, and estimates consistent low- and flare-state redshift ranges.

quotes.quote: PKS1424+240 PKS1424+240 is classified as a HSP ( 10^ 15 Hz < _ pk ). reported the redshift of z>0.6 and gave the redshift of z> 0.67 . also estimated an upper limit of 1.19. Combining the observed SEDs in the GeV and TeV bands, fitted the EBL-corrected spectra with power laws to get z=0.24 0.05 . studied the redshift of its likely host galaxy to give z= 0.601 0.003 .

PKS1424+240 . In the low state, comparing the results by three types of EEDs, we find that ^2/ d.o.f and the constraints on model parameters are comparable. We can not distinguish three types of EEDs from modeling the SEDs directly (see Fig 1-2 and table 1-3), but we get a similar redshift, which is from 0.56 to 0.67.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 1424+240 as a hard, distant VHE BL Lac whose SED is fit acceptably by the ALP-coupled GCA model when CIBER excess is included.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0229+200, 1ES 1101-232, 1ES 0347-121 and PKS 1424+240, may be hard spectrum sources with an intrinsic photon index _ VHE 1.5 . PKS 1424+240 is one of the most distant currently known VHE ray emitter and its red-shift was confirmed in 2017 .

fig:4 (left) shows the SED of PKS 1424+240(2009), overlaid with the three best-fitting spectra according to Eq.( eq:model ): GEBL (green line), GC (black line) and GCA(red line). Fig. fig:4 (right) reveals that the ALP/photon conversion in extragalactic medium plays an important role in shaping the harness of theoretical spectrum at VHE. Obviously the GC model cannot fit the VHE spectrum well, and the GCA model achieve an acceptable fitting. This improvement to the goodness of fit ( ^ 2 ) can be reflected quantitatively from the resulting ^ 2 : achieve with ^ 2 =4.78 relative to that from GC model spectrum. Upon closer inspection of the SED, the improvement is a result of photon/ALP conversion in jet and extragalactic space during the beam propagation, where a part of HE photons damping due to the conversion but partial VHE photons avoid to be absorbed by the EBL soft photons and lead to a hard spectrum at VHE.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 1424+240 as a BL Lac with constrained optical/radio components, campaign-level VHE flux changes, and measured MOJAVE jet component motion.

quotes.quote: 2014A&A...567A.135A found a common increasing trend in radio and optical light curves of PKS 1424+240 and used this to constrain the contribution of the two components to the optical part of the SED. For PKS 1424+240, no variability was found during 2014 (MJD 56740-56826) and 2015 (MJD 57045-57187) campaigns. However, the VHE gamma-ray flux of the 2015 campaign was 60 of the one observed during the 2014 campaign. Therefore, the data are divided into the 2014 and 2015 campaigns.

PKS 1424+240 has been observed three times in 2013-2016 in the framework of the MOJAVE programme. Significant motion was detected for two components. The fastest component has a maximum apparent speed of _ app =2.83 0.89 .

sources.summary: The paper revisits PKS 1424+240, a bright TeV BL Lac, but its new Lick/KAST spectrum is featureless and cannot confirm the proposed z=0.6047 emission-line redshift.

quotes.quote: PKS 1424+240 This very bright TeV BL Lac has been sometimes classified as an ISP Nieppo06 and sometimes as an HSP Fer4LAC20 . Its redshift has been constrained to be z 0.603 with HST-COS detection of intervening Ly absorbers Fur13 . A galaxy group at z = 0.601 has been possibly associated to it Rov16 . Moreover, the low significance detection of weak (EW 0.1 ) emission lines at z = 0.6047 Pai17a in a very high S/N GTC spectrum has been reported.

We observed PKS 1424+240 with Lick/KAST for 900 s to investigate this result. The resulting S/N = 107 spectrum (see Fig. fig_spec3 , bottom panel, on the left) is featureless but does not have the sensitivity to detect the aforementioned lines. We cannot confirm the proposed redshift.

sources.summary: PKS 1424+240 is a bright gamma-ray and TeV blazar; low-state GTC spectroscopy strengthens [O II]/[O III] detections and confirms z=0.6045.

quotes.quote: PKS 1424+240 is a very bright ray and TeV blazar, suggested as promising candidate neutrino emitter Aartsen20 . A featureless spectrum was reported by Shaw13 , while Fur13 determined a lower limit z 0.6035 from the detection of hydrogen absorption systems in HST/COS spectra. A galaxy group at z 0.60 was identified in its environment and possibly associated with it Rov16 .

We detected an historical minimum of PKS 1424+240 in March 2021 through the Tuorla monitoring: the flux level dropped to R = 14.70 0.03, 0.9 magnitudes lower than the values obtained during previous observations. We observed PKS 1424+240 on 2021 April 22 using OSIRIS on GTC (programme GTC02-21ADDT). Details are given in Table 1. The spectrum has a high S/N (305) and a power-law shape (Fig. fig_spec2 , second row panel, on the right). In it, we detected [ OII ] , [ OIII ] a and [ OIII ] b at EW = 0.15, 0.10, and 0.35 , respectively, with S/N ranging from 5 to 17 (see Table tabeqw2 ). The lines are thus about three times more intense than the previous detection (Fig. fig_PKS_old-new ), which demonstrates the usefulness of low state spectroscopy for BL Lacs. The computed redshift is z = 0.6045 0.0002, slightly higher than the value in Pai17b .

sources.summary: PKS 1424+240 is described as a newly detected VERITAS blazar, first found in gamma rays by Fermi, with weak VHE emission and multiwavelength constraints.

quotes.quote: The third IBL, and the latest blazar, to be discovered at very high energies by VERITAS is PKS 1424+240. This source, with an unknown redshift, was first detected in gamma rays by Fermi . The VERITAS detection came from 14 h of data taken in Spring 2009 .

The source was relatively weak at VHE energies, at a flux level 2 Crab Nebula above 200 GeV. PKS 1424+240 is the first VHE discovery motivated by Fermi observations. As shown in Figure 5, joint analysis of the VERITAS and Fermi data provides constraints on both the redshift of the source as well as the inverse-Compton model parameters .

sources.summary: The paper models PKS 1424+240 as the most distant VHE source then known, using VERITAS and SSC constraints while assuming photon-ALP mixing in its BL Lac jet.

quotes.quote: PKS 1424+240 A lower limit on the redshift of z 0.6035 of this intermediate peaked synchrotron blazar (IBL) was recently determined making it the most distant VHE source today. It has been observed by MAGIC for 33.6 hours between 2009 and 2011 and VERITAS in two campaigns for 28.5 and 67 hours. The second deep VERITAS observation campaign results in a significant detection beyond 500 GeV or = 4.1 using the lower limit on z . This spectrum will be used here for the simulations together with the same observation time.

Thus, we will only assume a photon-ALP mixing within the BL Lac jet with the values of the two-zone SSC model fit. We set the maximum scale of the coherent magnetic field in the jet to 1 kpc (and to zero above) and the angle between the transversal magnetic field and the propagation direction to 45^ (as done in ).

sources.summary: The paper presents Gemini spectroscopy of PKS 1424+240, describing it as a BL Lac blazar with uncertain redshift and one of the most distant VHE blazars.

quotes.quote: PKS 1424+240 is a BL-Lac blazar with unknown redshift detected at HE by Fermi-LAT with a hard spectrum . It was first detected at VHE by VERITAS , and latter confirmed by MAGIC . A z 0.24 for the redshift of PKS 1424+240 was estimated using a statistical approach that correlates the drop in the gamma-ray spectra to real redshift measurements from known sources , which strongly depends on the assumption that all blazars behave the same way and that there are not features in the gamma-ray spectra.

All these results place PKS 1424+240 in the very interesting condition to be one of the few most distant blazars detected at VHE, with redshift in a range never populated by other VHE blazar, i.e. 0.6 < z < 1.19 . This ambiguity in the redshift is still large enough to prevent precise studies of the EBL and the intrinsic blazar spectrum. We submitted a proposal in September, 2014, to realize spectroscopic observation of PKS 1424+240 and galaxies in its environment with the Gemini North telescope in Hawaii.

sources.summary: The paper uses PKS 1424+240 as a test blazar, finding that void-driven EBL inhomogeneity cannot explain its tentative VHE spectral hardening.

quotes.quote: Application to PKS 1424+240 had investigated the possibility that the unusually hard VHE ray spectra observed in some distant ( z 0.5 ) ray blazars might be due to a reduced EBL density caused by galaxy underdensities along the line of sight. Specifically, they investigated a possible correlation between galaxy-count underdensities based on the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) and the positions of hard-spectrum VHE blazars, and found a tentative hint for such a correlation (although the small sample size prevented the authors from drawing firm conclusions). Based on this result, as a first estimate of the effect of such underdensities on the EBL, they suggested a linear scaling of the line-of-sight galaxy density with the EBL opacity.

For the specific case of the distant z 0.6 , Furniss13 VHE blazar PKS 1424+240, they found that the reduction of the EBL resulting from such a direct linear scaling is not sufficient to remove the apparent spectral hardening of the VHE ray spectrum observed by VERITAS, when correcting for EBL absorption based on state-of-the-art (homogeneous) EBL models Archambault14 . For an assumed redshift of z = 0.6 for PKS 1424+240, our example case of R = 50 h^ 1  Mpc and z_v = 0.5 results in approximately the same galaxy-count underdensity factor as found by along the line of sight to this source.

For the specific case of PKS 1424+240, we have illustrated that the inferred (marginal) spectral hardening of the VHE ray spectrum, after correction for EBL absorption, if confirmed by future, more sensitive VHE ray observations, is most likely not an artifact of an over-estimation of the EBL opacity due to cosmic inhomogeneities.

sources.summary: The paper argues PKS 1424+240 is an IceCube-linked masquerading BL Lac, with quasar-like line/radio properties, high Eddington ratio, and unusual similarity to TXS 0506+056.

quotes.quote: We show that the blazar PKS 1424+240, which has been recently associated by IceCube with a neutrino excess at the 3.3 level together with three other sources, is similar to the first plausible non-stellar neutrino source, TXS 0506+056, in being also a masquerading BL Lac object, i.e., intrinsically a flat-spectrum radio quasar with hidden broad lines and a standard accretion disk. We point out that these two sources share other properties, including spectral energy distribution, high powers, parsec scale properties, and possibly radio morphology. We speculate that the relatively rare combination of proton-loaded jets, possibly typical of high-excitation sources, and efficient particle acceleration processes, related to their relatively high synchrotron peak frequencies, might favour neutrino production in these two sources.

We claim that PKS 1424+240 is yet another example of a masquerading BL Lac object linked to IceCube neutrinos. Following and P22 this classification is based on four criteria: its location on the radio power -- emission line power, P_ 1.4GHz - L_ O II , diagram Fig. 4 of Kalfountzou_2012 , which defines the locus of jetted (radio-loud) quasars. With a catalogued 1.4 GHz flux density of 0.43 Jy and a radio spectral index _ 0.4 (NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database) P_ 1.4GHz 5 10^ 26 W Hz ^ 1 , which, together with L_ O II 4 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 , puts this source exactly on the locus, as shown by Fig. fig:Lr_LOII ; a radio power P_ 1.4GHz > 10^ 26 W Hz ^ 1 , typical of HEGs; an Eddington ratio L/L_ Edd > 0.03 , hence > 0.01 , which is also one of the defining characteristics of HEGs.

sources.summary: The paper uses PKS 1424+240 as a moderately variable TeV blazar, adopting redshift 0.6, VERITAS spectra, Fermi-LAT flux history, SSC parameters, and group-environment magnetic fields.

quotes.quote: The blazar PKS 1424+240 has been observed by both VERITAS and MAGIC . The redshift of the source is uncertain, but current limits constrain 0.6 z 1.19 . Note that some sources set the lower limit of the redshift as low as 0.24 . In this paper, we set the blazar redshift to 0.6, which gives us a more conservative limit because it minimizes the value of yielding a smaller difference between the expected fluxes with and without ALP effects. The most recent observation by VERITAS (2013) extends to E 500 GeV , which corresponds to an optical depth of 4 for z = 0.6 .

Studies of the ray spectrum conducted by Aleksic et al. found that the emission from PKS1424 was best fit by a two-zone synchrotron self-Compton model with the following best-fit parameters: a magnetic field strength B = 0.033 G , Doppler factor = 30 and a radius of 4.8 10^ 16 cm of the VHE plasma emitting blob.

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 1424+240 as a Fermi-motivated VERITAS VHE discovery, with steady soft emission and a contemporaneous SED compatible with one-zone SSC modeling for redshifts below 0.66.

quotes.quote: PKS 1424+240 is an IBL with an unknown redshift. It was observed by VERITAS for 28.5 h of quality-selected live time, between February and June 2009, because of its inclusion in the Fermi Bright Source List . Soft-spectrum ( = 3.8 0.5 ) emission above 140 GeV is detected (370 , 8.5 ) with VERITAS , making this the first VHE discovery motivated by Fermi-LAT data. The VHE flux from VER J1427+238, 5 Crab, is steady within the observation period, as is the Fermi-LAT flux. A contemporaneous MWL SED is well-described by a one-zone SSC model regardless of the assumed redshift, provided that z < 0.66 . This limit is determined using a recent EBL model and combining the Fermi-LAT ( _ LAT = 1.73 ) and VERITAS spectra. Addition of an EC component does not improve the SED modeling, in contrast to the trend seen in other VERITAS IBL detections.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1424+240 as a very distant TeV source with an HST redshift lower limit above 0.6035 and unusually hard EBL-corrected intrinsic spectra.

quotes.quote: The most exciting new results in this area at the ICRC concerned two sources at the gamma-ray horizon. Hubble Space Telescope observations of hydrogen line absorption features in the optical spectrum of PKS 1424+240 have been used to place a firm lower limit on its redshift of >0.6035 , making this the most distant TeV source to be detected . The results of new VERITAS observations of this source were shown , as were MAGIC observations of another very distant TeV blazar, PG 1553+113, at z>0.4 .

For both PG 1553+133 and PKS 1424+240, the intrinsic spectra, corrected for absorption effects, appear unusually hard, with some indication of an upturn at the high-energy end of the spectrum. The strength of this feature depends on the EBL model which is applied, however, and stronger conclusions could be drawn with both better statistics at the highest energies and a firm redshift determination for each source.

sources.summary: The paper proposes a lepto-hadronic scenario for PKS 1424+240 in which UHE protons in the jet reproduce the GeV-TeV spectrum over redshifts 0.6 to 1.3.

quotes.quote: we propose a scenario for self-consistent interpretation of GeV - TeV spectrum of a distant blazar PKS 1424+240. In this scenario, ultra-high energy (UHE) protons are assumed to exist in the blazar jet and produce gamma rays through synchrotron emission emitted by relativistic protons and pair cascades (resulted from p interaction); meanwhile, some of these UHE protons may escape from the jet and are injected into intergalactic space. Therefore, we assume that UHE cosmic rays (CR) originate from relativistic protons in the jet and use energy-independent escape timescale to obtain UHECR injection spectrum.

Both contributions of gamma rays injected by the source and secondary gamma rays produced in interactions of UHECRs emitted by the blazar with photon background during their propagation through intergalactic space are calculated. Our results show that this scenario is able to reproduce the GeV-TeV spectrum of PKS 1424+240 self-consistently in a broad range of redshifts 0.6<z<1.3 .

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1424+240 as a distant HBL discovered by VERITAS, combining 2009 and 2013 datasets and finding marginal high-energy spectral hardening after EBL correction.

quotes.quote: PKS 1424+240 is a HBL discovered by VERITAS in 2009 that was recently found to be very distant, with z>0.6 . The early 2009 VERITAS dataset was complemented by a deep 2013 dataset and both were analyzed over the 1-750 GeV energy range . Measured and deabsorbed spectra for these datasets are shown in Fig. 1424Plot using the EBL model from Gilmore . Unfortunately, the flux in 2013 was less than in 2009, so while the detection of the source is improved with the factor of 2.3 in exposure(14.4 vs. 8.5 ), we did not extend the energy for which there was a spectral measurement. In both datasets a marginal hardening in the spectrum at higher energies is seen after correcting for EBL absorption.

sources.summary: The paper measures faint [O II] and [O III] emission from PKS 1424+240, assigning z=0.6047 and linking the absorber redshift to the BL Lac object.

quotes.quote: PKS 1424+240 : The source was classified as BLL by fleming1993 and a featureless spectra was reported by marcha1996, white2000 and shaw2013a . furniss2013 , from the Ly _ and Ly _ absorptions observed in the far-UV spectra from HST/COS ( Hubble Space Telescope /Cosmic Origins Spectrograph) spectra, reported a lower limit z > 0.6035. This is consistent with the non detection of the host galaxy in HST images scarpa2000 . In our high SNR 350 optical spectrum, we detect two faint emission lines at 5981 and 8034 (see Fig. fig:spectraCU ) due to [ O II ] 3727 and [ O III ] 5007 . The redshift corresponding to this identification is 0.6047, suggesting that the absorber at that redshift limit is associated to the BLL.

sources.summary: The paper models PKS 1424+240 as an HBL with unknown redshift, VERITAS VHE detection, steady Fermi/LAT contemporaneous emission, and one-day X-ray variability.

quotes.quote: PKS 1424+240. A HBL object with an unknown redshift, its VHE emission was detected by VERITAS with a flux normalization at 200 GeV of 5.1 10^ 11 TeV ^ 1 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 (Acciari et al. 2010c). The photon spectrum above 140 GeV can be described well by a power law with 3.8 . During the period from February 2009 to June 2009, the flux of VHE emission was steady and the contemporaneous observation of Fermi /LAT also did not detect any variability (Acciari et al. 2010c). The broadband SED is established by simultaneous observations of VERITAS, Fermi /LAT, Swift and MDM.

sources.summary: The paper finds that broken-power-law and BLR-absorption scenarios fit PKS 1424+240 better than a power law, with fit statistics suggesting additional spectral-shaping mechanisms.

quotes.quote: BPL and full BLR absorption scenarios seem to fit well the combined spectra of the blazars PKS 1424+240 and PKS 2155-304. A BPL (full BLR absorption) function is preferred over the PL for the blazars PKS 1424+240 and PKS 2155-304 with a significance of 5 ( 4.8 ) and 12 ( 8.5 ), respectively (see Figure fig:gevTevFits ). The ^ 2 /dof values of PL, BPL and APL fits are 32/9, 3.5/7, 5.3/7 for PKS 1424+240 and 148/8, 5.8/6, and 71/6 for PKS 2155-304, respectively. BPL fits yield =1.4 (PKS 1424+240) and =0.7 (PKS 2155-304), both larger than what electron cooling would predict, which might indicate that an additional mechanism is at work.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1424+240 as a VHE BL Lac source in its monitoring sample and reports its optical/radio flux level, variability trends, and correlation behavior.

quotes.quote: PG 1424+240 : A significant increasing trends extending through several years is found in both optical and radio light curves. In addition the optical light curve shows several fast outburst, the one starting around MJD 55600 is marginally visible in the radio light curve. In it was concluded that optical emission originates in two components, one connected to variable high energy emission and one to 15GHz VLBA core. For the study presented here, we have added two more years of data, which increases the significance of the radio trend and decreases that of the optical, but both remain significant.

sources.summary: The paper gives PKS 1424+240 an individual blazar variability note, summarizing TeV-band detections, GeV or hard-X-ray behavior, and variability classification.

quotes.quote: PKS 1424+240 : At the redshift of z=0.60 2017ApJ...837..144P , this is the most distant blazar among the sources considered in this work. This blazar was significantly detected in several time bins of the TeV band light curve and also by the VERITAS and MAGIC telescopes 2010ApJ...708L.100A,2014A&A...567A.135A,2014ApJ...785L..16A . The TeV flux variations were accompanied by that observed in the GeV band light curve which was confirmed by the overlapping Bayesian blocks. The results of the analysis carried out during the high-activity period (MJD 55357 56707) also supported this finding though a strong claim cannot be made due to the large uncertainties in the coefficient (Table tab:dcf ).

sources.summary: PKS 1424+240 returned a negative global regression coefficient, but shorter intervals showed hints of behavior while the optical spectrum stayed nearly constant.

quotes.quote: PKS 1424+240: The source was observed during 94 nights in 2009--2012. While the global regression fit returns negative CM correlation coefficient, the analysis of shorter intervals reveals some hints for behavior. The observed flux variations were only models ( 0.5 mag), and the optical spectrum remained basically constant, _ BR 3 .

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 1424+240 as a bright IHBL blazar in IceCube point-source excesses, with parsec-scale properties similar to HBLs.

quotes.quote: A search for point sources in the IceCube 10 year data collection, which includes events with energy typically 1 TeV, found an excess of neutrinos at the 2.9 level from the direction of the local ( z = 0.004 ) Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068 and a 3.3 excess in the northern sky due to significant p-values in the directions of NGC 1068 and three blazars: TXS 0506+056, PKS 1424+240, and GB6 J1542+6129 2020PhRvL.124e1103A .

An upcoming paper (Padovani et al., 2022, in preparation) concludes, based on VLBI data, that the two neutrino candidates TXS 0506+056 and PKS 1424+240 have overall parsec-scale properties similar to HBLs and different from those of LBLs.

The search for point-like sources in the 10 year IceCube sample 2020PhRvL.124e1103A resulted in a 3.3 excess over the expected background associated with the bright Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068 and the three blazars PKS 1424+240, TXS 0506+056, and GB6 J1542+6129, all of which are bright (f _ radio 200 mJy) IHBL objects with very similar overall SEDs, as illustrated in Figure fig:sedcomp .

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 1424+240 as a blazar contributing to the candidate-source neutrino excess, with potential emission and an increased local significance in the updated analysis.

quotes.quote: The main contributors are the active galaxy NGC 1068 and the blazars TXS 0506+056, PKS 1424+240 and GB6 J1542+6129.

Besides NGC 1068, the other two objects contributing to the excess are the blazars PKS 1424+240 and TXS 0506+056, for which we find potential neutrino emission with local significance of 3.7 and 3.5 , respectively.

While the local significance of PKS 1424+240 increased from 3.0 to 3.7 , the local significance of GB6 J1542+61 decreased from 2.9 to 2.2 , falling now below the threshold corresponding to the best binomial p-value.

sources.summary: The paper lists PKS 1424+240 as one of three active-galaxy neutrino-source candidates, contributing to a combined excess with a local significance of 3.7.

quotes.quote: Additionally, we find evidence for neutrino emission from the active galaxies PKS 1424+240 and TXS 0506+056.
The most recent search of the neutrino sky has revealed three sources : the active galaxies NGC 1068, PKS 1424+240, and TXS 0506+056.

A binomial scan of the list of sources reveals that three dominant sources contribute an excess with a combined significance of 3.4 after correcting for all trials: NGC 1068, and two additional sources PKS 1424+240 and TXS 0506+056, with local significances of 3.7 and 3.5 , respectively.

gmw_00001535.name: PKS 1440 389
gmw_00001535.overview: PKS 1440 389 is a blazar and BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2012.05176, arXiv:2004.03306] located at a redshift of z = 0.1385 [arXiv:2012.05176]. The active galaxy features a compact radio jet [arXiv:2310.10206] and exhibits variable flux states in the GeV band [arXiv:2401.11762].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 1440 389 as a GeV/TeV source, measures z=0.1385 from absorption features, and reports an average host-galaxy magnitude.

quotes.quote: PKS 1440 389, in addition to its strong GeV gamma-ray emission detected by Fermi LAT, is also a bright TeV source Abda20 . A tentative redshift of z = 0.069 has been reported from 6dF low S/N spectroscopy Jon04 . Later, spectra with higher S/N (up to 80) taken by Shaw13 and Lan15 were featureless and could not confirm this result. The magnitudes of the source at the times these spectra were taken are unknown.

Very recently, combining optical Shaw13 and gamma-ray results, the redshift has been constrained to be in the range 0.14 z 0.53 at the 95 confidence limit Abda20 . We were able to obtain a very high S/N EFOSC2 spectrum (S/N 200, see Table tabres1 ) of PKS 1440 389 (see Fig. fig_spec1 , second row, right). Careful inspection of the spectrum reveals the presence of CaHK, CaIG and NaID features at z = 0.1385.
The host galaxy magnitude is average, M _R = -22.4 0.2.

sources.summary: The paper discusses PKS 1440-389 as a bright radio BL Lac/HSP blazar monitored by TANAMI, with uncertain redshift and VHE gamma-ray detection.

quotes.quote: PKS 1440-389 \ \ ; Jackson02 was first detected as a bright radio source in the Parkes survey WO90 . The source has been observed repeatedly by the TANAMI Tracking of AGN with Austral Milliarcsecond Interferometry, Ojha10 project between 2010 and 2016. present three quasi-simultaneous SEDs of PKS 1440-389, which indicate a synchrotron peak at 10^ 15  Hz _ sy 10^ 16  Hz, consistent with an HSP classification.

In the first data release of the 6dF Galaxy Survey 2004MNRAS.355..747J , the redshift of the source is listed as z=0.065 , but this redshift value is no longer included in the final version of the 6dF catalogue 2009MNRAS.399..683J due to the poor quality of the optical spectrum. Despite many follow-up observations in different wavelength regimes, the redshift of \ remains uncertain due to its featureless continuum spectrum e.g., 2015AJ....149..163L . The current constraint from optical spectroscopic observations is 0.14 < z < 2.2 2013ApJ...764..135S .

sources.summary: The paper gives PKS 1440 389 an individual blazar variability note, summarizing TeV-band detections, GeV or hard-X-ray behavior, and variability classification.

quotes.quote: PKS 1440 389 : This blazar has not exhibited any significant flux variability in the TeV band. It was sporadically detected throughout the 15 years of the LAT operation and and flux enhancement was seen mainly in the later half of the covered time period. The GeV band flux showed a similar behavior and the brightest GeV flux identified in the time bin MJD 59002 59092 had a TeV band counterpart. The Bayesian block analysis has found variable flux states.

sources.summary: The paper notes prior TANAMI imaging of 1440 389, a compact S-band jet modeled by one component, and VHE plus recent Fermi-LAT high-state detections.

quotes.quote: 1440 389: 8.4 GHz images from TANAMI were presented in müller18 . The source shows a compact structure in S band with a jet that can be modeled by a single component. 1440 389 has first been detected at VHE in 2012 hofmann12 , and have been recently detected in a high state by the Fermi LAT for the first time ciprini22 . h

gmw_00001536.name: RGB J2056+496
gmw_00001536.overview: RGB J2056+496 is a high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lac blazar [arXiv:1104.3006, arXiv:2105.00983] that exhibits very-high-energy gamma-ray emission [arXiv:1708.02374, arXiv:2105.00983]. The source displays variability in both radio and X-ray bands [arXiv:2105.00983], although its TeV-band emission is consistent with a non-variable state [arXiv:2401.11762].

sources.summary: The paper links 1RXS J205644.3+494011 to a multiwavelength high-energy source, models its radio-to-X-ray SED, and argues that its broad-band properties support an HSP BL Lac blazar interpretation.

quotes.quote: The radio source 4C +49.35, classified as symmetric double in NED, is found within the intersection of these circles. Radio measurements at 1.4 GHz reported in the NVSS catalogue ( ) resolve two components separated by 3' : a North-East component (RA= 20^h 56^m 42^s.69 , Dec= +49^ 40' 05''.6 ) with a flux density F_ NE =167 mJy and a South-West component (RA= 20^h 56^m 29^s.45 , Dec= +49^ 38' 01''.0 ) with F_ SW =124 mJy. A soft X-ray emission was detected in this region for the first time by the Ariel V satellite and reported by Warwick et al. (1981) with the name 3A 2056+493.

Recently, Haakonsen Rutledge (2009) reported a low probability of association between 1RXS J205644.3+494011 and the star in their statistical cross-association of the ROSAT Bright Source Catalogue ( ) and the 2MASS Point Source Catalogue ( ). As reported by Landi et al. (2010) the counterpart of this high-energy source is, with high probability, an object located at RA= 20^h 56^m 42^s.719 and Dec= +49^ 40' 06''.9 at only 12''.9 from SAO 50269. We collect for the first time all the available data in literature to build the SED of this object, that is reported in Fig. figura06 , to investigate the broad-band emission properties of u.

More precise measurements are requested to characterise both emission components of this source. Nevertheless, the analysis of its broad-band properties such as the radio spectral index, the low Compton dominance and the synchrotron peak frequency higher than 10^ 16 Hz support for u the classification as a blazar and in particular as a HSP BL Lac object, but only the analysis of the optical spectrum can confirm this interpretation.

sources.summary: The paper reports VERITAS observations of RGB J2056+496, identifying it as a hard-spectrum blazar with newly detected VHE gamma-ray emission and simultaneous Swift coverage.

quotes.quote: RGB J2056+496 is one of the brightest, hard-spectrum ( _ 2FHL 2.3 ) objects in the Fermi LAT 2FHL ( > 50 GeV) catalog not directly observed prior to the 2016-17 season by VERITAS. This blazar, of unknown redshift, is also a XMM-Newton source and a Swift-BAT source. It was observed with the array for 9.0 h of good-quality live time between October 10, 2016 and November 9, 2016. A preliminary analysis of these observations yields an excess of 120 events above the background at the position of the blazar, corresponding to a statistical significance of 6.3 standard deviations ( ).

The VERITAS collaboration is interpreting this excess as the discovery of VHE ray emission from the blazar, noting that the blazar is only 13 arc-seconds from the micro-quasar candidate LS III +49 13, and the two objects are effectively co-located for VERITAS. The observed VHE spectrum from RGB J2056+496 is shown in Figure Misc_spec . Four Swift XRT / UVOT observations were taken simultaneous with the November VERITAS observations of this blazar, which comprise 85 of the data set.

sources.summary: The review describes IGR J20569+4940 as a TeV blazar-like INTEGRAL source associated with Fermi and radio counterparts, classified as a highly absorbed high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lac object.

quotes.quote: One extra source, IGR J20569+4940 was up to now generically classified as a blazar, but of unknown class and redshift 2016MNRAS.462.3180C . This source has only recently been announced as a TeV emitter 2017ICRC...35..641B after observations with VERITAS, and it is also associated with a Fermi source (2FGL J2056.7+4939, 2016ApJS..222....5A,2020ApJ...892..105A ); it has a bright radio counterpart (NVSS 205642+494005) with a flat 2.7-10 GHz spectrum 2000A&A...363..141R , as typically observed in radio-loud AGNs. It appears to be variable both in radio 2011ApJ...737...45O and in X-rays 2010MNRAS.403..945L . Recently, using a Keck/LRIS spectrum, 2019ApJ...887...32C classified this source as a highly absorbed BL Lac object on the basis of a red and featureless continuum; the optical extinction is consistent with the high column density found in X-rays, which can be ascribed to both Galactic and intrinsic absorption.

In Figure igrj20569 , we assemble high energy data on this source using a NuSTAR observation that was performed in November 2015 with the average INTEGRAL and Fermi/LAT spectra with the addition of the reported TeV data: as can be seen in the figure the source SED probably has a peak in the GeV region. This SED resembles that of IGR J19443+2117 = HESS J1943+213 (see also Table hbl ), also in our list of associations, and one of the most extreme high peaked blazars shining through the Galactic plane. Therefore, we conclude that IGR J20569+4940 is another example of a high synchrotron peaked BL Lac, as originally proposed by 2016DPS....4850902F .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NVSS J205642+494005 as a partially resolved radio source coincident with X-ray, optical, infrared, and high-energy detections; its spectrum favors an extragalactic AGN, possibly a quasar or blazar.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The radio source is centered at the J2000.0 position 20^h 56^m 42.738^s and +49^ 40^ 06.55^ , with an estimated uncertainty of about 0.01^ in each coordinate.

quotes.quote: NVSS J205642+494005 appears as partially resolved. An elliptical Gaussian fit using the AIPS task JMFIT gives a deconvolved angular size of (290 11) (173 14) mas ^2 , with a position angle of 17 5^ . The radio source is centered at the J2000.0 position 20^h 56^m 42.738^s and +49^ 40^ 06.55^ , with an estimated uncertainty of about 0.01^ in each coordinate.

The coincidence in position within astrometric error of the X-ray source 1RXS J205644.3+494011, the flat-spectrum radio emitter NVSS J205642+494005, the point-like infrared and optical sources 2MASS J20564271+4940068 / IPHAS J205642.74+494006.7 and all high energy error boxes listed in Table conioya , strongly points to all being the same astronomical source.

Therefore, the spectrum of this source does not resemble to be stellar and the possibility of being an extragalactic object is thus reinforced. If this is the case, the point-like appearance of the optical/infrared counterpart would be typical of a distant AGN (possibly a quasar or a blazar).

sources.summary: The review describes IGR J20569+4940 as a blazar-like high-energy source of uncertain class and redshift, with VERITAS VHE emission, high synchrotron peak, and HBL/extreme BL Lac interpretation.

quotes.quote: A peculiar source is IGR J20569+4940. It is certainly a blazar but still of unknown class and redshift: the source has recently been announced as a VHE emitter 2017ICRC...35..641B after observations with VERITAS performed in November 2016. It is most likely a BL Lac 2016MNRAS.462.3180C with a high synchrotron peak 2016ApJS..226...20F .

In Figure nufnu , we assembled high energy data on this source using an unpublished NuSTAR observation performed in November 2015 with the average INTEGRAL and Fermi /LAT spectra plus the reported TeV data. As evident from the figure, two peaks can be located, one below the NuSTAR low energy threshold, at around few keV, likely associated to the synchrotron part of the SED, and the other at around 0.1-0.3 TeV, probably related to the Compton part of the SED. This qualifies IGR J20569+4940 to be another HBL or alternatively another extreme BL Lac.

sources.summary: The paper gives RGB J2056+496 an individual blazar variability note, summarizing TeV-band detections, GeV or hard-X-ray behavior, and variability classification.

quotes.quote: RGB J2056+496 : This object was occasionally detected in the TeV band at a flux level consistent with the 15 years averaged value. The calculated parameter suggested this blazar to be non-variable. This object was also detected with the VERITAS 2016ATel.9721....1M . The hard X-ray band light curve of this object showed an enhanced flux state around MJD 57524 (Figure fig:2 ), however, the presence/absence of the TeV counterpart could not be quantified due to sparseness of the data points.

gmw_00001537.name: NGC 2449
gmw_00001537.overview: NGC 2449 is a spiral galaxy classified as an Sab-type [arXiv:astro-ph/0304553]. It is located near the galaxies IC 746 and IC 2205, and it has been detected in neutral hydrogen observations [arXiv:astro-ph/0304553].

sources.summary: The paper argues the Arecibo H I detection toward the 2MASS/IC 746 target is probably nearby spiral NGC 2449, matching previous Arecibo velocity, flux, and linewidth measurements.

quotes.quote: S1062O : 2MASX J07471628+2657037 = IC 746: our \ detection appears to be of nearby spiral galaxy NGC 2449, rather than of the target 2MASS object. Our spectrum centered on the 2MASS galaxy shows V_ HI =4776 , I_ HI =1.4 \ and W_ 50 =248 , while its optical redshift is 229 \ higher, 5005 39 . NGC 2449, which lies only 1 5 from the target galaxy and well within the Arecibo HPBW, is an Sab spiral of B_T =14.13 mag and D_ 25 = 1 4 , with V_ opt =4817 34 . A detection of NGC 2449 at Arecibo (Bicay Giovanelli 1986), shows V_ HI =4778 , I_ HI =2.0 \ and W_ 50 =221 . Two other galaxies of similar redshift, NGC 2449 ( V_ opt = 4801 33 ) and IC 2205 ( V_ opt =4726 43 ), both about one magnitude fainter in B than NGC 2449, lie about 6 ' from the target galaxy.

gmw_00001538.name: NGC 2944 pair
gmw_00001538.overview: The NGC 2944 pair is an interacting galaxy pair also known as Arp 63 [arXiv:astro-ph/0304553]. Its two nuclei have a projected separation of 16 arcseconds, and optical spectroscopy of the system has revealed H-alpha and nitrogen II emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0304553].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2944 as an interacting pair split into two nuclei in 2MASS images, with prior H I detections whose Nancay flux is likely confused by UGC 5146/Arp 129.

quotes.quote: S1317O : 2MASXI J0939167+321838 = NGC 2944 pair: interacting galaxy pair (= Arp 63). The separation of NGC 2944 into two galaxies is best seen on the 2MASS images, where the projected distance of their nuclei is 16 '' , as the DSS image is saturated in the inner regions. The selected 2MASS source corresponds to the centre of the least bright, W nucleus. Optical spectroscopy (Maehara et al. 1987) only showed H + [ N ii ] emission lines, with an equivalent width of 57 \ for H .

NGC 2944 was detected previously in \ at Arecibo (Williams Kerr 1981) and at Nan ay, (Chamaraux 1977). The central \ velocities measured at the two telescope differ significantly: 6783 and 6753 \ at Arecibo compared to 6831 \ at Nan ay. The much higher Nan ay I_ HI of 13.6 \ compared to the 4.0 \ we measured at Arecibo can be explained partially by confusion within the larger Nan ay beam with the interacting UGC 5146 pair (=Arp 129), at 3 7 separation, for which Bicay Giovanelli (1986) measured at Arecibo V_ HI =6862, I_ HI =4.8 \ and W_ 50 =163 .

gmw_00001539.name: UGC 5156
gmw_00001539.overview: UGC 5156 is a galaxy with an optical redshift of 10,028 km/s [arXiv:astro-ph/0304553]. It has a partially band-limited neutral hydrogen profile that may be mildly confused by CGCG 122-47, a highly inclined neighboring galaxy of similar redshift and size located 5.8 arcminutes away [arXiv:astro-ph/0304553].

sources.summary: The paper reports that UGC 5156 has a 10,028 km/s optical redshift, was previously detected at Arecibo, and may have a partially band-limited, mildly confused H I profile.

quotes.quote: L388O : 2MASX J09403295+2529255 = UGC 5156: its optical redshift is 10,028 43 . It was detected previously in \ at Arecibo (Bicay Giovanelli 1986), at V_ HI =9953 , 174 \ higher than our central velocity, which appears to be due to the fact that our \ profile lies partially outside the search range. At 5 8 distance lies a galaxy of similar redshift ( V_ opt =10,082 43 ), size, magnitude and high inclination, CGCG 122-47. The \ profiles may in principle be confused by this object, as we estimate that about 25 of its line flux would be detected at the target position.

gmw_00001540.name: Ark 569
gmw_00001540.overview: Ark 569 is an SBbc spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0304553]. It has a neutral hydrogen velocity of 6000 km/s and a line width of 266 km/s based on Arecibo observations [arXiv:astro-ph/0304553].

sources.summary: The paper suggests the profile toward PGC 2012878 may instead be Ark 569, whose prior Arecibo velocity and width closely match the new pointing but with much larger flux.

quotes.quote: S2730P : 2MASX J22551306+3248080 = PGC 2012878: our \ profile may be a detection of nearby galaxy Ark 569, an SBbc spiral with B_T =15.6 and D_ 25 = 0 8 without a known optical redshift. At 4 3 distance, we estimate that about 30 of its line flux would be detected at the target position. An Arecibo detection (Giovanelli Haynes 1985) of Ark 569 shows V_ HI =6000 \ and W_ 50 =266 , values very close to the V_ HI =6018 \ and W_ 50 =270 \ we measured pointing towards the 2MASS object. The integrated \ line flux we measured towards the 2MASS galaxy, 0.4 , is 10 times smaller than the 4.0 \ measured towards Ark 569.

gmw_00001541.name: 2MASX J22580271+3227586
gmw_00001541.overview: The galaxy 2MASX J22580271+3227586 was previously detected in neutral hydrogen observations at the Arecibo Observatory [arXiv:astro-ph/0304553]. However, a subsequent survey failed to recover the galaxy, yielding an upper limit below the previously measured neutral hydrogen intensity [arXiv:astro-ph/0304553].

sources.summary: The paper notes that 2MASX J22580271+3227586 had a previous Arecibo H I detection, but was not recovered in the survey despite an upper limit below the earlier measurement.

quotes.quote: L802P : 2MASX J22580271+3227586 = PGC 2800849: detected previously in \ at Arecibo (Cabanela Dickey 1999), with V_ HI =6588 \ and I_ HI =1.5 , and an average of the W_ 50 and W_ 20 line widths of 283 . Surprisingly, the object was not detected in our survey, with an rms noise level of 1.19 mJy. For a flat-topped profile with width of 283 , this would imply a 3 upper limit to I_ HI of 1.0 , well below the value measured previously.

gmw_00001542.name: UGC 12289
gmw_00001542.overview: UGC 12289 is a spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0304553]. Its neutral hydrogen profile has been observed at a velocity of approximately 9,986 km/s, and it is located near the SBab spiral galaxy UGC 12283 [arXiv:astro-ph/0304553].

sources.summary: The paper reports an H I profile for UGC 12289 that partly falls outside the search band and is not expected to be strongly confused by nearby UGC 12283.

quotes.quote: L807O : 2MASX J22594147+2404297 = UGC 12289: our \ profile of the target galaxy ( V_ HI =9986 , W_ 50 =217 , I_ HI =4.3 ), with V_ opt =10,160 42 , lies partially outside our search range, which explains the 174 \ difference with the optical redshift and the Arecibo detection by Giovanelli et al. (1986), which shows V_ HI =10,160 , W_ 50 =218 \ and I_ HI =3.1 . The profile is not expected to be confused significantly by the B_T 16.1 SBab spiral UGC 12283, with V_ opt =9949 60 , which was detected at Arecibo by Giovanelli et al. (1986), who found V_ HI =9949 , W_ 50 =274 \ and I_ HI =0.7 . At 5 3 distance, we estimate that about 25 of its line flux would be detected at the target position.

gmw_00001543.name: KUG 2332+236
gmw_00001543.overview: KUG 2332+236 is an Sc-type spiral galaxy with no known optical redshift [arXiv:astro-ph/0304553]. Observations of its neutral hydrogen lines are heavily confused by two nearby galaxies, PGC 169947 and PGC 169948, which also lack optical redshifts [arXiv:astro-ph/0304553].

sources.summary: The paper reports two H I lines toward KUG 2332+236 and argues the lower-velocity detection is completely confused by nearby PGC 169947 and PGC 169948.

quotes.quote: S2834O : 2MASX J23343591+2353362 = KUG 2332+236: two \ lines were detected in our spectrum, at 5615 and 9802 , respectively. The B_T =16.5 Sc-type target galaxy has no known optical redshift. For the distance dependent parameters listed for S2834Oa and b in Table 4b.2 it was assumed that the \ is associated with the target galaxy. Our lower velocity detection ( V_ HI =5615 , I_ HI =0.9 , W_ 50 =298 ) will be completely confused by two nearby galaxies, both without known optical redshift, which were detected in \ at Arecibo by Spitzak Schneider (1998): the B_T =17.3 mag PGC 169947 ( V_ HI =5603 , I_ HI =1.7 , W_ 50 =162 ) at 2 1 , and the B_T =17.6 mag PGC 169948 ( V_ HI =5558 , I_ HI =2.7 , W_ 50 =109 ) at 5 1 distance.

gmw_00001544.name: ZF-8197
gmw_00001544.overview: ZF-8197 is a massive high-redshift galaxy that has only recently quenched its star formation within the last 100 million years [arXiv:2212.11638]. Spectroscopic observations reveal strong emission lines and a newly detected Balmer break, placing the galaxy at the boundary between star-forming and quiescent states [arXiv:2212.11638].

sources.summary: JWST/NIRSpec observations confirm ZF-8197 as a massive high-redshift galaxy with emission lines, a newly observable Balmer break, recent quenching, and boundary star-forming/quiescent colors.

quotes.quote: ZF-8197 has strong \ doublet detection that falls within the MOSFIRE- K band. Thus, S18 obtained a confident redshift confirmation with an on-source exposure time of 7h reaching an emission line S/N of 12 . Even though MOSFIRE observations reached a continuum S/N of 16 with 70 \ spectral binning, it was not possible to obtain signatures of absorption lines. Given the Balmer break largely falls within the atmospheric cutoff between H and K bands, it was not possible to confirm the post-starburst nature of this galaxy. However, with our new NIRSpec observations we are able to observe the Balmer break and other strong emission lines such as \ and .

ZF-8197 has only reached the 0.1 SFR _ main quenching definition very recently in the last 100 million years, while ZF-7329 has been quenched for 1 Gyr. Once rest U-V and V-J colors are recomputed for our sample at their spectroscopic redshifts with strong emission line contributions removed Antwi-Danso2022a , we find that all galaxies lie inside or near the border of the quiescent region. ZF-8197 was previously observed in S18 and was ruled out as a star-forming galaxy based on rest U-V and V-J colors, however with newer constraints from JWST, we find that it falls at the boundary between star-forming and quiescent.

gmw_00001545.name: ZF-7329
gmw_00001545.overview: ZF-7329 is a quiescent galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z = 3.2 [arXiv:2405.02242, arXiv:2212.11638]. It features an older stellar population that formed very early in cosmic history [arXiv:2405.02242, arXiv:2212.11638], having quenched for about 1 billion years prior to observation [arXiv:2212.11638].

sources.summary: The paper describes ZF-UDS-7329 as a z=3.19 fossilised quiescent galaxy with early extended formation, high stellar metallicity, measured Mg enhancement, and compact stellar-dominated structure.

quotes.quote: ZF-UDS-7329: a fossilised galaxy at z=3.19 The final galaxy in our sample, shown in the bottom panel of Fig. fig:spec2 , is ZF-UDS-7329 (PRIMER-EXCELS-55410). This object was selected from our PRIMER catalogue with a 99.94 per cent chance of being z>2 and quiescent, having been selected as a non-robust candidate in our earlier, pre-JWST analysis. We summarise the physical properties we derive for this galaxy from EXCELS and PRIMER in Table table:galaxies .

As can be seen from Figs fig:spec1 and fig:spec2 , this galaxy has a significantly redder spectral shape than the other 3 objects in our sample. This is largely due to its significantly older stellar population, as this object is at a much lower redshift whilst still having formed very early on in cosmic history. We derive a stellar mass of log _ 10 (M_*/ M_ )=11.14 0.03 for this object, slightly lower than (but consistent with) the value of log _ 10 (M_*/ M_ )=11.26^ +0.03 _ 0.16 reported by . The SFH we derive for this galaxy from full spectral fitting of our EXCELS data is shown in Fig. fig:sfh . We derive a formation redshift of z_ form =11.2^ +3.1 _ 2.1 for this galaxy, which again is consistent with the value of z_ form =10.4^ +4.0 _ 2.2 reported by .

sources.summary: JWST/NIRSpec places ZF-7329 at z=3.207, shows a smoother D4000-like break from an older stellar population, and indicates it formed half its mass 1.8 Gyr before observation and quenched for 1 Gyr.

quotes.quote: ZF-7329 was observed for 10h in K band with MOSFIRE reaching a continuum S/N of 17 . However, S18 was unable to obtain a spectroscopic redshift confirmation for this source due to the limited wavelength coverage. Our NIRSpec observations show the clear spectral shape of this object and our slinefit https://github.com/cschreib/slinefit redshift estimate puts it at z=3.207 . The Balmer break of this galaxy is smoother compared to our other five galaxies and the spectral features have transitioned to a D4000 break suggesting a much older underlying stellar population Bruzual1983 .

At the opposite end ZF-7329 reached its 50 of the stellar mass 1.8 billion years before it is observed redshift of z 3.2 . While all galaxies classify as quenched based on the S18 definition, it is clear that there is a large variety in quenching time scales. ZF-8197 has only reached the 0.1 SFR _ main quenching definition very recently in the last 100 million years, while ZF-7329 has been quenched for 1 Gyr.

gmw_00001546.name: RGB J0521.8+2112
gmw_00001546.overview: RGB J0521.8+2112 is a radio-loud active galaxy [arXiv:1111.1209, arXiv:2305.09935] classified as an intermediate-peaked BL Lac blazar [arXiv:2203.04332]. The source exhibits strong, highly variable emission across all wavebands [arXiv:1308.5017, arXiv:2305.09935], including very high energy gamma rays [arXiv:1111.1209, arXiv:2305.09935] and moving components within its radio jet [arXiv:2006.04493].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VER J0521+211 as a gamma-ray-emitting BL Lac blazar associated with RGB J0521.8+2112, reporting VERITAS VHE detection, point-like variable emission, and unknown redshift from optical spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: One of the few previously undetected hotspots was less than 0.1^ away from RGB J0521.8+2112: a radio-loud AGN with significant X-ray emission located 8.7^ off of the galactic plane. VERITAS observations centered at the position of RGB J0521.8+2112 were taken between 22 and 24 October 2009 (MJD 55126-55128), leading to the detection of a new VHE gamma-ray source: VER J0521+211 . An excess with significance of 5.5 was obtained during the first 230 minutes of exposure.

The excess distribution of VER J0521+211 is consistent with a point-like source and its location compatible with the radio position of RGB J0521.8+2112. Figure fig:0521 shows the derived gamma-ray light curve for energies >200 GeV in 1-day time bins and the energy spectrum obtained between 200 GeV and 1 TeV. The light curve shows significant variability.

The observations revealed a continuum-dominated spectrum, identifying the optical counterpart as a BL Lac-type blazar. No spectral absorption lines could be identified, and therefore the redshift of VER J0521+211 remains unknown.

sources.summary: The survey treats VER J0521+211 as a radio-loud BL Lac detected in TeV gamma rays, with uncertain redshift and a hard HAWC spectrum of particular interest.

quotes.quote: VER J0521+211 was discovered as a TeV ray source by the VERITAS collaboration, during observations following-up its detection above 30 GeV by LAT 2009ATel.2260....1O . 3FHL J0521.7+2112 itself is associated to the radio-loud BL Lac object TXS 0518+211, and the X-ray ROSAT source RX J0521.7+2112. The redshift survey of 2013ApJ...764..135S assigned z=0.108 to the optical counterpart. This is the value listed in the 3FHL catalog and assumed for this analysis. 2013ApJ...776...69A did not confirm this redshift, and neither did 2017ApJ...837..144P , who only set a lower limit z>0.18 using deep spectroscopy with the GTC 10.4 m.

The VHE emission from this object has been measured at least up to 1 TeV, with no apparent decline in the spectrum 2015ICRC...34..864P . This makes the high-energy characterization of VER J0521+211 of particular interest. The =2.5 search resulted in TS=9.49 for K=(2.85 0.93_ stat 0.43_ syst ) 10^ 12 TeV^ 1 cm^ 2 s^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper treats VER J0521+211 as an intermediate-peaked BL Lac source with unknown redshift, variable Fermi-LAT flux, VERITAS and HAWC optical depths, and SSC jet parameters.

quotes.quote: The intermediate-peaked BL Lac object VER J0521 was first discovered in 2009 and has now has been observed by VERITAS for 14.5 hours. The redshift of this source is still unknown, but a lower limit can be set at z > 0.18 . The VERITAS observations extend to 1.1 TeV, corresponding to an optical depth of 1.7 using the lower limit of the redshift. The HAWC observation at 7 TeV is at an optical depth of 4.2 . Observations in the 4FGL catalog detect this source at a significance of 120 and with a variability index of 809, which makes this source somewhat more variable than PKS 1424+240 and PG 1553+113.

A two-zone SSC model of the SED was performed by the MAGIC collaboration in 2020 . The zone responsible for the VHE emission has the following best-fit parameters: B = 0.1 G , = 12 and radius R = 1.3 10^ 16 cm . Given a viewing angle of = 1.8^o and a simple, conical jet geometry, the distance between the VHE emitting region and the jet is r_ VHE = 0.026 pc .

sources.summary: The paper models VHE flares of the BL Lac object VER J0521+211, deriving redshift limits and describing its variable multiwavelength blazar behavior.

quotes.quote: Observation of several very high energy (VHE) flaring events of the BL Lac object VER J0521+211 were reported by the VERITAS and MAGIC collaborations between 2009 and 2014. The redshift of this source is uncertain and several analysis have derived different limits for it. In the framework of the photohadronic model, and using three different extragalactic background light (EBL) models, we analyze seven independent VHE spectra of VER J0521+211 and determine the limiting values on its redshift.

It is further observed that the photohadronic scenario, along with the EBL model of Domínguez et al., puts the most restrictive limits on the redshift z of VER J0521+211: 0.29 z 0.31 from the confidence level (CL) intervals at 2 , or a more conservative 0.28 z 0.33 , at 3 .

VER J0521+211 was first detected as a very high energy gamma-ray (VHE > 100 GeV) source by VERITAS in the year 2009 2009ATel.2260....1O . The source is highly variable at all wavebands and is associated with the radio-loud active galaxy RGB J0521.8+2112. Several VHE flaring events from VER J0521+211 were observed between 2009 and 2014.

sources.summary: The paper reports VERITAS discovery of the TeV source VER J0521+211, finds strong multiwavelength variability, and classifies the counterpart as a BL Lac blazar with uncertain redshift.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: R.A. = 05^ h 21^ m 46^ s , Decl. = +21^ 12^ 51. ^ 5 (J2000), corresponding to the position of RGB J0521.8+2112 RGB J0521.8+2112

quotes.quote: We report the detection of a new TeV gamma-ray source, VER J0521+211 VER J0521+211 , based on observations made with the VERITAS imaging atmospheric Cherenkov telescope array. These observations were motivated by the discovery of a cluster of >30 GeV photons in the first year of \ observations. \ is relatively bright at TeV energies, with a mean photon flux of (1.93 0.13_ stat 0.78_ sys ) 10^ 11 cm 2 s 1 above 0.2 TeV during the period of the VERITAS observations. The source is strongly variable on a daily timescale across all wavebands, from optical to TeV, with a peak flux corresponding to 0.3 times the steady Crab Nebula flux at TeV energies. Follow-up observations in the optical and X-ray bands classify the newly-discovered TeV source as a BL Lac-type blazar with uncertain redshift, although recent measurements suggest z=0.108 .
\ exhibits all the defining properties of blazars in radio, optical, X-ray, and gamma-ray wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper models VER J0521+211 as a BL Lac in a multiwavelength SSC sample, finding no MAGIC variability in the campaign and reporting MOJAVE jet motion constraints.

quotes.quote: In this work we have selected a sub-sample of five of the BL Lacs from 2016A&A...593A..98L , based on the availability of MWL data (VER J0521+211, PKS 1424+240, 1ES 1727+502, 1ES 1959+650, 1ES 2344+514) with the aim of placing observational constraints on two-component SSC models. Independent from these common trends on long-term light curves, we also use optical polarisation data to disentangle the contribution of the two components and we take into account constraints on jet parameters from the VLBI observations.

In the case of VER J0521+211, we do not find any significant variability during 4 nights of MAGIC observation in 2013 2015ICRC...34..864P . VER J0521+211 has been observed seven times in the framework of the MOJAVE programme between 2014 and 2018. 2019ApJ...874...43L reported several moving components in the jet. The fastest component has a maximum jet speed of = 774 45 as yr ^ 1 which corresponds to an apparent projected speed of _ app = 7.72 0.42 considering z=0.18 .

sources.summary: VERITAS selected RGB J0521.8+2112 from nearby Fermi high-energy photons, detected significant variable VHE emission, and follow-up optical spectroscopy showed a BL Lac-like continuum without a redshift.

quotes.quote: RGB J0521.8+2112 was initially observed by VERITAS because of the identification of a nearby cluster of high-energy photons in a simple binned search ( > 30 GeV) of the initial public release of the Fermi-LAT photon data. VERITAS observed the likely blazar for 15 h of quality-selected live time between October 2009 and January 2010. An excess of VHE rays (VER J0521+211), corresponding to a statistical significance of 16 , is observed from the direction of RGB J0521.8+2112.
