The observed VHE flux is variable, with an average value of 5 Crab above 200 GeV, and the time-averaged VERITAS spectrum is soft ( = 3.47 0.19 ). Follow-up optical spectroscopy of the VERITAS source, with MDM and the MMT, reveal a continuum dominated spectrum typical for BL Lac objects. Unfortunately no absorption lines are identified and hence the redshift cannot determined.

sources.summary: The paper reports TXS 0518+211 has a slightly longer but still compatible period, lacks radio data in the authors' analysis, and has optical periods matching earlier work.

quotes.quote: The results of TXS 0518+211 show a slightly longer ( 4 years) period than that reported in P20 and P22 ( 3.1 0.4 years). We note that the MWL LCs of this source have a shorter coverage w..t. the Fermi LAT curve. Nevertheless, due to the large uncertainties derived by the periodicity analysis for the different inferred values of the period, the two estimates are still compatible.
These authors report time lags for the radio- ray correlation of 160-240 days for TXS 0059+581, S4 0814+42, TXS 0518+211, and TXS 1902+556, with local significance < 3 .
The blazars S4 0814+42, TXS 0518+211 also have compatible periods with Table tab:periodicity_results .

sources.summary: The paper applies BDT processing to VER J0521+211 because standard detection was weak, assumes a 10 TeV cutoff for cascade modeling, and derives upper limits.

quotes.quote: The hadron separation was achieved using selection on mean-scaled Hillas parameters for all sources other than H 1426+428, 1ES 0229+200, and VER J0521+211. For these three sources, which were not detected to a high significance with standard hadron separation, a boosted decision tree (BDT) analysis was used.
For VER J0521+211, 1ES 0229+200, and PG 1553+113, E_ cut is taken to be 10 TeV and the fraction of the total emission due to cascade emission is within VERITAS sensitivity (cascade fraction > 5 ).
Upper limits were derived for PG 1553+113 and VER J0521+211 as well, however 1ES 0229+200 allows the strongest constraints on f_ compared to the prediction.

sources.summary: The paper describes VER 0521+211 as a VHE BL Lac source in its monitoring sample and reports its optical/radio flux level, variability trends, and correlation behavior.

quotes.quote: VER 0521+211 : The source is rather bright both in the optical and radio bands, and shows common increasing trend, as well as significant correlation with optical leading by 110 days. There are several 3 points in the correlation plot and actually these points form rather flat plateaus than single peaks, indicating that the correlation is probably due to common trend rather than correlated flares (see Fig. Fig:lc8 ). Subtracting the polynomial fit of the radio light curve from optical light curve shows that 50 of the optical flux originates from the common radio-optical component (which should be considered as lower limit, see main text).

sources.summary: The paper does not confirm the proposed VER J0521+211 redshift, treating the source as still unknown and setting a new spectroscopic lower limit.

quotes.quote: VER J0521+211 : On the basis on a weak emission line at 5940 attributed to [ N II ] 6583 , shaw2013a proposed this source to be at z = 0.108. This feature was not confirmed by archambault2013 that reports a featureless spectrum. We do not confirm the redshift of the source, which therefore is still unknown, setting a lower limit of z > 0.18.

sources.summary: The paper finds RX J0521.7+2112 below the 0.01 p-value level, with a 1136-day period inconsistent with literature and an AR(2)-dominated light curve without a linear trend.

quotes.quote: Two sources, PKS 0301-243 and RX J0521.7+2112, are below a p_ value of 0.01, with a period of 821 days ( 2.3 years) and 1136 days ( 3.1 years), respectively. Both values are higher and not compatible with those on literature, where periods of 2 and 2.8 years are found in the high significance level ( >3 ), respectively. RX J0521.7+2112 does not show a linear trend and it is dominated by an auto-regressive component of second order with _1 = 0.38 and _2 = 0.24 . PKS 0301-243 shows a special sinusoidal behaviour, a principal periodic component of 821 days ( 2.3 years) with a second harmonic.

gmw_00001547.name: RBS 0413
gmw_00001547.overview: RBS 0413 is a high-frequency peaked BL Lacertae object located at a redshift of z = 0.190 [arXiv:1110.0040, arXiv:1210.3567]. The active galaxy features a radio jet with visible apparent motion [arXiv:1801.00817] and exhibits a decreasing optical flux trend alongside an increasing radio flux trend [arXiv:1606.06431].

sources.summary: The review describes RBS 0413 as a VERITAS HBL whose SED disfavors a pure SSC model; EC and hadronic fits reproduce it with near-equipartition parameters.

quotes.quote: The HBL RBS 0413 ( z = 0.19 ) was detected by VERITAS in data accumulated between 2008 September and 2010 January. Contemporaneous multiwavelength coverage was provided by Fermi LAT, Swift XRT and UVOT, and MDM (R-band). A leptonic SSC model fit (solid curve in the right panel of Fig. noSSCHBLs ) produces a GeV ray spectrum significantly harder than indicated by Fermi LAT, and requires a magnetic field energy density a factor 6 10^ 2 below equipartition. A much more satisfactory representation of the entire SED (including the Fermi LAT spectrum), can be achieved by including an EC component with a thermal blackbody radiation field at T = 1.5 10^3 K.

The dashed curve in the right panel of Fig. noSSCHBLs shows a hadronic fit to the SED of RBS 0413, which also provides a better representation than the pure SSC model. For this fit, protons need to be accelerated to E_ p, max = 1.6 10^ 19 eV with a kinetic power of L_p = 2 10^ 46 erg/s. The magnetic field of B = 30 G is in almost exact equipartition with the energy content in relativistic protons.

sources.summary: The paper describes RBS 0413 as an HBL targeted by VERITAS because of X-ray and Fermi properties, then reports a significant VHE excess with steady low Crab-level flux and a measured photon index.

quotes.quote: RBS 0413 is an HBL with a redshift of z = 0.190 . This blazar was initially selected for observation with VERITAS because it is one of the X-ray-brightest HBLs in the Sedentary Survey , and met the selection criteria used to indentify likely VHE emitters in . Deeper observations were later motivated by the identification of this blazar as a bright, very hard-spectrum ( 1.5 ; ) MeV-GeV source by the Fermi-LAT collaboration. RBS 0413 was observed by VERITAS for 26 h of quality-selected live time between September 2008 and January 2010. An excess of 108 VHE rays (VER J0319+187), corresponding to a statistical significance of 5.5 , is observed from the direction of RBS 0413. The observed VHE flux has an average value of 2 Crab above 200 GeV and does not vary within the limited statistics. The time-averaged VERITAS photon index is = 3.2 0.7 .

sources.summary: The paper describes RBS 0413 as a bright HBL detected by VERITAS with a soft, low-flux VHE spectrum; its SED favors external-Compton or lepto-hadronic modeling over pure SSC.

quotes.quote: RBS 0413 is a bright HBL, at z = 0.190 , that was initially targeted based on its inclusion in the Sedentary Survey . It was observed for 26 h of quality-selected live time between 2008 and 2010 . The data yielded the detection of a soft-spectrum ( = 3.2 0.7 ) source with 1 Crab Nebula flux. Its SED, shown in Figure blazar_plots , is again not well described by a SSC model. The modeling favors the inclusion of an additional external-Compton (EC) component, and can also be fit by a lepto-hadronic scenario.

sources.summary: The paper describes HB89 0317+185 as a VHE BL Lac source in its monitoring sample and reports its optical/radio flux level, variability trends, and correlation behavior.

quotes.quote: HB89 0317+185 : One of the weakest sources of the sample in the optical, while the radio flux density is above the median of the sample. The optical light curve shows significant decreasing trend, while the radio light curve shows significant increasing trend. Also DCF finds no significant correlation between the two bands, and therefore it is not surprising that our polynomial subtraction method does not decrease the rms much. We note that the optical light curve is poorly sampled compared to most other sources in our sample (see Fig. Fig:lc5 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports a clearly visible apparent jet motion in 0317+185, with component 1 observed at four epochs, a measured proper motion, and a Good quality code.

quotes.quote: 0317+185 Component 1 is observed at four epochs, with a proper motion of 0.493 0.100 mas yr ^ 1 . It is a well-defined feature whose motion is clearly evident, as is shown in the two-epoch image comparison in Figure 7. It receives a quality code of `Good'.

gmw_00001548.name: RX J0648.7+1516
gmw_00001548.overview: RX J0648.7+1516 is a BL Lacertae blazar located at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 0.179 [arXiv:1111.1209, arXiv:1110.0040]. The active galactic nucleus has been detected as a very-high-energy gamma-ray source [arXiv:1111.1209, arXiv:1205.0539] and exhibits an increasing trend in both its optical and radio flux [arXiv:1606.06431].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RX J0648.7+1516 as a low-latitude BL Lac blazar, reporting VERITAS VHE discovery, prior AGN classification, a z=0.179 optical redshift, and point-like gamma-ray emission compatible with its radio position.

quotes.quote: We report on the discovery of two gamma-ray sources at E>200 GeV with VERITAS: RX J0648.7+1516 and VER J0521+211. Both sources are located at low galactic latitudes ( |b| < 10^ ), and were previously classified as active galactic nuclei of unknown type. The discovery and characterization of gamma-ray emission together with follow-up optical spectroscopy permitted the identification of RX J0648.7+1516 and VER J0521+211 as BL Lac-type blazars. In the case of RX J0648.7+1516, a spectroscopic redshift of z=0.179 was derived.

RX J0648.7+1516 was observed with VERITAS between 4 March and 15 April 2010 (MJD 55259-55301), for a total exposure of 19.3 hours after rejecting data taking under poor weather conditions or with hardware problems. After event parameterization and background rejection, an excess of 283 gamma-ray-like events from the direction of RX J0648.7+1516 was found, corresponding to a significance of 5.2 . Figure fig:0648-gamma shows the differential energy spectrum of the obtained signal, which can be parametrized by a power law of the form d N/ d E = N_0 (E/0.3 TeV )^ with N_0=(2.3 0.5) 10^ 11 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and = 4.4 0.8 . The excess event distribution of the signal is consistent with a point-like source and compatible within errors with the radio position of RX J0648.7+1516.

sources.summary: The review describes RX J0648.7+1516 as a VERITAS HBL whose SED is poorly fit by pure SSC but better represented by EC or hadronic models with reasonable jet power.

quotes.quote: The HBL RX J0648.7+1516 ( z = 0.179 ) was detected as a VHE ray source by VERITAS in 2010 March -- April aliu11 . Simultaneous multiwavelength coverage was provided by Fermi LAT, Swift XRT and UVOT and the MDM Observatory (optical B, V, R bands). The resulting SED is shown in the left panel of Figure noSSCHBLs . An attempt to fit this SED with a pure SSC model clearly overshoots both the simultaneous Fermi upper limits and the 2-year average spectrum. This is a consequence of the apparent sharp spectral break between the Fermi and VERITAS energy ranges, which is not consistent with the smooth curvature of an SSC spectrum (resulting from the broad ranges of scattering electron energies and synchrotron seed photon frequencies, in combination with the gradual onset of Klein-Nishina effects).

Alternatively, the hadronic model described above also provides a satisfactory fit to the SED (the dashed curve in the left panel of Fig. noSSCHBLs ). The model requires protons to be accelerated up to E_ p, max = 1.5 10^ 19 eV, with a total kinetic luminosity in relativistic protons of L_p = 2 10^ 45 erg/s. While hadronic models often suffer from extreme power requirements, this appears to be a very reasonable jet power.

sources.summary: The paper reports RX J0648.7+1516 as a Fermi-motivated unidentified radio/X-ray emitter later detected by VERITAS, with soft steady VHE flux and BL Lac-like optical spectroscopy at redshift 0.179.

quotes.quote: RX J0648.7+1516 observations were motivated by the identification of a nearby cluster of > 10 GeV photons by the Fermi-LAT collaboration. This unidentified radio and X-ray emitter was observed by VERITAS for 19 h of quality-selected live time between March 4 and April 15, 2010. An excess of VHE rays (VER J0648+152), corresponding to a statistical significance of 5.3 , is observed from the direction of RX J0648.7+1516. No strong variations are observed in the VHE flux ( 2 Crab above 300 GeV), and the time-averaged VERITAS spectrum is very soft ( = 4.4 0.8 ). Follow-up optical spectroscopy was performed with the Shane 3-m telescope at Lick Observatory. A continuum dominated spectrum typical of BL Lac objects is observed, along with weak absorption lines compatible with z = 0.179 .

sources.summary: The paper presents RX J0648.7+1516 as a VERITAS VHE discovery motivated by Fermi-LAT photons, with optical spectroscopy identifying a BL Lac-like continuum and an HBL SED poorly fit by SSC modeling.

quotes.quote: The successful VHE discovery observations of RX J0648.7+1516 were motivated by the identification of a cluster of E >10 GeV photons in the Fermi-LAT data. A total of 19 h of quality-selected live time was taken in March-April 2010. Significant VHE emission ( = 4.4 0.8 , 3 Crab Nebula flux) was detected at the position of RX J0648.7+1516, an unidentified radio, optical and X-ray emitter. Follow-up optical spectroscopy of this object yielded a continuum-dominated spectrum typical of BL Lac objects and weak absorption lines compatible with z = 0.179. The SED of this blazar indicates it is an HBL, but it is not well fit by an SSC model.

sources.summary: The paper describes VER 0648+152 as a VHE BL Lac source in its monitoring sample and reports its optical/radio flux level, variability trends, and correlation behavior.

quotes.quote: VER 0648+152 : Weak optical and radio source (see Fig. Fig:lc9 ), and for the radio the modulation index calculation fails due to large uncertainties and low measured flux density. However, we find significant increasing trend in both bands. This is particularly puzzling as the simulations indicate that no significant trends are expected in radio. Visual inspection supports the result of the trend analysis as does the polynomial fitting to the radio data.

sources.summary: The paper reports a fast jet component in 0645+153, observed at four epochs, over-resolved in full images but distinct in tapered images, and assigned a Good quality code.

quotes.quote: 0645+153 Component 1 is observed at four epochs, with a proper motion of 0.511 0.145 mas yr ^ 1 . It is fairly distant from the core, and is over-resolved in the images in Figure 1. However, it is seen as a distinct feature in tapered images (see the following source for an example of this). It has a quality code of `Good'.

gmw_00001549.name: 1ES 1440+122
gmw_00001549.overview: 1ES 1440+122 is an active galactic nucleus classified as an intermediate-frequency-peaked BL Lacertae object with a host galaxy [arXiv:1109.6000, arXiv:2012.00008]. It is surrounded by 20 companion galaxies [arXiv:1109.6000, arXiv:1410.8810] and exhibits steady, non-variable very-high-energy emission [arXiv:1109.6000, arXiv:1110.0040].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1ES1440+122 as an AGN/IBL BL Lac at z=0.162, notes its hard Fermi spectrum, and reports a VERITAS detection without VHE variability.

quotes.quote: 1ES1440+122 was initially identified as an AGN in the Einstein Slew Survey . It is surrounded by 20 galaxies, which suggests that it may belong to a cluster of galaxies . 1ES1440+122 is classified as an intermediate-frequency-peaked BL Lac object by and its redshift, z = 0.162, is based on the identification of at least three spectral lines . 1ES1440+122 belongs to the the first Fermi LAT MeV-GeV catalog , having an average photon index of 1.77 0.25. It is one of the hardest spectrum sources (top 10 ) in the catalog.

The blazar was observed with the VERITAS array for 50 hours between June 2008 and July 2010. Analysis of these data has resulted in the detection of the object with a statistical significance of 5.5 . The observed flux is less than 1 of the flux from the Crab Nebula above 200 GeV and the observed VHE light curve does not show any evidence for variability .

sources.summary: The paper chooses 1ES 1440+122 as a blazar case study because nearby companions may absorb VHE rays, but finds individual-galaxy absorption unlikely unless the companion is unrealistically compact.

quotes.quote: Therefore, we have chosen 1ES 1440+122 for a case study, as a primary candidate for possible absorption because of the nearby companions. The source \ is a BL Lac type object detected in all energy bands across the electro-magnetic spectrum, from radio to VHE rays. It is located at a redshift z = 0.162 1993ApJ...412..541S and appears to be located in a rich environment, being surrounded by 20 galaxies 1999A&A...341..683H .

In Figure tau_E_1440 , the optical depth of the absorption caused by the radiation field of the companion galaxy of \ is shown as a function of \ energy for different values of the effective radius of the galaxy. Substantial absorption is possible only in the case of a relatively small effective radius of the galaxy, R_ eff 1 kpc. However, the typical size of such luminous galaxies is typically much bigger, of the order of R_ eff 10 kpc. Therefore, significant absorption by the radiation field of this individual galaxy is very unlikely.

sources.summary: VERITAS observed 1ES 1440+122 as a hard-spectrum Fermi IBL/borderline HBL source, detected a significant VHE excess, and measured steady faint emission with a soft photon index.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1440+122 is an IBL (borderline HBL), with a well-determined redshift of z = 0.162 . This hard-spectrum Fermi source ( 1.8 ; ) was selected for observation with VERITAS because it was recommended as a likely VHE emitter by . The blazar was observed by VERITAS for 47 h of quality-selected live time between May 2008 and June 2010. An excess of VHE rays (VER J1442+120), corresponding to a statistical significance of 5.5 , is observed from the direction of 1ES 1440+122. The observed VHE flux does not vary within limited statistics and has an average value of 1 Crab above 200 GeV. The time-averaged VERITAS photon index is = 3.4 0.7 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1442+1200 with 1ES 1440+122, notes its prominent host and nearby Gaia source, and uses a fixed aperture to limit polarization contamination.

quotes.quote: J1442+1200: 1ES 1440+122 has a prominent host galaxy. There is also a nearby source located 2.5 arcsec from the core according to Gaia data GaiaDR2 . It could potentially affect the polarization. We measured the nucleus with a fixed 4.5 arcsec aperture.

gmw_00001550.name: OT 081 (1749+096)
gmw_00001550.overview: OT 081 (1749+096) is an ultra-luminous, low-frequency-peaked BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1204.4150, arXiv:1708.01083] located at a redshift of z = 0.322 [arXiv:2010.12393]. It exhibits strong multiwavelength variability [arXiv:2010.12393] and hosts a curved, parsec-scale radio jet [arXiv:1204.4150] with a limb-brightened inner structure [arXiv:2010.12393].

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 1749+096 as a BL Lac with a one-sided curved parsec-scale jet, measuring superluminal component speeds and linking component ejections to radio outbursts.

quotes.quote: PKS 1749+096 is a BL Lac object showing weak extended jet emission to the northeast of the compact VLBI core on parsec scales. We aim at better understanding the jet kinematics and variability of this source and finding clues that may applicable to other BL Lac objects. The jet was studied with multi-epoch multi-frequency high-resolution VLBI observations. The jet is characterized by a one-sided curved morphology at all epochs and all frequencies.

Superluminal motions in the range of 5--21 were measured in 11 distinct components. We estimated the physical jet parameters with the minimum Lorentz factor of 10.2 and Doppler factors in the range of 10.2--20.4 (component C5). The coincidence in time of the component's ejection and flares supports the idea that, at least in PKS 1749+096, ejection of new jet components is connected with major outbursts in flux density.
PKS 1749 + 096 (also known as OT 081 and 4C +09.57) is an ultra-luminous BL Lac object with an optical polarization of up to 32 1999ApJS..121..131F .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the BL Lac object 1749+096 during a 2016 active state, linking gamma-ray outbursts, multiwavelength flux maxima, spectral hardening, and VLBI-core disturbances.

quotes.quote: Recent Fermi Large Area Telescope (LAT) light curves indicate an active ray state spanning about five months from 2016 June to 2016 October in the BL Lac object 1749+096 (OT 081). During this period, we find two notable ray events: an exceptionally strong outburst followed by a significant enhancement (local peak). In this study, we analyze multi-waveband light curves (radio, optical, X-ray, and ray) plus very-long baseline interferometry (VLBI) data to investigate the nature of the ray events.

We find a spectral hardening of the ray photon index during the ray outburst. The photon index shows a transition from a softer-when-brighter to a harder-when-brighter trend at around 1.8 10^ 7 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . We see indication that both the ray outburst and the subsequent enhancement precede the propagation of a polarized knot in a region near the VLBI core. The highest polarized intensity, 230 mJy, and an electric vector position angle rotation, by 32 ^ , are detected about 12 days after the ray outburst. We conclude that both ray events are caused by the propagation of a disturbance in the mm-wave core.

sources.summary: J1751 is described as an unusual BL Lac with high jet power, bulk Lorentz factor, and transition radius, whose inverse-Compton emission is best fitted by low-redshift CMB scattering.

quotes.quote: We find that the blazar J1751 has a larger power, bulk Lorentz factor and transition region radius than the other BL Lac blazars. The physical parameters of the fit are more similar to the Compton-dominant blazars found in Paper III. We find that in order to reproduce the low frequency synchrotron peak the fit requires a large transition region (and a large inferred black hole mass if the transition region occurs at 10^ 5 R_ s as in M87). We are unable to fit the inverse-Compton emission with SSC emission due to the low magnetic field strength required to produce the low peak synchrotron frequency.

This large transition region is located at a distance 18 pc from the black hole in our fit, placing it outside the BLR and dusty torus. Somewhat surprisingly, we find that the inverse-Compton emission of J1751 is well fitted by scattering of CMB photons even at the comparatively low redshift z=0.322 , if the jet has a large bulk Lorentz factor (see Figure fig:2 ). These characteristics indicate that the blazar has similar physical properties to the Compton-dominant blazars from Paper III but is located at low redshift where the CMB has a lower energy density.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS1749+096 as a low-frequency-peaked BL Lac object detected by H.E.S.S. during a July 2016 flaring episode after Fermi-LAT activity.

quotes.quote: PKS1749+096, also known as OT081, is a bright radio-source, classified as low frequency-peaked BL Lacertae object, that shows weak extended jet emission to the northeast of the compact VLBI core on parsec scales . Redshift measurements from give a value of z = 0.32. It shows radio emission typical of blazars: a core-jet morphology and superluminal motion of the jet components detected being in the range of 5-21c .

As reported in ATel 9267, MAGIC detected VHE emission from PKS 1749+096 during the night of July 24th. Here we report H.E.S.S. observations of the same period, showing a clear detection of the source above the 5 level for the full observation period. The Li Ma significance map derived from the observations is shown in the left plot of Fig.  fig:PKS1749MapSED . We can also confirm the detection of a flaring flux increase during the night of 24th of July, where we reach a detection above 6 in only 2.3 h of observations.

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 1749+096 as a BL Lac object with strong variability, a limb-brightened inner jet, and measured opening angles from stacked VLBA imaging.

quotes.quote: galaxies: quasars: individual: PKS 1749+096 --- galaxies: jets --- radio continuum: galaxies L. Cui et al. Resolving the inner jet of High resolution observations of jets in Active Galactic Nuclei (AGNs) with Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) show that some objects have regular or irregular swings in the position angle of the innermost structure i.e., jet wobbling, Agudo+etal+2007, Lu+etal+2012 . The physical origin of this phenomena is currently still not well understood. Possible mechanisms for the jet wobbling include accretion disk precession, orbital motion of accretion systems (binary black hole), and jet instabilities e.g., Agudo+2009 . In some sources, a correlation between the variations of the inner jet position angle and of the flux density in radio, X-ray, or gamma-ray is observed e.g., and references therein Rani+etal+2014 , suggesting a connection between the inner jet morphology and the corresponding emitting regions. At a redshift of 0.322 Stickel+etal+1988 , the ultra-luminous BL Lac object showed strong variability from radio to X-ray regime. In 2016 July, unprecedented bright flaring activity in very high energy gamma-ray emission, together with X-ray and optical flares were detected Becerra Gonzalez+etal+2016, Ciprini+etal+2016, Mirzoyan+2016, Balonek+etal+2016 .

Interestingly, the transverse jet structure of the stacked BSMEM image of is transversely resolved and shows a limb-brightened morphology (Figure fig:stacked , middle). The limb brightening is most pronounced in the inner part of the jet ( < 0.4 mas), while the jet farther out is resolved out and invisible. By determining the transverse jet width, we can measure the apparent opening angle of the underlying jet with the caveat that such a stacked opening angle is different from a single epoch jet opening angle. We found that the apparent jet opening angle is 50 0 8 0 at the core distance of 0.2 mas (0.9 pc) and 42 0 6 0 at the core distance of 0.3 mas (1.4 pc) (Figure fig:stacked , right), suggesting that the jet still undergoes collimation on these scales.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1749+096 as compact at all observed frequencies, with unusual inverse depolarization, RM values of 10^3-10^4 rad/m^2, and stable RM behavior over three months.

quotes.quote: 1749+096 This source displays a rather compact jet geometry at all frequencies and in both epochs (2016 December and 2017 March). Interestingly, the degree of linear polarization is larger at lower frequencies, which is not usually seen in other sources (Section fracpol ). This might suggest that we are looking at a mixture of different polarization components with different EVPAs and/or RMs or that internal Faraday rotation occurs in this source (Section sectscreen ).

The values of RM range from 10^3 to 10^4 \ rad/m^2 in both epochs data. The values of a are consistent within errors. Therefore, the sign, absolute value, and frequency dependence of RM appear to be stable over 3 months.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1749+096 as a BLO with extensive Metsahovi and VLBI monitoring, finding a complex, increasing 2.7-year timescale plus short flare timescales.

quotes.quote: PKS 1749+096 : This BLO type object has been monitored in Mets ahovi for 20 years at 22 GHz and 25 years at 37 GHz. At 90 GHz the data sets are 14 years long. The source has been a target of many Very Long Baseline observations e.g. homan01, wiik01, lister05, piner07 . We found a timescale of 2.7 years in all the frequency bands but the structure of the wavelet plot is very complex and the timescale is increasing in time from 1.7 to 2.7 years. At 90 GHz it also repeated only 3.4 times while at 22 GHz 4.4 and at 37 GHz 5 times.

sources.summary: The paper says 1749+096 has weak jet emission that prevents core-shift measurements; model-core alignment gives a flat core region and a spectrum flattening at high frequencies.

quotes.quote: For these two sources, we were unable to obtain core-shift measurements due to the weak nature of the jet emission. Hence, in this case we cannot be sure if the core corresponds to a stationary feature or the =1 surface. To produce the spectral index maps (Figs. 1156spix , 1749spix ), we aligned the images by their model-fitted core positions. This produced uniform flat spectra in the core regions (e.g., the slice along the 4.6--7.9 GHz spectral index map for 1156+295, Fig. 1156spix a).
The core spectrum of 1749+096 (Fig. 1749spix ) displays similar properties, with _ 7.9 GHz ^ 4.6 GHz 1.0 and becomes flat at the highest frequencies with _ 43 GHz ^ 22 GHz 0.01 .

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 1749 + 096 as a BL Lac with a radio jet, radio flaring, optical and gamma-ray variability, polarization variability, and the campaign’s strongest internight amplitude.

quotes.quote: PKS 1749 + 096: is a BL-Lac object with a redshift z=0.32 according to Stickel88 and White88 . Its radio jet was originally detected by Lobanov00 . This source presents a history of radio flaring, possibly with a long-term period Hovatta08,Nieppola09 . Flux variability was also detected at optical Stickel93 and ray frequencies Abdo10A . The optical and infrared polarizations also show variability Brindle86 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses OT 081 as a very active BL Lac object with radio outbursts, ordered EVPA swings, and gamma-ray flares during the Fermi monitoring period.

quotes.quote: Figure fig2 shows the radio band data during the period 2008.0 through 2009.5 for the very active BL Lac object, OT 081 (1749+096), one of the three sources originally modeled using the transverse shock formulation hug91 . Several resolved events are apparent in both the total flux density and fractional linear polarization light curves; an ordered swing in EVPA at 14.5 GHz occurred from August to December 2008 followed by a shorter-duration ordered swing during January-February 2009. The Fermi light curve for this source is included in the variability study based on data from the first year of Fermi operation abd10b . A very large flare in the ray band was in progress in August 2008, and a second flare which peaked in mid-March 2009 is also apparent in these data.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes 1749+096 and finds a 5.5 sigma monotonic core RM gradient that is also visible in earlier 8.1-15.2 GHz imaging.

quotes.quote: A visual inspection of the published RM images indicated the possible presence of transverse RM gradients across the jets of 0355+508 and 0748+126, as well as 1749+096, whose transverse RM gradient was previously reported by Gabuzda et al. (2008).

1749+096. An RM map made from the same visibility data was published by Gabuzda et al. (2008). Our analysis shows that the previously reported monotonic, transverse RM gradient across the core has a significance is 5.5 . This gradient is also visible in the 8.1--15.2 GHz RM image of Zavala Taylor (2004).

sources.summary: The paper treats J1751+0939 as the epoch-2 ALMA grid calibrator and rules out an incorrect quasar spectral index as the source of the observed flux-calibration behavior.

quotes.quote: Checking for variability in the pipeline spectral index near the date of epoch 2, we find that the original and updated spectral index of J1751+0939 are 0.590 0.037 and 0.482 0.014 , both of which are reasonably consistent with historical measurements (see Section sec:spix_epoch2 of the Appendix). If we correct the epoch 2 updated spectral index of J1751+0939 using the value of the normalized MCFs, the true spectral index would be 0 . This value is extremely inconsistent with monitoring of J1751+0939 , and moreover, such an index is unlikely for a quasar, as the quasar brightness at mm wavelengths is dominated by synchrotron emission vankempen2014 vankempen2014,planck2011 . A large systematic error introduced by an incorrect quasar spectral index is therefore ruled out for epoch 2.

gmw_00001551.name: 87GB 025547.6+132220
gmw_00001551.overview: The elliptical galaxy 87GB 025547.6+132220 is associated with a bright active radio core [arXiv:2209.13930]. This core connects to a long radio tail and remnant plasma that extends toward the A399-401 radio bridge [arXiv:2209.13930].

sources.summary: The paper links 87GB 025547.6+132220 to an active radio core with a connected long tail and remnant plasma extending toward the A399-401 radio bridge.

quotes.quote: Region B in Figure fig:subimages has a bright active radio core (B1) corresponding to the elliptical galaxy 87GB 025547.6+132220 at z=0.084 huchra2012 . Whereas there was a gap between the long tail (B2) and the core (B1) in the images from , we detect it as one connected structure. The radio emission from B2 has an LLS of 320 kpc. Connected at the end of this emission, we detect remnant plasma stretched to the west over a similar LLS of 270 kpc (B3). We observe signs of plasma extending southward into the radio bridge filament at the edges of B2 and B3.

gmw_00001552.name: 2MASX J02591535+1314347
gmw_00001552.overview: 2MASX J02591535+1314347 is an elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.078 [arXiv:2209.13930]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus associated with a bright radio core, jet-like components, and a possible remnant tail [arXiv:2209.13930].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J02591535+1314347 as an elliptical galaxy with a radio core, jet-like components, and a possible remnant tail connected to the bridge.

quotes.quote: In region D in Figure fig:subimages , we detect two sources next to each other: 2MASX J02591878+1315467, located northeast at z=0.073 , and southwest from this source, we find 2MASX J02591535+1314347 at z=0.078 hill1993 . The first is an elliptical galaxy associated with a tail with an LLS of 220 kpc (D1), and the second is an elliptical galaxy associated with the bright radio core to the southwest (D2) with an LLS of 90 kpc. From the core, a fainter component extends southwest (D3) with an LLS of 80 kpc and a long bent structure pointed to the south (D4). Following the same direction to the southwest, the diffuse emission again becomes brighter (D5), and in the lower-resolution images in Figure fig:lowres , this area seems to be connected to the bridge.

Figure fig:subimages shows that the emission from D5 is stretched in the same direction as the jet from D3, which is associated with an AGN in 2MASX J02591535+1314347. Therefore, we propose that the emission from D5 is a remnant tail from the AGN, which is reenergized by the equatorial shock or adiabatic compression.

gmw_00001553.name: NGC 024
gmw_00001553.overview: NGC 24 is an edge-on, low-surface-brightness spiral galaxy located in the background of the Sculptor group [arXiv:2008.08954, arXiv:astro-ph/0609148]. It features regular kinematics [arXiv:astro-ph/0609148], a large ultraviolet halo relative to its star formation rate [arXiv:1401.4170], and two nearby dwarf candidates that may be satellite galaxies [arXiv:2008.08954].

sources.summary: The survey includes NGC 24 as an edge-on Local Volume spiral and identifies two nearby dwarf candidates as possible satellites, with satellite numbers low relative to simulations.

quotes.quote: We have surveyed a 72 deg ^2 area of the nearby Sculptor group using the Dark Energy Camera -- also encompassing the two low-mass Local Volume galaxies NGC 24 and NGC 45 residing behind the group -- to search for hitherto undetected dwarf galaxies. Apart from the previously known dwarf galaxies we have found only two new candidates down to a 3 surface brightness detection limit of 27.4 mag arcsec ^ 2 . Both systems are in projection close to NGC 24. However, one of these candidates could be an ultra-diffuse galaxy associated to a background galaxy. We compared the number of known dwarf galaxy candidates around NGC 24, NGC 45, and five other well-studied low-mass giant galaxies (NGC 1156, NGC 2403, NGC 5023, M 33, and the LMC) with predictions from cosmological simulations and found that for the stellar-to-halo mass models considered, the observed satellite numbers tend to be on the lower end of the expected range.

Both dwarf galaxy candidates dw0009-25 and dw0010-25 are in projection close to the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 24 and also close to each other (see Fig. field ). Their on-sky separation is only 0.11 deg and 0.40 deg from NGC 24, which at its distance ( D=7.3 Mpc) corresponds to 14.5 kpc and 52.5 kpc, respectively. Assuming that the two dwarfs are satellites of NGC 24, the effective radius and absolute magnitude would be r_ eff =283 pc and M_r=-8.7 mag for dw0009-25 and r_ eff =1069 pc and M_r=-11.9 mag for dw0010-25.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 24 as a background Sculptor-field spiral with regular kinematics, a transition low-surface-brightness profile, and a high stellar mass-to-light ratio.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Optical parameters of NGC 24 R.A. (2000) ^ a & 00^ h 09^ m 56.6^ s DEC. (2000) ^ a & 24^ 57'43.0''

quotes.quote: These two galaxies of similar magnitude M_B 17.4 lie in the background of the Sculptor group and are low surface brightness galaxies, especially NGC 45. The distribution and kinematics are regular for NGC 24 while NGC 45 exhibits a kinematical twist of its major axis. A tilted-ring model shows that the position angle of the major axis changes by 25 . A best-fit model of their mass distribution gives mass-to-light ratios for the stellar disk of 2.5 and 5.2 for NGC 24 and NGC 45 respectively.

In this area, besides the five main members studied so far (NGC 55, 247, 253, 300 and 7793), two more galaxies, NGC 24 and NGC 45 (Figure dssimages ), fall in the same region and have similar sizes and degree of resolution as well as 20 known dwarf galaxies (C ot e, Freeman Carignan 1997; C ot e, Carignan Freeman 2000). However, when looking at the estimated distances, a mean distance of 2.4 0.6 Mpc is found for the main members (de Vaucouleurs 1978) but 6.8 Mpc (7 ) and 5.9 Mpc (6 ) for NGC 24 and NGC 45, respectively (based on a Hubble constant of 75 Mpc ^ 1 , Tully 1988). Moreover, when doing dynamical mass estimates of the group, any combination of the main members gives consistently 3 10^ 12 M _ for the group, while including NGC 24 and NGC 45 makes the estimate jump to 3 10^ 13 M _ , clearly too large for a loose group like Sculptor. All this suggests strongly that NGC 24 and NGC 45 are not members of the group but background objects.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 24 does not need a thick dust disk; its less edge-on geometry weakens the signal, and an extended PSF better reproduces its profile.

quotes.quote: However, it is found that the rest three targets (IC 5249, NGC 24, and NGC 4173) do not necessarily need a thick dust disk to model the ultraviolet (UV) halo, because its contribution is too small and the UV halo may be caused by the wing part of the point spread function.

However, we found that the existence of thick dust disk is questionable for the rest three targets---IC 5249, NGC 24, and NGC 4173. As Figure fig-diff shows, for these targets, the magenta-solid line is hardly distinguishable from the black-solid line, the profile from the best-fit model including both thin and thick dust disks (see eq. ( eq-kappa )) and being convoluted with the standard PSF. These targets have lower _B^ thick ( h_d^ thick )^2 than other targets (see Table tbl-fit ), which indicates having lower amount of the extraplanar dust (see section ana-res-sfr ) and subsequently makes the two profiles indistinguishable. In the case of NGC 24, its less edge-on appearance makes the difference even weaker.

The data profiles of IC 5249 and NGC 24 seen in Figure fig-diff show hints of extended, decreasing wing-like features, but with intensities comparable to or lower than the background upper bound. The feature for IC 5249 can be explained by the standard PSF wing solely. However, for NGC 24, the extended PSF seems to reproduce the profile better.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 24 as a small spiral with a relatively large UV halo and high-quality data whose profile resembles deeper spiral-galaxy halo observations.

quotes.quote: IC 5249, NGC 24, NGC 6503, and NGC 4173 These galaxies are significantly smaller than the other spiral galaxies in our sample, and were included because they met our cutoff criteria and had good data. NGC 24 and NGC 6503 have large UV halos for their specific SFR relative to the other galaxies in the sample (on absolute scales, they are smaller than the halos of the more massive galaxies). NGC 24 is remarkable in that it has very high quality data. Its flux height profile and SED (Figure figure.results_sc ) look very similar to other spiral galaxies with very deep data, suggesting that all star-forming late-type galaxies have similar UV halos.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 024 as a high-baryonic-acceleration SPARC comparison galaxy whose observed inner rotation speed is difficult for NIHAO simulations but closer to EAGLE/APOSTLE diversity-plot behavior.

quotes.quote: Here, the gravitational acceleration generated by baryons at 2 kpc is much smaller in F568--3, UGC 5750 and IC 2574 than in NGC 024, UGC 5721 and UGC 7690, and so is the measured circular velocity, and hence the total gravitational acceleration at 2 kpc.

But the three high-baryonic acceleration galaxies of Fig. RC_selected_galaxies , NGC 024, UGC 5721 and UGC 7690 seem to all have too high observed V_ rot (2 kpc ) to be reproduced by the NIHAO simulations.

Interestingly, as originally shown by Oman2015 , galaxies such as NGC 024, UGC 5721 and UGC 7690 can in principle be more closely reproduced in the diversity plot by simulations from the EAGLE and APOSTLE projects, which in turn cannot reproduce the smaller V_ rot (2 kpc ) galaxies.

gmw_00001554.name: UGC 7690
gmw_00001554.overview: UGC 7690 is a galaxy characterized by a high baryonic acceleration in its inner regions [arXiv:1811.06554]. It appears to exhibit a declining rotation curve, though a modest kinematic warp makes the exact shape of this curve uncertain [arXiv:0901.4222].

sources.summary: The paper says 7690 appears to have a declining rotation curve, but a modest kinematic warp makes the inclination and rotation-curve shape uncertain.

quotes.quote: 7690 appears to have a declining rotation curve. However, this galaxy also has a modest kinematic warp, with an inclination changing from about 40^ in the central parts to 30^ in the outer parts. Because the inclination as a function of radius is uncertain, so is the shape of the rotation curve.

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 7690 as a high-baryonic-acceleration SPARC comparison galaxy whose observed inner rotation speed is difficult for NIHAO simulations but closer to EAGLE/APOSTLE diversity-plot behavior.

quotes.quote: Here, the gravitational acceleration generated by baryons at 2 kpc is much smaller in F568--3, UGC 5750 and IC 2574 than in NGC 024, UGC 5721 and UGC 7690, and so is the measured circular velocity, and hence the total gravitational acceleration at 2 kpc.

But the three high-baryonic acceleration galaxies of Fig. RC_selected_galaxies , NGC 024, UGC 5721 and UGC 7690 seem to all have too high observed V_ rot (2 kpc ) to be reproduced by the NIHAO simulations.

Interestingly, as originally shown by Oman2015 , galaxies such as NGC 024, UGC 5721 and UGC 7690 can in principle be more closely reproduced in the diversity plot by simulations from the EAGLE and APOSTLE projects, which in turn cannot reproduce the smaller V_ rot (2 kpc ) galaxies.

gmw_00001555.name: NGC 1530
gmw_00001555.overview: NGC 1530 is a strongly barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1002.1252, arXiv:astro-ph/0504393] featuring a large bar [arXiv:1002.1252] and a nuclear region with a nuclear bar, spiral, and ring structure [arXiv:astro-ph/9904354, arXiv:astro-ph/0504393]. This nuclear feature exhibits dense molecular gas [arXiv:astro-ph/9904354] and an enhanced rate of star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/9904354, arXiv:1204.6212].

sources.summary: The paper maps molecular gas in the center of the barred spiral NGC 1530, finding nuclear and arc structures, lower nuclear CO ratios, denser gas, and enhanced central star formation.

quotes.quote: ^ 13 CO(1--0) and ^ 12 CO(2--1) in the center of the barred galaxy 15 D. Reynaud 1,2 and D. Downes 1 D. Reynaud (dreynaud@discovery.saclay.cea.fr) Institut de Radio Astronomie Millim etrique, F-38406 Saint Martin d'H eres, France CEA/DSM/DAPNIA/SAp, C.E. Saclay, F-91191 Gif-sur-Yvette Cedex, France Received date; accepted date center of 15 We present ^ 13 CO(1--0) and ^ 12 CO(2--1) aperture synthesis maps of the barred spiral galaxy 15. The angular resolutions are respectively 3''.1 and 1''.6 . Both transitions show features similar to the ^ 12 CO(1--0) map, with a nuclear feature (a ring or unresolved spiral arms) surrounded by two curved arcs.

The ^ 12 CO/ ^ 13 CO ratio is lower in the circumnuclear ring ( 6-8 ) than in the arcs ( 11-15 ). We fit the observed line ratios by escape probability models, and deduce that the gas density is probably higher in the nuclear feature ( 5 2 cm ^ 3 ) than in the arcs ( 2 2 cm ^ 3 ), confirming earlier HCN results. The kinetic temperatures are in the range 20-90 K, but are weakly constrained by the model. The average filling factor of the ^ 12 CO(1--0) emitting gas is low, 0.15 . The cm-radio continuum emission also peaks in the nuclear feature, indicating a higher rate of star formation than in the arcs.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1530 as a strongly barred galaxy, deriving a slower bar pattern speed and comparing simulated gas dynamics with observed velocity fields.

quotes.quote: NGC 1530 is an archetypal barred galaxy, at intermediate inclination (between 40 ^ -50 ^ according to different studies), it has one of the largest bars ever observed, around 24 kpc in length. The bar is dominated morphologically in the optical bands by dust lanes running straight along the bar. It has a complicated morphology in the nuclear region characterised in the optical and CO bands by a nuclear ring/spiral.

We have carried out a detailed modelling of NGC 1530 to study the position of the resonances, and the correspondence of the dynamics with the star formation in this galaxy. As already mentioned, this is not the first attempt to understand the relation between the star formation and the morphology and kinematics of this galaxy but this is the first attempt to do a detailed comparison with numerical models, where the potential is derived directly from the light distribution of NGC 1530.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1530 as a barred-galaxy template, connecting its velocity field, bar geometry, arms, circumnuclear disc, and non-circular gas motions to star-formation analysis.

quotes.quote: For this we use our recently measured velocity field of the strongly barred galaxy NGC 1530. In Zurita et al. (2004) we describe the TAURUS Fabry--P erot interferometer on the 4.2m WHT, La Palma (Spain) with which we made the two dimensional velocity map of the ionized hydrogen using \ emission, and give details of the observations and reduction. The map has a velocity interval of 18.6 km s \ and an angular resolution of 1 , which mark improvements on previous optical and radio maps by Regan et al. (1996), by Downes et al. (1996) and by Reynaud Downes (1997, 1998, 1999). We derived first the classical rotation curve, and used its two dimensional projection to subtract off from the observed velocity field, yielding a residual field of non--circular projected velocity over the full face of the galaxy. In Fig. mos_gal we show how this was done and illustrate our results. NGC 1530 is an excellent subject for kinematic analysis. Its arms, bar and circumnuclear disc are very well separated; the major axis and bar position angle are well separated, and the inclination permits good velocity and intensity measurement.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1530 as a strongly barred spiral where the observations reveal major-axis kinematic detail, a nuclear spiral, a large velocity gradient, and a nuclear bar with hot spots.

quotes.quote: NGC 1530: This strongly barred spiral galaxy has been extensively studied by Zurita et al. 2004. Here the observation provides much more details of the kinematics along the major axis due to its large FOV and high sensivity. A nuclear spiral and a large velocity gradient are observed. In the V-band images, a nuclear bar ( 1993AJ....105.1344B 1993AJ....105.1344B ) with hot spots ( 1973PASP...85..103S 1973PASP...85..103S ) can be seen.

sources.summary: The paper resolves star-forming knots in NGC 1530, finds additional larger-radius gas emission, and reports small abundance-calculation differences for it.

quotes.quote: In NGC 1832 and NGC 1530 we resolve knots of star formation (SF hereafter) at radius R 0.25 R _ 25 ( R_ 25 being the length of the projected major axis of a galaxy at the isophotal level 25 mag/arcsec ^2 in the B-band).

For seven galaxies (NGC 1433, NGC 1530, NGC 1832, NGC 2665, NGC 2935, NGC 3081 and NGC 4314) we have also found knots of gas emission at larger radii, corresponding to one or several crowded \ regions.

For example, in NGC 1530 the mean values of these differences are 0.04 0.04 dex ( R _ 23 ), 0.05 0.04 dex ( Z94 ), 0.03 0.02 dex ( KD03 ) and 0.13 0.09 dex ( P05 ) () The error introduced if we consider an I( )/I( ) value of either 2.85 or 3.1 is as low as 0.05 dex.

sources.summary: The paper uses H II regions in NGC 1530 to test optically thick clumpy models, estimate large neutral-to-ionized gas ratios, and compare observed radial surface-brightness profiles with model predictions.

quotes.quote: We estimate the mean H ^ /H ^+ ratio for a population of \ regions in the spiral galaxy NGC 1530 to be the order of 10, and support this inference using dynamical arguments.

We selected one of the largest isolated \ regions with large radii in the galaxy NGC 1530 for which we had continuum subtracted \ images with good signal to noise ratio, measured its radial surface brightness profile in , and from this profile derived the distribution of volume emissivity in \ as a function of radius within the region.
The results of calculations of this type for a set of observed \ regions in the barred spiral NGC 1530 are shown in Fig. mass .

sources.summary: The paper studies H II-region emission-line profiles in the barred spiral NGC 1530, finding low-intensity high-velocity wing features in more than a third of the regions and interpreting them as shell signatures.

quotes.quote: We study the presence of low intensity high velocity components, which we have termed wing features in the integrated \ emission line profiles of the \ region populations of the spiral barred galaxies NGC 1530, NGC 3359 and NGC 6951. We find that more than a third of the \ region line profiles in each galaxy show these components. The highest fraction is obtained in the galaxy whose line profiles show the best S:N, which suggests that wing features of this type may well exist in most, if not all, \ region line profiles.

gmw_00001556.name: 1ES 1927+654
gmw_00001556.overview: Previously classified as a true type-2 Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1903.11084, arXiv:2102.05666], 1ES 1927+654 is a nearby active galactic nucleus that underwent a changing-look event beginning with an optical and ultraviolet outburst in late 2017 [arXiv:1903.11084, arXiv:2309.10610]. This transient event was followed by the emergence of broad Balmer emission lines [arXiv:1903.11084, arXiv:2007.07275] and extreme X-ray variability, including the temporary disappearance of its power-law X-ray component [arXiv:2007.07275, arXiv:2309.10610].

sources.summary: The paper presents 1ES 1927+654 as a changing-look AGN whose optical/UV brightening, delayed broad Balmer lines, UV-line absence, and X-ray flares challenge simple obscuration or inflow-change explanations.

quotes.quote: Remillard MIT Kavli Institute for Astrophysics and Space Research, 70 Vassar Street, Cambridge, MA 02139, USA 0000-0002-1661-4029 Michael Loewenstein Astrophysics Science Division, NASA Goddard Space Flight Center, 8800 Greenbelt Road, Greenbelt, MD 20771, USA Department of Astronomy, University of Maryland, College Park, MD 20742, USA We study the sudden optical and ultraviolet (UV) brightening of 1ES 1927+654, which until now was known as a narrow-line active galactic nucleus (AGN). 1ES 1927+654 was part of the small and peculiar class of ``true Type-2'' AGN, which lack broad emission lines and line-of-sight obscuration. Our high-cadence spectroscopic monitoring captures the appearance of a blue, featureless continuum, followed several weeks later by the appearance of broad Balmer emission lines.

Hubble Space Telescope spectroscopy reveals no evidence for broad UV emission lines (e.g., C iv 1549 , C iii ] 1909 , Mg ii 2798 ), probably owing to dust in the broad-line emission region. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first case where the lag between the change in continuum and in broad-line emission of a ``changing-look'' AGN has been temporally resolved. The nature and timescales of the photometric and spectral evolution disfavor both a change in line-of-sight obscuration and a change of the overall rate of gas inflow as driving the drastic spectral transformations seen in this AGN. Although the peak luminosity and timescales are consistent with those of tidal disruption events seen in inactive galaxies, the spectral properties are not. The X-ray emission displays a markedly different behavior, with frequent flares on timescales of hours to days, and will be presented in a companion publication. galaxies: active --- galaxies: nuclei --- quasars: general --- quasars: emission lines --- galaxies: individual: 1ES 1927+654 Large-scale time-domain surveys have allowed the recent identification of new types of extreme variability among both dormant and active supermassive black holes (SMBHs).

sources.summary: The paper follows 1ES 1927+654 through a changing-look outburst, broad-line emergence, extreme X-ray variability, apparent corona destruction, and later reappearance of the power-law component.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1927+654 ( z=0.019422 , Trakhtenbrot:2019qy ) was previously classified as a type 2 AGN (e.g., Gallo:2013hq and references therein). In March 2018 the All-Sky Automated Survey for SuperNovae Shappee:2014mw showed that the source suddenly increased in flux by an order of magnitude in the optical V-band (ASASSN-18el/AT2018zf, Nicholls:2018yo ). The analysis of Asteroid Terrestrial-impact Last Alert System (ATLAS, Tonry:2018qy ) data indicated that the event was first detected on December 23 2017. An optical spectroscopic follow-up campaign showed first a very blue continuum and then, 1-3 months after the optical flux rise, evidence of strong broad Balmer emission lines Trakhtenbrot:2019qy .

After the optical brightening event, 1ES 1927+654 showed a spectrum completely different from the 2011 XMM-Newton observation. In the first XMM-Newton observation of our campaign (June 2018, red spectrum in Fig. fig:spectra ), the spectrum was extremely soft, with the emission being dominated by a blackbody-like component. The disappearance of the power-law component, which has not hitherto been observed in AGN, implies that the dramatic event that created the broad lines also destroyed the X-ray corona. The X-ray spectra of 1ES 1927+654 can be typically well modeled by a blackbody, two Gaussian emission lines and a power-law component.

sources.summary: The paper follows 1ES 1927+654 as a nearby changing-look AGN, emphasizing its optical brightening, true type-2 history, and unusually soft, variable X-ray spectrum.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1927+654 is the first accreting SMBH that was detected in the act of changing phase Trakhtenbrot:2019qy . This object is a rather well known nearby ( z=0.019422 ) AGN, showing peculiar characteristics, completely unexpected within the framework of the AGN unification model (e.g., Antonucci:1993fu,Urry:1995xy,Netzer:2015zn,Ramos-Almeida:2017qq,Hickox:2018mz ). While its optical spectrum suggested a type-2 classification Bauer:2000rp , in the X-rays the AGN appears completely unobscured (i.e., N_ H 10^ 20 cm^ 2 ; Boller:2003cq,Gallo:2013hq ), making it a member of the true type 2 class (e.g., Panessa:2002cv,Brightman:2008wo,Shi:2010fq,Bianchi:2012bq ). Consistent with this idea, did not find any evidence of hidden polarized broad H or direct broad Pa and Br lines. 1ES 1927+654 was found to have increased its flux by at least two magnitudes in the optical V-band in March 2018 (AT2018zf/ASASSN-18el, Nicholls:2018yo,Stanek:2018an ) by the All-Sky Automated Survey for Supernovae (ASAS-SN; Shappee:2014mw ).

X-ray spectral properties 1ES 1927+654 The X-ray spectral shape of 1ES 1927+654 inferred from our X-ray monitoring campaign is very different from what has been observed in typical AGN; the main peculiarities of this object are the supersoft continuum, dominated by a blackbody component ( kT 80-200 eV), the disappearance of the power law component Ricci:2020 , the broad emission features at 1 keV and 1.8 keV, and the extremely low cutoff energy of the power-law ( E_ C 3 keV). In the following we discuss first the X-ray characteristics of 1ES 1927+654 inferred by previous studies ( sect:previousXrayobs ), and then the exceptional properties observed over the past 2 years ( sect:Xrayproperties1819 ). Previous X-ray observations of 1ES 1927+654 1ES 1927+654 was first detected in the X-ray band by the Einstein slew survey, and classified as an AGN ( Elvis:1992aa , see also Perlman:1996aa and Simcoe:1997zm ).

sources.summary: The paper studies 1ES 1927+654 as a changing-look AGN, deriving host-galaxy structure and mass while tracking broad-line-region evolution after its outburst.

quotes.quote: We investigate follow-up optical spectroscopy spanning 500 days after the outburst of the changing-look AGN 1ES 1927+654. The emission lines displayed dramatic, systematic variations in intensity, velocity width, velocity shift, and symmetry. Analysis of optical spectra and multi-band images indicate that the host galaxy contains a pseudobulge and a total stellar mass of 3.56_ 0.35 ^ +0.38 10^ 9 M_ .

A changing-look event was recently reported and studied Trakhtenbrot2019ApJ,Ricci2020ApJL,Ricci2021ApJS in the nearby ( z=0.019422 ) galaxy 1ES 1927+654, a previously known type 2 (narrow-line) AGN Boller2003AA,Tran2011ApJ . Following its discovery by the All-Sky Automated Survey for Supernovae (ASAS-SN; Shappee2014ApJ ), its V band flux increased by at least 2 magnitudes in March 2018 ( Nicholls2018ATel ). Subsequent optical spectroscopic follow-up spanning 500 days found the emergence of broad Balmer emission several weeks after the outburst, followed by broad \ emission detected on 28 August 2018 Trakhtenbrot2019ApJ .

With a stellar mass of merely M_ = 3.56_ 0.35 ^ +0.38 10^ 9 M_ , the host galaxy of 1ES 1927+654 is relatively young (stellar population age 1 Gyr) and likely hosts a pseudo bulge that comprises 44 of the total mass. We use these mass measurements and an estimate of the stellar velocity dispersion of the bulge, in conjunction with standard scaling relations between BH mass and host galaxy properties, to bracket the BH mass in the range (1-5) 10^ 6 M_ , with a preferred, final adopted value of M_ BH = 1.38_ 0.73 ^ +1.57 10^ 6 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 1927+654 as a changing-look Seyfert whose 2017 UV/optical outburst and X-ray dip may result from a tidal-disruption-driven episodic accretion event.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage 1ES 1927+654 was known as a type 2 Seyfert galaxy, which exhibited drastic variability recently in ultraviolet (UV)/optical and X-ray bands. An UV/optical outburst was observed in the end of 2017, and it reached the peak luminosity 50 days later. The high-cadence observations showed a rapid X-ray flux decline with complete disappearance of the power-law hard X-ray component when the soft X-ray thermal emission reached its lowest level about 150 days after the UV/optical peak. The power law X-ray component reappeared with thermal X-ray emission brightening from its lowest flux within next 100 days.

1ES 1927+654 was a narrow-line Seyfert galaxy, z=0.019422 , before the UV/optical outburst observed in 2017 e.g., 2003A&A...397..557B,2007ApJ...660.1072W,2011ApJ...726L..21T , while it emerges as an unusual CLAGN since 2017 2019ApJ...883...94T,2020ApJ...898L...1R,2021ApJS..255....7R,2022ApJ...931....5L,2022ApJ...934...35M . 2021MNRAS.502L..50S argued that a change in the accretion rate and an inversion of magnetic flux in a magnetically arrested disc (MAD) are required to explain the extraordinary feature of the X-ray dip after the UV/optical outburst in 1ES 1927+654, though the detailed physical mechanism triggering such a flux inversion event is still quite unclear. For 1ES 1927+654, a Seyfert 2 galaxy in the pre-outburst period, its inner accretion disc with corona would be destructed instantly when a normal star is tidally disrupted by the BH. The UV/optical outburst was observed at the end of 2017 for 1ES 1927+654, and the peak of the UV/optical emission is about four magnitudes brighter than that in the pre-outburst state, which implies that the accretion rate of the newly formed outer disc after the TDE is 40 times of that of the primary inner thin disc.

sources.summary: The paper presents 1ES 1927+654 as a local face-on changing-look Seyfert whose 2017-2019 transient revealed strong spectral changes, host-galaxy starlight, and a disk-dominated broad-line region.

quotes.quote: More recent detections are, for example, NGC 2617 shappee14 , Mrk 590 denney14 , HE 1136-2304 parker16, zetzl18, kollatschny18 , WISE J1052+1519 stern18 , or 1ES 1927+654 trakhtenbrot19b . However, only a few of them -- most notably 1ES 1927+654 -- have been studied spectroscopically in greater detail in temporal proximity to the transient event. This lack of high-quality data, however, significantly complicates the understanding of the CL phenomenon. ( _ 2000 =04^ h 20^ m 00.42^ s , _ 2000 = -54^ 56^ 16.1^ ) is a local ( z=0.00502 ) face-on Seyfert galaxy https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ and is known for exhibiting recurrent outbursts accompanied by changes in spectral type shobbrook66,pastoriza70,alloin85,alloin86 . The most recent transient event occurred from 2017 to 2019, and an accompanying CL event (a change in Seyfert classification from Sy 1.8 to Sy 1.2, i.e., from showing only weak broad emission in and to showing stronger broad emission) was reported by oknyansky19, oknyansky20 .

All spectra of (see Fig. fig:all_spectra ) show a strong stellar signature from the underlying host galaxy. This holds especially true for the low-state spectra before and after the outburst, where the stellar signature of the host galaxy clearly dominates the continuum regions of the spectra. In order to determine the host galaxy contribution, we perform a spectral synthesis on Spectrum 1 from 2015 September 24.
This allows us to estimate the host galaxy contribution in the original low-state spectrum to be 60 and 70 in the B and V band, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes es as an optical type-2 AGN with little X-ray obscuration, typical Eddington ratio, rapid X-ray variability, and spectra compatible with blurred reflection or partial covering models.

quotes.quote: es \ is an active galactic nucleus (AGN) that appears to defy the unification model. It exhibits a type-2 optical spectrum, but possesses little X-ray obscuration. \ and \ observations obtained in 2011 are used to study the X-ray properties of es.

The spectral energy distribution derived from simultaneous optical-to-X-ray data obtained with \ shows the AGN has a typical Eddington ratio ( L/L_ Edd = 0.014-0.11 ). The X-ray spectrum and rapid variability are consistent with originating from a corona surrounding a standard accretion disc. Partial covering models can describe the x-ray data; however, the narrow \ emission line predicted from standard photoelectric absorption is not detected.

sources.summary: The paper includes 1ES 1927+654 as a changing-look AGN with very steep Balmer decrement, adopted black-hole mass, and low-Eddington-ratio behavior in its Balmer relation.

quotes.quote: The H /H in some optical CLAGNs showing the appearance and disappearance of broad emission lines can reach up to 10 or even higher with a decrease of luminosities ( e.g., H /H =8-15 at 150 days after the transient in 1ES 1927+654, Trakhtenbrot2019 ; H /H =8-10 in NGC 2992, Guolo2021 ; H /H =4-6 in NGC 3516, Shapovalova2019 ; H /H =4-9 in Mrk 1018, Cohen1986,McElroy2016 ). The effects of collisional excitation and/or dust extinction are suggested to be the reason for such a large deviation of the Balmer decrement .

For CLAGNs, we include 6 sources with multiple optical spectral observations (NGC 5548, NGC 4151, 3C 390.3, NGC 3516, Mrk 1018, and 1ES 1927+654) and 16 sources with only two observations from . We note that only the emission-line intensities at a high state (or turn-on state) with evident broad lines are adopted in our work because the emission lines are weak or absent in a low state (or turn-off state). The multiple observational data for NGC 5548, NGC 4151, and 3C 390.3 are selected from . For Mrk 1018, we adopt quasi-simultaneous observation of H and H lines from and . The emission line data of NGC 3516 are selected from , where we exclude the data from 2014 to 2018 due to the H line being too weak and the measurements on the broad component are not accurate. The data of 1ES 1927+654 is selected from . For CLAGNs with only two spectra in , we exclude the objects observed by XinLong Telescope for the bad spectral quality and several sources with very large differences of velocity even in active state (i.e., FWHM(H )/FWHM(H ) 1.5 or FWHM(H )/FWHM(H ) 1.5 ). The source ZTF18aajupnt is considered to be a candidate for tidal disruption event Jin2022 , which is also removed from our sample. The BH mass log M_ BH =8.61, 7.72, 7.81, 7.28, 7.70, and 7.90 are adopted for the CLAGN of 3C 390.3 Afanasiev2019 , NGC 4151 Du2015 , NGC 5548 Afanasiev2019 , 1ES 1927+654 Trakhtenbrot2019 , NGC 3516 Vasudevan2010 and Mrk 1018 Noda2018 , respectively.
Except for the evident deviation of H /H for sources with _ Edd < 1-2 , the slope of the H /H _ Edd correlation also become steeper in CLAGNs of 3C 390.3 and 1ES 1927+654 at lower Eddington ratios.

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 1927+654 as a changing-look AGN whose optical brightening and X-ray collapse arise from accretion-rate changes and magnetic-flux inversion in a MAD.

quotes.quote: We argue that the changing-look event in the active galactic nucleus 1ES 1927+654, followed by a dip of 3 orders of magnitude in the X-ray luminosity, is controlled by a change in the accretion rate and an inversion of magnetic flux in a magnetically arrested disk (MAD). Before the changing-look event, strong magnetic flux on the black hole powers X-ray emission via the Blandford-Znajek process while the UV emission is produced by a radiatively inefficient magnetized disk. An advection event, bringing flux of the opposite polarity, propagates inward leading, first, to a rise in the UV/optical luminosity and, then, to a dip in the X-ray luminosity when it reaches the black hole. We estimate the timescale for magnetic flux advection and find that the observed timescale between the beginning of the changing-look event and the minimum in the X-ray luminosity, 200 days, is in agreement with the time needed to cancel the magnetic flux in a MAD extending to 180 r_g .

sources.summary: The paper follows the changing-look AGN 1ES 1927+654 through a bright soft-X-ray state, finding strong soft-X-ray brightening without matching UV change.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1927+654 (alias AT2018zf, ASASSN-18el) is one such CL-AGN that has undergone some dramatic changes trak19, Ricci2020, Ricci2021,laha2022, Li_broad_line_1es, masterson2022 in the recent past showing behaviour, unlike other known CL-AGNs.

We followed up the enigmatic changing look AGN 1ES 1927+654 with multi-wavelength observations spanning a period over 1 year (Jan 2022- May 2023). Below we list the most important conclusions. We have observed a recent brightening of the soft X-ray flux since May 2022, although there is no appreciable change in the UV flux. The total energy pumped into the soft X-rays over a period of 11 months (20th May 2022- 5th May 2023) is 3.6 10^ 50 erg, and that of the hard X-rays is 5.9 10^ 49 , an order of magnitude lower.

gmw_00001557.name: NGC 4939
gmw_00001557.overview: NGC 4939 is an SAbc spiral galaxy [arXiv:2111.10036] harboring a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2104.11263, arXiv:2111.10036] with a biconical ionization region [arXiv:1708.02683]. The galaxy features an intermediate-age stellar bar [arXiv:2111.10036] and a clumpy, geometrically thick gas-and-dust torus [arXiv:2104.11263].

sources.summary: The paper groups NGC 4939 with Sy2 sources showing X-ray absorption variability, high covering factors, and intermediate/high opening angles, preferring a thick clumpy gas-and-dust torus intercepted along the line of sight.

quotes.quote: Panel 5 of Figure fig:cartoon_Sy2 (e.g. NGC 4507, NGC 1052, NGC 4939, and IC 5063): Sy2 sources with absorption variability at X-rays, high covering factors, and half opening angles at both X-rays and mid-IR consistent with intermediate or high values. A clumpy distribution of both gas and dust is preferred for this group of objects. All the properties described above can be explained with a geometrically-thick torus where dust and gas is distributed in clumps. The gas is probably located at both the outer dusty and inner dust-free regions, the latter producing the observed absorption variability. Indeed the inclination angles constrained for these sources are consistent with these clumps being intercepted by the LOS.

sources.summary: NGC 4939 is an SAbc Seyfert 2 galaxy with no significant young populations, a bar of intermediate age and elevated metallicity, constant stellar dispersion, higher nuclear reddening, and no significant featureless continuum.

quotes.quote: NGC 4939 NGC 4939 is an SAbc galaxy, harboring a Seyfert 2 AGN. No significant traces of SPs under 2 Gyr were detected in this galaxy. However, a bar with 4 Gyr and <z>_L 1.5 Z _ can be observed, contrasting with the rest of our FoV (t > 10 Gyr and solar metallicity). Also, the stellar is roughly constant, at 150 km s ^ 1 , and the A _ V is higher in the nucleus compared to the rest of our FoV (0.6 mag vs 0.2 mag). Lastly, we did not detect any significant contributions of a FC except for border effects.

sources.summary: The paper favors a Compton-thick cold-reflection model for NGC 4939, while noting possible lower-energy warm scattering and residual uncertainty because the iron line and luminosity ratio are less extreme.

quotes.quote: NGC 4939 The Compton thick, cold reflection model (with N _H>10^ 25 cm ^ 2 , i.e. reflection throughout the PDS range) provides the best fit to the data in the 3--100 keV spectral range. The warm scattering model is ruled out at a high significance level (reduced _r^2 > 2 ); the Compton thin, transmission model (N _H=3.0 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) is worse than the cold reflection model at a confidence level larger than 90 (above 3 keV, ^2 = 6 with only one degree of freedom less); both alternatives are especially bad in the 10--100 keV band, where they fall short of accounting for the observed flux. As in the case of NGC2273, if in the transmission model the photon index is thawed (see Appendix) the best fitting value is 0.7, adding further support to the Compton thick, cold reflection interpretation. The iron line appears to contain a H--like component at 6.96 keV that is as strong as the neutral-fluorescence line at 6.4 keV. Perhaps a warm scattered component is present at lower energies (i.e. NGC 4939 might be similar to NGC 1068, Matt et al. ), but the signal--to--noise in our data is not high enough to detect any additional component of the continuum. We should note that the EW of the 6.4 keV line is only 400 eV and that the L _X /L _ [ OIII ] only about one order of magnitude lower than the average in Sy1s (Fig. fig_xoiii ). As a consequence, the preference for the cold reflection, Compton thick model over a transmission model with N _H=3 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 is still questionable.

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe NGC 4939 as a compact Seyfert 2 with bar-extended emission, HST biconical ionization perpendicular to the disk, and faint HII regions in the field.

quotes.quote: NGC 4939: A compact Sy 2 nucleus is seen in \ but it is clearly extended by 16 arcsec along the bar ( PA = 5^ ) in . However, with HST, a biconical ionization region is seen both in \ and in the UV extended perpendicularly to the disk of the galaxy Martini03,Munoz07 . There are a number of faint \ regions in the field. The \ regions in this galaxy have been catalogued by Tsvetanov95 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4939 as the sample's most luminous galaxy, with a steeply rising then flat rotation curve and consistent kinematic-photometric parameters.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:N4939 presents our results for NGC 4939, which is the most luminous galaxy in our sample. The rotation curve rises steeply before becoming approximately flat for R 25 at a value of 270 km s ^ 1 out to nearly 40 kpc in the disk plane. Our kinematic projection parameters and center for this galaxy agree very well (see Figure fig:projcomp ) with those derived from the I band image by RINGSPhot .

gmw_00001558.name: NGC 1023A
gmw_00001558.overview: NGC 1023A is a low-surface-brightness Magellanic irregular dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1511.08219] that acts as an interacting companion to NGC 1023 [arXiv:1204.3472, arXiv:1511.08219]. This ongoing interaction has produced a stellar stream [arXiv:1101.5092, arXiv:1512.01185], a gaseous bridge [arXiv:2311.09925], and enhanced star formation within the dwarf companion [arXiv:2311.09925].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1023A as an interacting dwarf companion, finding it connects to NGC 1023E by a gaseous bridge and shows tidal tails and enhanced star formation.

quotes.quote: Within the innermost part of the NGC 1023 group, there are four late-type dwarf companion galaxies Trentham+09 , called NGC 1023A, NGC 1023B, NGC 1023C, and NGC 1023D. Only NGC 1023A was considered to be an ongoing interaction with the main galaxy NGC 1023 and that it has been stripped of its gas Capaccioli+86,Dolfi+21,Silva+22 , resulting in an extended and irregular distribution detected in NGC 1023.

NGC 1023A and NGC 1023E in Fig. fig:NGC1023_FAST blue a are connected not only in space but also in velocity, implying that there is an gaseous bridge between them. Moreover, both the dense gases within NGC 1023A and NGC 1023E show a bowed head-tail structure in the column-density maps of Fig. fig:NGC1023_FAST blue b . Fig. fig:NGC1023_FA blue a shows the overlapping maps of the dense regions of NGC 1023A and NGC 1023E. We found that the orientation of these identified tails is not random. The tail of NGC 1023A bends towards the head of NGC 1023E.
The enhanced star formation activity in NGC 1023A further confirmed that NGC 1023A is interacting with NGC 1023.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1023A as the merging companion whose H I stream and tidal stripping are tested, then rejects it as the origin of the faint fuzzy clusters.

quotes.quote: The underlying B-band image, from noord08 , has been false-coloured to show both the over-all structure of the galaxy and the companion NGC 1023A. Also shown are the locations of the PNe from noord08 and contours showing the highest column densities of H i data from . It is immediately apparent that NGC 1023A contains significant H i , and that there appears to be a tidal tail of gas being stripped from this system, stretching right across the north side of the galaxy, leaving open the possibility that the FFs could be associated with such a merger stream. Indeed, it is notable that a great fraction of the FFs lie close to the gas distribution. They do appear somewhat offset from the maximum of the H i emission, but so are the PNe associated with this stream (see Section sec:velocity ), shown as filled squares in Fig. fig:pos - if they do trace the same feature, it would appear that collisional processes are separating the gas from the stellar components.

This plot immediately clarifies the situation: the H i stream passes right through the velocity of NGC 1023A, confirming the association of the two, and the PNe with peculiar kinematics also largely follow the stream kinematics, with the same kind of modest offset effects seen in the spatial data. However, the FFs, although matching the position and velocity of NGC 1023A, soon part company with the stream, having completely the opposite sign of velocity relative to NGC 1023 on the far side of the system. The FFs are clearly not associated with any tidal stripping of NGC 1023A.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1023A as the companion of NGC 1023, assigning six globular clusters to it and linking rejected clusters, UCDs, and a stream to the interaction.

quotes.quote: Following we have assigned 6 GCs to the companion galaxy using the luminosity ratio between the two galaxies and requiring that the radial velocity of the object has a maximum difference with respect to the systemic velocity of NGC 1023A of | V| 100 km/s. The companion galaxy GCs are 3 red and 3 blue.

The rejected objects seem to form a coherent structure in the phase-space, connected with the companion galaxy, suggesting they may have been stripped from the companion while orbiting the main galaxy. Three of the UCDs are in the same locus as the companion galaxy and one belongs to the stream, suggesting a possible origin of the UCD connected with the interaction of the two galaxies ( duc97 ). This result needs confirmation with more data. Figure fig:Figure8 support a scenario where the rejected GCs are part of a stream connecting NGC 1023A with the main galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1023A as a fainter interacting companion embedded near NGC 1023, producing an asymmetric-arm appearance and coinciding with an H I cloud.

quotes.quote: The most peculiar feature of NGC 1023 is its proximity to the (likely tidally interacting) fainter galaxy NGC 1023A. The latter galaxy appears as a small companion located at the East end of NGC 1023. It appears as a low-luminosity condensation partially embedded in the disc of the larger galaxy. This configuration gives the appearance of an asymmetric spiral arm and led arp66 to include the two galaxies in his Atlas of Peculiar Galaxies as Arp 135.
