Only later BC proposed NGC 1023A to be an interacting individual galaxy stressing also its coincidence with a cloud of a neutral hydrogen (HI) identified by renzo .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1023A as NGC 1023's small eastern companion, bluer from recent star formation, associated with H I gas, and plausibly undergoing accretion by NGC 1023.

quotes.quote: NGC 1023 is included in Arp's Atlas of Peculiar Galaxies arp66 because of a small eastern companion, NGC 1023A. On optical images NGC 1023A looks like an extension of the main galaxy to the E, but its \ color is noticeably bluer than that of NGC 1023 itself and implies that active star formation has proceeded in the companion until 200 Myr ago cap86 . NGC 1023 is known to be rich in HI gas, possibly associated with the companion, and HI maps san84 show that the highest concentration of HI gas in the NGC 1023 system is indeed coincident with the location of NGC 1023A. The radial velocity of the HI gas near NGC 1023A is also consistent with the gas being associated with NGC 1023A cap86 .

Several other dwarf galaxies are known near NGC 1023 dav84 and it seems plausible that the ongoing accretion of NGC 1023A may just be the most recent of several similar events that have taken place in the past.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1023A as NGC 1023's dwarf satellite, associating blue faint fuzzies with it and with clusters formed during a recent interaction.

quotes.quote: Half of the blue FFs appear to be associated with the dwarf satellite galaxy NGC 1023A (which was largely absent from the original HST/WFPC2 coverage), while the remainder are spatially coincident with the densest HI gas that surrounds NGC 1023. The blue FFs have colours that are consistent with young (few 100 Myr old) star clusters that formed during the most recent interaction between NGC 1023 and its satellite NGC 1023A.

It also includes the dwarf satellite galaxy NGC 1023A, which was largely missing from the HST/WFPC2 coverage. With a wider and more continuous field-of-view than the original study of Larsen Brodie (2000) using HST/WFPC2 we search for additional red FFs to confirm their disk-like 2D distribution. Larsen Brodie (2000) also found two blue FFs associated with NGC 1023A.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1023A as a blue companion of NGC 1023, likely affected by recent star formation and gas stripping during a close encounter.

quotes.quote: The companion galaxy is quite noticably bluer than NGC 1023. Its integrated broad-band colors, U-B = 0.0 and B-V=0.7 cap86 would imply a metallicity of [ Fe/H ] = 1.7 or less for an entirely old stellar population bc2001 , or the galaxy might have experienced recent star formation activity. There are no traces of line emission in its spectrum cap86 , indicating no current star formation, but stars might have formed until a few 100 Myr ago when any residual gas was stripped during a close encounter with NGC 1023. With an absolute B magnitude of M_B=-15.7 cap86 , NGC 1023A is about one magnitude fainter than the Small Magellanic Cloud devau91 , but the ongoing star formation in the SMC presumably gives it a somewhat lower mass-to-light ratio than NGC 1023A so the two galaxies might have quite comparable masses.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1023A as the companion galaxy tied to a stellar stream of rejected planetary nebulae, consistent with tidal interaction and an ongoing minor merger.

quotes.quote: The method also successfully identifies a population of outliers whose kinematics are different from those of the main galaxy; these objects can be identified with a stellar stream associated with the companion galaxy NGC 1023A. galaxies: elliptical and lenticular -- galaxies: evolution -- galaxies: individual: NGC 1023 -- galaxies: individual: NGC 1023A -- galaxies: kinematics and dynamics.
The companion galaxy NGC 1023A is mildly apparent even in the raw images, but shows up very clearly in the residuals when the model is subtracted.

A clue to their origin comes from considering the location of NGC 1023A in this plot, as the rejected PNe seem almost all to form a continuous stream that passes through this companion galaxy, as one might expect if the systems are tidally interacting Capaccioli .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1023A as NGC 1023's low-surface-brightness Magellanic irregular dwarf companion and possible processed-gas reservoir involved in the ongoing merger.

quotes.quote: Its closest companion, NGC 1023A, is a Magellanic irregular dwarf galaxy of low surface brightness CAG , located about 2 7 (RC3) East of NGC 1023 centre.
The main disc of NGC 1023 and interacting satellite NGC 1023A are both a possible reservoir of processed gas.

Assessing the properties of the stellar populations in the main disc of NGC 1023 and measuring the metallicity of the gas in the stream arising from the on-going merger between NGC 1023 and NGC 1023A will allow a direct test of any role they may have played in the formation of the NSD.

gmw_00001559.name: SDSS J090152.04+624342.6
gmw_00001559.overview: SDSS J090152.04+624342.6 is an overlapping-trough iron low-ionization broad absorption line quasar at a redshift of approximately 2.1 [arXiv:1710.09527]. Two spectra taken six years apart show no variation in its broad absorption lines [arXiv:1710.09527].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J090152.04+624342.6 as a z about 2.1 overlapping-trough FeLoBAL quasar, finds no significant BAL variation between two SDSS spectra, and recommends follow-up spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we report an identification of SDSS J090152.04+624342.6 as a new unusual FeLoBAL quasar with ``overlapping-trough'' (OFeLoBAL quasars) at z 2.1 . History of SDSS J090152.04+624342.6 SDSS J090152.04+624342.6 was serendipitously extracted from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS, York et al. 2000) Data Release 7 spectroscopic catalog, when we examined the spectrum of the ``unknown'' objects one by one by eye. The object was then classified as a quasar at z=2.09 in the 7^ th SDSS Quasar Catalog (Schneider et al. 2010; Shen et al. 2011) by identifying the broad emission line at the red end as MgII 2800 .

SDSS J090152.04+624342.6 has been observed twice by SDSS with a time interval of 6 yr, which corresponds to a rest-frame time of 2 yr. The two spectra are compared in Figure 1, along with a difference spectrum.
SDSS J090152.04+624342.6 is identified as a new OFeLoBAL quasar at z 2.1 . The spectra taken by SDSS at two epochs with a time interval of 6 yr do not show significant variation of its BAL.

gmw_00001560.name: AGC 122939
gmw_00001560.overview: AGC 122939 is a dwarf galaxy that is genuinely metal-rich for its luminosity [arXiv:2212.06203]. It may have formed from a tidal encounter with UGC 1970 between 1 and 2 Gyr ago, though this scenario is not considered highly likely [arXiv:2212.06203].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AGC 122939 as a metal-rich luminosity-metallicity outlier, examines whether it formed as a tidal dwarf, and finds UGC 1970 a possible but not preferred parent.

quotes.quote: The more extreme outlier in Figure fig:LZR is AGC 122939 (M _B = 15.36, log(O/H)+12 = 8.68), which is located more than 0.7 dex above the trend line from berg2012 . As with AGC 103503, its weighted mean strong-line abundance is perhaps inflated by a higher R23-O23 value (8.78 0.10). However, its O3N2 abundance of 8.41 0.15 is still more than 0.4 dex above the trend line.

However, the nebular lines in the red portion of the spectrum ( > 4700 ) are well defined and clearly indicate an abundance in the range specified by our strong-line method analysis. Hence, this galaxy does appear to be genuinely metal rich for its luminosity.

We conclude, based on the available evidence, that we cannot rule out the possibility that AGC 122939 was formed from a tidal encounter with UGC 1970 between 1 and 2 Gyr in the past. However, we do not consider this scenario to be very likely.

gmw_00001561.name: UGC 1970
gmw_00001561.overview: UGC 1970 is an edge-on spiral galaxy with a large H I mass [arXiv:2212.06203]. It is considered a possible parent galaxy to AGC 122939, although neither galaxy shows visible signs of tidal distortion in optical images [arXiv:2212.06203].

sources.summary: The paper considers UGC 1970 as a possible parent galaxy for AGC 122939, citing its edge-on spiral morphology, larger H I mass, projected separation, velocity offset, and lack of visible tidal distortion.

quotes.quote: The fourth galaxy is UGC 1970, an edge-on spiral with an H 1 mass 15-20 times larger than AGC 122939. UGC 1970 has the smallest projected (minimum) separation of any galaxy from AGC 122939 of 550 kpc. The velocity difference between the two galaxies is 390 km s ^ 1 . The current separation between the two galaxies is fairly large, and neither galaxy exhibits any indication of tidal distortions in their archival optical images.

We can derive an order of magnitude estimate for the amount of time that has elapsed since the tidal encounter that created AGC 122939, assuming UGC 1970 is in fact the parent galaxy. First we assume that the two galaxies are at approximately the same distance, so that the projected separation is similar to their true separation. Then we adopt the approximation that the observed velocity difference between the two galaxies is comparable to how fast the two galaxies are receding from each other in the plane of the sky. This results in a minimum time since the tidal encounter of 1.4 Gyr.

gmw_00001562.name: AGC 103503
gmw_00001562.overview: AGC 103503 is a dwarf galaxy discovered in the ALFALFA survey [arXiv:2212.06203]. Its spectrum reveals a strong stellar continuum and a moderately elevated metal abundance [arXiv:2212.06203].

sources.summary: The paper describes AGC 103503 as an ALFALFA dwarf with a strong stellar continuum and moderately elevated abundance, then argues its high O/H value is unlikely to be caused by high N/O.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of AGC 103503 shows a strong stellar continuum, consistent with the centralized location of the emission region. Both this galaxy and AGC 122939 exhibit low-to-moderate [ O 3 ] /H ratios and clear detections of the [ N 2 ] 6584 line, suggesting relatively high metal abundances.

AGC 103503 (M _B = 16.95, log(O/H)+12 = 8.47) is only slightly elevated in abundance relative to the comparison samples. If one adopts the O3N2 abundance (log(O/H)+12 = 8.37 0.15) rather than the weighted mean of the two strong-line abundances, it would be located within the envelope of values from the comparison data set.

In the case of both AGC 122939 and AGC 103503, their R23-O23 abundances are higher than their O3N2 abundances. Furthermore, neither galaxy has a high [ N 2 ] / [ O 2 ] ratio. Hence, we do not believe that these galaxies are exhibiting higher O/H values because of high N/O ratios.

gmw_00001563.name: AGC 113305
gmw_00001563.overview: AGC 113305 is a dwarf emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2212.06203]. It exhibits a strong H-alpha flux, a solid detection of the [O III] 4363 auroral line, and an oxygen-to-hydrogen abundance ratio that is one-tenth of the solar value [arXiv:2212.06203].

sources.summary: The paper reports that AGC 113305 has the sample's largest H-alpha flux, a solid [O III] 4363 detection, a robust electron-temperature abundance, and the most accurate direct O/H estimate.

quotes.quote: Notably, the galaxy with the largest H flux is AGC 113305 with (6.42 0.13) 10 ^ 14 ergs s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 .

Both AGC 113305 and AGC 336095 have very solid detections of the [ O 3 ] 4363 auroral line, while this line is only weakly detected in AGC 103425. As seen in 5.3, all three yield robust T _e measurements.

AGC 113305, the ALFALFA dwarf with the strongest emission-line flux from our narrow-band imaging data, yields the most accurate direct abundance (uncertainty of 0.03 dex) and is seen to have an O/H abundance ratio of 1/10th of the solar value, adopting (log(O/H)+12) _ = 8.69 asplund2009 .

gmw_00001564.name: AGC 336095
gmw_00001564.overview: AGC 336095 is a dwarf galaxy characterized by active star formation [arXiv:2212.06203]. It exhibits a solid detection of the [O III] 4363 auroral line, which has enabled a robust direct abundance measurement with moderate uncertainty [arXiv:2212.06203].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AGC 336095 as the sample dwarf with the largest star-formation rate, a solid [O III] auroral-line detection, and a direct abundance with moderate uncertainty.

quotes.quote: Therefore, the dwarf galaxy with the largest SFR, AGC 336095, has approximately one third to one fifth the SFR of the Milky Way.

Both AGC 113305 and AGC 336095 have very solid detections of the [ O 3 ] 4363 auroral line, while this line is only weakly detected in AGC 103425. As seen in 5.3, all three yield robust T _e measurements.

For AGC 103425 and AGC 336095, the direct abundances have somewhat higher uncertainties (0.07 dex), which is a reflection of the somewhat higher uncertainties in the derived temperatures (12830 731 K and 14500 760 K, respectively).

gmw_00001565.name: AGC 103425
gmw_00001565.overview: AGC 103425 is a dwarf galaxy that has been observed with multiple spectra [arXiv:2212.06203]. It exhibits a weak detection of the [O III] 4363 auroral line, which nevertheless provides a robust electron-temperature measurement with a moderately uncertain direct abundance [arXiv:2212.06203].

sources.summary: The paper notes that AGC 103425 received two spectra, has a weak [O III] 4363 detection, nevertheless yields a robust electron-temperature measurement, and has a moderately uncertain direct abundance.

quotes.quote: Only one spectrum was obtained for each source, with the exception of AGC 103425, which was observed twice.

Both AGC 113305 and AGC 336095 have very solid detections of the [ O 3 ] 4363 auroral line, while this line is only weakly detected in AGC 103425. As seen in 5.3, all three yield robust T _e measurements.

For AGC 103425 and AGC 336095, the direct abundances have somewhat higher uncertainties (0.07 dex), which is a reflection of the somewhat higher uncertainties in the derived temperatures (12830 731 K and 14500 760 K, respectively).

gmw_00001566.name: AGC 123350
gmw_00001566.overview: AGC 123350 is a dwarf star-forming galaxy with a low neutral hydrogen mass [arXiv:2212.06203]. It has a relatively low metallicity, characterized by weak nitrogen emission and an oxygen abundance of 7.46 [arXiv:2212.06203].

sources.summary: The paper highlights AGC 123350 as the lowest-metallicity galaxy in its sample, with weak nitrogen emission and a low H I mass, but not low enough to be classified as XMP.

quotes.quote: None were found to be extremely metal poor, although one system (AGC 123350) was found to have an oxygen abundance of log(O/H)+12 = 7.46, or 6 solar.

On the other hand, three galaxies show low [ O 3 ] /H ratios but only a very weak detection of [ N 2 ] 6584 (AGC 104765, AGC 123350, and AGC 121439), which is characteristic of galaxies with relatively low metallicities.

The galaxy found to have the lowest metallicity is AGC 123350 with a strong-line abundance of log(O/H)+12 = 7.46 0.10. This system has one of the lowest H 1 masses found in our sample. Overall, the galaxies in our ALFALFA-based sample possess abundances that are completely consistent with those measured in other samples of dwarf star-forming galaxies.

gmw_00001567.name: AGC 104765
gmw_00001567.overview: AGC 104765 is a dwarf galaxy characterized by a low metal abundance [arXiv:2212.06203]. Although its spectrum displays a feature at the expected wavelength of [O III] 4363, this signal is currently considered unreliable due to noise [arXiv:2212.06203].

sources.summary: The paper describes AGC 104765 as a relatively low-metallicity dwarf with a noisy possible [O III] 4363 feature, rejects that feature as unreliable, and says longer spectra may yield a direct abundance.

quotes.quote: We note in passing that the spectrum of AGC 104765 shows a feature at the expected location of [ O 3 ] 4363. We did not expect to be able to detect this line in such a noisy spectrum, but measured it nonetheless.

We concluded that this feature is either noise, or is significantly impacted by noise, and have opted to ignore it. Longer-exposure spectral observations of AGC 104765 might well result in a detection of [ O III ] 4363 and thus a direct abundance estimate.

One of these galaxies, AGC 104765, is located close to the region in the diagnostic diagram occupied by XMP galaxies e.g., izotov2012,hirschauer2016alfalfa . We will show in subsequent sections that the metal abundance of this galaxy is low, but not extreme.

gmw_00001568.name: F257
gmw_00001568.overview: F257 is a high-redshift disk galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.03 [arXiv:0806.3369]. It exhibits a regular velocity field and a rotation curve that flattens at one to two disk scale lengths, closely resembling local late-type disk galaxies [arXiv:0806.3369].

sources.summary: The paper presents F257 as a z=2.03 high-redshift large disk galaxy whose SINFONI velocity field, rotation curve, angular momentum, and stellar-mass Tully-Fisher placement resemble local late-type disks.

quotes.quote: This galaxy, F257, at z=2.03 , was selecte from a sample of candidate high redshift large disk galaxies in the Hubble Deep Field South that were selected with photometric and morphological criteria. We used SINFONI at the VLT to obtain an integral field spectrum of the H line and hence a velocity field and rotation curve. We also use UBVIJHK+IRAC band photometry to determine a stellar photometric mass.

We find that F257 is indistinguishable from local late type galaxies in many respects: it has a regular velocity field, increasing velocity disperion towards its center, its rotation curve flattens at 1-2 disk scale lengths, it has the same specific angular momentum as local disks, its properties are consistent with the local K band TFR. F257 is then a nearly ( 75 ) maximum disk. The dynamical properties of F257 are more like those of local galaxies than those of any other galaxy at similar redshift observed to date.

gmw_00001569.name: Q2343-BX660
gmw_00001569.overview: Q2343-BX660 is a compact emission-line galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.1741 [arXiv:1405.5473]. It exhibits compact rest-optical continuum emission and shares similar rest-UV and rest-optical strong line ratios with the galaxy Q2343-BX418 [arXiv:1405.5473].

sources.summary: The paper treats Q2343-BX660 as an extreme KBSS-MOSFIRE galaxy, noting its BPT location, good spectra, compact emission, and rest-UV and rest-optical line similarities to BX418.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies in the current KBSS-MOSFIRE sample (Q2343-BX418, Q2343-BX660, and Q0207-BX74) are found near the EGPs in the BPT diagram, and have particularly good MOSFIRE (J, H, and K bands) as well as LRIS-B (rest-frame UV) spectra; they are indicated in Figure fig:bpt_mod_peas . Q2343-BX660 ( z=2.1741 ) and Q0207-BX74 ( z=2.1889 ) have similar rest-UV spectra to that of BX418, as shown in Figure fig:q2343_bx660_uv , as well as similar rest-optical strong line ratios. Both Q2343-BX418 and Q2343-BX660 are known to be compact, both in \ emission (from Keck/OSIRIS laser guide star AO IFU observations; law09 ) and in the rest-optical continuum (from HST/WFC3 F160W observations; law2012 ). We performed similar analyses for Q2343-BX660 and Q0207-BX74.

gmw_00001570.name: Haro 11
gmw_00001570.overview: Haro 11 is a luminous blue compact dwarf galaxy undergoing a major merger [arXiv:1609.04020, arXiv:2312.13358]. The system is dominated by three major star-forming knots [arXiv:1701.08024] and is a confirmed emitter of both Lyman-alpha and Lyman-continuum radiation [arXiv:1106.1178, arXiv:1609.04020].

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes low-S/N FUSE observations of Haro 11, develops a new background model, and confirms significant Lyman-continuum leakage from the blue compact galaxy.

quotes.quote: With data from the Far Ultraviolet Spectroscopic Explorer (FUSE), we previously found an absolute escape fraction of ionizing photons ( f_ esc ) of 4-10 for the blue compact galaxy Haro 11 . However, using a revised version of the reduction pipeline on the same data set, Grimes and collaborators were unable to confirm this and derived an upper limit of f_ esc 2 . } We attempt to determine whether Haro 11 is emitting ionizing radiation to a significant level or not. We also investigate the performance of the reduction pipeline for faint targets such as Haro 11, and introduce a new approach to the background subtraction.

Applying the new background fit, a significant signal is detected in the LyC in both detector segments covering these wavelengths. Thus, the leakage is confirmed with a flux density of f_ 900 =4.0 10 ^ 15 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ^ 1 (S/N=4.6), measured on the airglow free regions in the LyC for the night-only data. This corresponds to an absolute escape fraction of ionizing photons from Haro 11 of f_ esc =3.3 0.7 . We confirm these results by investigating the two-dimensional data, the count rates, and the residual flux in C ii 1036 .

sources.summary: The paper studies Haro 11 as a Ly-alpha-emitting blue compact galaxy, measuring Na D absorption that shows outflow from knot B, infall toward knot C, and low neutral-gas covering fractions.

quotes.quote: Haro 11 is a blue compact galaxy at z=0.02 with three large condensations or "knots". The knots are traditionally called A, B and C vader-1993 as shown in the left panel of Figure Fig_Slits . Knot C appears to be the brightest knot in the ultraviolet and it also shows Ly in emission, while knot B is brightest in H , where Ly is instead absorbed. These two knots are therefore of particular interest as we try to explain why Ly is seen from one region but is absorbed in the other. Some evidence suggests that Haro 11 might be the result of a merger of dwarf galaxies ostlin-2001 , particularly due to the irregular appearance, the high relative velocities of several hundred km/s, the broad emission lines, and the presence of a tidal arm structure with a high redshifted velocity relative to the rest of the system ( O stlin et al. 2013, in preparation).

For Haro 11, we see an outflow from knot B at 44 13 km/s and infalling gas towards knot C with 32 12 km/s. Based on the relative strength of the Na D absorption lines, we estimate low covering fractions of neutral gas (down to 10 ) in all three cases. The Na D absorption likely occurs in dense clumps with higher column densities than where the bulk of the Ly scattering takes place.

sources.summary: The paper reports the first robust 21 cm H I detection in Haro 11, finding it H I deficient, gas-poor, merger-driven, and highly efficient at forming stars.

quotes.quote: We present the first robust detection of \ 21 cm emission in the blue compact galaxy Haro 11 using the 100m Robert C. Byrd Green Bank Telescope (GBT). Haro 11 is a luminous blue compact galaxy with emission in both Lyman Alpha and the Lyman continuum. We detect (5.1 \ 0.7 10 ^8 ) \ of \ gas at an assumed distance of 88 Mpc, making this galaxy \ deficient compared to other local galaxies with similar optical properties. Given this small \ mass, Haro 11 has an elevated M _ H2 /M _ HI ratio and a very low gas fraction compared to most local galaxies, and contains twice as much mass in ionized hydrogen as in neutral hydrogen.

Haro 11 is a local starburst galaxy undergoing a major merger of two dwarf galaxies Ostlin:1999cl, Ostlin:2015tc . It has long been known to be a local emitter, but until now has only had upper limits placed on its cold gas content. Our main contribution is the first robust detection of \ gas in emission from this blue compact galaxy consistent with previous upper limits Bergvall:2000tg, MacHattie:2014ipa . We find a dearth of \ gas and a linewidth at 50 of maximum that is smaller relative to other local starburst and LARS galaxies Ostlin:2014vk, Hayes:2014jv, Pardy:2014ir .

sources.summary: The paper presents resolved HST CIII] imaging of Haro 11, describing it as a nearby star-forming blue compact Lyman-break analog undergoing a merger, with LyC leakage and knot-scale emission behavior tied to morphology.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present the results for Haro 11, a.k.a. ESO-350-IG-038, which is at a distance of 87 Mpc, and is well-studied. Haro 11 is a strongly star-forming (SF) blue compact galaxy (BCG) and a Lyman Break Analog (LBA) at redshift z=0.02 ( 87 Mpc), with a metallicity of 12+ O/H =7.9 Bergvall2002 for the galaxy as a whole and 8.09 0.2 , 8.25 0.15 , and 7.8 0.13 for knots A, B, and C, respectively James2013 . In other words, both the galaxy as a whole and the individual knots populate a metallicity range seemingly hospitable to strong \ emission e.g., Berg2016,Senchyna2017 . Haro 11 is undergoing a merger, as indicated by its complex kinematics Ostlin2001, Ostlin2015, James2013,Menacho2019 .

Haro 11 has a Ly escape fraction of 9 Hayes2007 and is the first spectroscopically detected Lyman continuum (LyC) leaker in the local universe Bergvall2006,Leitet2011 , with f_ esc =3.3 Leitet2011 . By all accounts, Haro 11 has all the SF properties and the right metallicity to be a strong \ emitter. Yet from Internation Ultraviolet Explorer (IUE) archival spectra, we measure a total effective 3 . In this paper we study the \ emission with the high spatial resolution offered by the HST, in an attempt to understand the behavior and properties of the \ line.

sources.summary: The paper uses VLT/MUSE data to map Haro 11, a starburst merger and LyC leaker, finding complex gas kinematics, intense knotty star formation, low-density halo gas, and differing metal enrichment from the knots.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We use high quality VLT/MUSE data to study the kinematics and the ionized gas properties of Haro 11, a well known starburst merger system and the closest confirmed Lyman continuum leaking galaxy. We present results from integrated line maps, and from maps in three velocity bins comprising the blueshifted, systemic and redshifted emission. The kinematic analysis reveals complex velocities resulting from the interplay of virial motions and momentum feedback. Star formation happens intensively in three compact knots (knots A, B and C), but one, knot C, dominates the energy released in supernovae.

The halo is characterised by low gas density and extinction, but with large temperature variations, coincident with fast shock regions. Moreover, we find large temperature discrepancies in knot C, when using different temperature-sensitive lines. The relative impact of the knots in the metal enrichment differs. While knot B is strongly enriching its closest surrounding, knot C is likely the main distributor of metals in the halo.

sources.summary: The paper studies Haro 11 as an extreme nearby starburst dwarf, resolving young clusters in knots A, B, and C and connecting them to propagating star formation and outflows.

quotes.quote: We investigate this process in the dwarf galaxy Haro 11, forming stars in three knots (A, B, C). The exquisite resolution of HST imaging allows us to resolve the starburst into tens of bright star clusters. We derive masses between 10^5 and 10^7 M_ and ages younger than 20 Myr, using photometric modeling. We observe that the clustered star formation has propagated from knot C (the oldest) through knot A (in between) towards knot B (the youngest).

Haro 11 is one of the most extreme starburst galaxies known in the nearby Universe (z = 0.021, this work) with a young cluster population (1-20 Myr, this work) and a gas consumption timescale of only 50 Myr oestlin2021 . The central starburst in this galaxy consists of a number of young star clusters, grouped in the three starburst knots called A, B and C, that were presented in e.g. vader1993,kunth2003,adamo2010 (hereafter AA10) using multi-band photometry. Haro 11 is, at the same time, the closest confirmed Lyman continuum leaker in the local Universe bergvall2006,leitet2011 and it is particularly interesting for its similarity to high-redshift Lyman break galaxies, e.g. young stellar population, low metallicity, low stellar mass, e.g. this work overzier2006,grimes2007,bergvall2002,adamo2010 .
The morphology and kinematics of the stars and ionised gas in Haro 11 suggests a resemblance with a dwarf galaxy merger oestlin2015 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Haro 11 as a turbulent merger-system outlier whose inferred ionized-wind flux fraction and outflow rates are far below some previous measurements.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:moment2 shows that the typical velocity dispersion for the ionised gas is in the range 40 - 60 (Fig. fig:moment2 ), with the exceptions of He 2-10 and Haro 11, both reaching values slightly above 100 . Thus the ionised gas in these systems is slightly more turbulent than that of typical star-forming galaxies where 30 10 e.g. Epinat+10,Green+14 , not surprising given their higher-than-average SFR e.g. Bacchini+20 . Clearly, gas turbulence can also be increased by recent mergers/interactions events, which may trigger the starburst episode in the first place. In fact, Haro 11 is thought to be a merger system Ostlin+15 , and He 2-10 shows signatures of a recent accretion event Vanzi+09 .

An extreme case is that of the galaxy Haro 11, for which Chisholm+17 report = 0.08 0.15 , whereas our estimate is 3.4 , about 2 10^3 times smaller. Comparatively large outflow rates are also found by Xu+22 , who infer 3.3 for CGCG007-025 and 1.8 for Haro 11, which are factors 70 and 2600 larger than our measurements, respectively.

Using MUSE data and a dynamical approach that is conceptually similar to that adopted here, Menacho+19 inferred the ionised gas fraction that could escape the gravitational potential in Haro 11, finding values between 0.1 and 0.3 . In our study, instead, the flux fraction of the wind component in Haro 11 is only 0.01 . This discrepancy is largely driven by the different dark matter halo mass assumed for this galaxy: we use 4 10^ 11 from the SHMR of Moster+13 , while Menacho+19 adopt 7 - 9 10^ 10 , from the estimate of Ostlin+15 based on the rotational speed of the ionised gas in the galaxy outskirts.

sources.summary: The paper uses Haro 11 as a nearby luminous blue compact dwarf merger and Lyman-continuum leaker to simulate how spatially inhomogeneous SEDs alter JWST/Euclid morphology and color maps.

quotes.quote: To this end, we use as an illustrative example the nearby ( D=82 Mpc) luminous BCG Haro 11 BO86 . This dwarf galaxy merger O15 is a bright \ emitter Hayes07,O09 and the nearest confirmed Lyman continuum ( ) leaking galaxy Bergvall06,Leitet11 , properties that have made it a popular local analog of galaxies in the epoch of reionization. It contains three high- knots (A--C) with quite different properties O21,Sirressi22 in terms of emission line strength, ionization level and reddening, and therefore serves as a good example of the intrinsic complexity of a starburst galaxy, and how it is amplified through . Section IFS provides a brief summary of the physical characteristics of Haro 11 as obtained from integral field spectroscopy (IFS) and spectral synthesis, and is limited to a few aspects with special relevance to , in particular, the spatially inhomogeneous SED of the BCG as the result of its intrinsic stellar age gradients, a large-scale spatial decoupling of \ from ionizing young stellar clusters (YSCs), and differing extinction patterns in the stellar and nebular component both in their amount and spatially. In turn (Sect. zSim ) we use the method introduced in P23 to compute out to =5.4 the effect of \ on the morphology and color maps of Haro 11 in James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) and Euclid filters.

The morphology and color patterns of Haro 11 result from i) the spatially inhomogeneous SED of its stellar populations, ii) the (SFH- and metallicity-dependent) intensity _ Ly ( ) of \ radiation produced and iii) its reprocessing into \ both on the spot and out to kpc away from it, and iv) the spatially differing extinction patterns of stars and . Evidently, in view of these multiple factors, a meaningful reconstruction of the 2D rest-frame UV (ObsF optical and NIR) characteristics of a distant analog of Haro 11 is barely possible with the usual assumption of a spatially constant "morphological k-correction" adapted to the integral (thus, luminosity-weighted) SED of the BCG (see discussion in PO12 and P23 ).
Using the nearby BCG Haro 11 as an example, we show that \ drastically affects the morphology and color patterns of higher- \ starburst galaxies observed with the JWST and Euclid.

sources.summary: The paper maps Haro 11 as a luminous blue compact, actively star-forming merger candidate whose Ly-alpha morphology, low escape fraction, star clusters, and LyC emission make it a nearby analogue of high-redshift emitters.

quotes.quote: Here we map the \ emission from the local luminous blue compact galaxy Haro 11, a known emitter of \ and the only known candidate for low- z Lyman continuum emission (LyC). To aid in the interpretation we perform a detailed UV and optical multi-wavelength analysis and model the stellar population, dust distribution, ionising photon budget, and star-cluster population. We use archival X-ray observations to further constrain properties of the starburst and estimate the neutral hydrogen column density. The \ morphology is found to be largely symmetric around a single young star forming knot and is strongly decoupled from other wavelengths.

It exhibits a complex morphology consisting of three main star-forming condensations and an unrelaxed kinematic structure ostlin99,ostlin01 , suggestive of a dwarf galaxy merger. It is actively star-forming, exhibits a large number of bright young star clusters ostlin00 , is a known emitter of \ kunth98 , and the only known local candidate emitter of Lyman continuum (LyC) bergvall06,grimes07 . The FUV continuum luminosity puts Haro 11 at the brighter end of the distribution of \ emitters at z 3.1 gronwall07 .

sources.summary: The paper finds Haro 11 has knot-dependent Ly emission and absorption, a diffuse halo, and Ly photons escaping from dusty knot C through scattering or inhomogeneous ionized gas.

quotes.quote:
Blended emission and absorption systems

htbp

htbp

Haro 11   -- The first plot in Fig. haro_plots presents the correlation between the Ly \ emission and the extinction determined from the Balmer decrement tracing the dust in the gas phase ( E_ B-V, gas ). The color-code represents different regions of interest consisting of circular apertures centered on the three main knots of the galaxy, which are marked on the Ly \ image. We can see a diffuse emission component extending up to E_ B-V \ 1.5. Knot C shows a bright and spread emission with a mean extinction of 0.48, while the absorption is essentially localised around knot A and B .

The presence of two distinct emission components is indicative of two different physical processes implied in the escape of Ly \ photons. Firstly the diffuse component that shows the resonant decoupling of Ly \ photons resonantly scattered on hydrogen atoms until they escape far away from there production sites (thus, experiencing a large range of extinction). On the other hand, the emission from knot C is less spread and represents photons escaping directly from this small region with a mean extinction E_ B-V \ 0.48.

sources.summary: The paper presents ESO 350-IG38 as a local Lyman-break-galaxy analogue and uses long-slit CaT spectroscopy to compare stellar and gas kinematics in its starburst regions.

quotes.quote: BCGs in the local universe ( D 100 Mpc) span the range between star-forming dwarfs and systems whose properties more closely resemble starbursting galaxies at higher redshift ( \ Lyman break galaxies ; LBG) while still being close enough for spatially resolved spectroscopy. \ at D =88 Mpc is one of the best local LBG analogues . Its high star formation rate ( 20 ) and lack of detected \ suggests the starburst may be running out of gas. Here we summarise the program so far, present early results for \ (ESO 350-IG38) and take a critical look at the CaT method and which stars it traces.

Star and gas velocity fields We present our spatially-resolved stellar and gas velocity measurements for \ in Figure rc . Our slit joins knots A and C (Fig. \ f-slits ). The velocities measured in the gas and the stars follow one another to within the likely experimental errors. The stellar velocity dispersions are likewise similar to the velocity dispersions measured from the emission line widths.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Haro 11 as a low-redshift blue compact starburst with knots, merger-like kinematics, and a clumpy neutral medium that lets some ionizing radiation escape.

quotes.quote: The galaxy treated in this study is Haro 11, a well-studied Blue Compact Galaxy at low redshift ( z = 0.021 ). Morphologically, it is dominated by three major star-forming knots, called knot A, B and C respectively, following the terminology of Vader1993 see also and fig. fig:apert Kunth1998, Hayes2007 . Knots B and C are both very strong in H , revealing that they also are producing strong Ly But while knot C is a strong Ly emitter, knot B is a strong absorber, indicating that the neutral ISM properties along the line of sight to the two knots are quite different Hayes2009,Ostlin2009 . Its complex kinematics indicates that it is undergoing a merger or major interaction event Ostlin2001, Ostlin2015, James2014 .

We discuss the two extremes of an optically thin, density bounded ISM and a riddled, optically thick, ionization bounded ISM, and how Haro 11 fits into their theoretical predictions. We find that the most likely ISM model is a clumpy neutral medium embedded in a highly ionized medium with a combined covering fraction of unity and a residual neutral gas column density in the ionized medium high enough to be optically thick to Lyman- , but low enough to be at least partly transparent to Lyman continuum and undetected in Si II. This suggests that SF feedback and galaxy-scale interaction events play a major role in opening passageways for ionizing radiation through the neutral medium.

sources.summary: The paper treats Haro 11 as an AGN-contaminated source with three components, X-ray and radio evidence for accretion, merger evidence, and BPT-line ratios indicating AGN excitation.

quotes.quote: Haro 11 displays three distinct components (Fig. Fig:lgoh12_lgsfr_agn , first row). The Eastern knot is associated with a young, high mass, X-ray binary 2015ApJ...812..166P with ultraviolet emission 2003ApJ...597..263K . The southern knot shows strong ultraviolet emission 2003ApJ...597..263K . The central knot is coincident with HI observed in absorption 2014MNRAS.438L..66M , and harbors a luminous, compact, hard X-ray source, perhaps an intermediate mass black hole binary.

The evidence for an accreting black hole is reinforced by the detection of a radio source and references therein 2006ApJ...643..173S . There is also strong evidence that Haro 11 is the result of a merger and references therein Ostlin2015 . The BPT diagram of this galaxy (i.e., O3 = ( [ OIII ] 5007/ H ) versus N2 = ( [ NII ] 6583/ H ) ; after Baldwin, Phillips, and Terlevich 1981PASP...93....5B ) clearly shows evidence for an AGN exciting the emission-line spectrum of this source.

sources.summary: The paper treats Haro 11 as a warm, metal-poor starburst template whose young population and ISM conditions resemble luminous high-redshift infrared galaxies.

quotes.quote: According to lyu2016 , after subtracting the AGN contribution, the host FIR SEDs of z 5--6 quasars are better described by the relatively warm SED of the metal-poor starbursting galaxy Haro 11 ( z 0.02 ), instead of the normal metal-rich SF templates used at z 2-3 or modified blackbodies. derossi2018 modelled the FIR SEDs corresponding to massive Population (Pop) II galaxies (formed after the Pop III stage) during the first phase of significant star formation, finding that these SEDs are significantly shifted to bluer ("warmer") wavelengths compared to the SEDs of local galaxies. In addition, adopting a silicate-rich interstellar dust composition with a small percentage of carbon dust, the theoretical SEDs have very similar behavior to that of Haro 11. Given its moderately-low metallicity and very young stellar population, Haro 11 probably describes the relevant properties in young massive Pop II galaxies at high z .

Fig. fig:fig1 also shows that the SED template based on the local galaxy Haro 11 constitutes a good representation of the z 6 data and is also well described by the theoretical model. As discussed in derossi2018 , this is expected as the interstellar medium conditions in Haro 11 are in reasonable agreement with those corresponding to luminous infrared galaxies at z 6 . Thus, derossi2018 proposed the Haro 11 SED as a proxy template to describe the trends of FIR SEDs at high z .

sources.summary: The paper uses HST/COS spectroscopy of Haro11 knots A, B, and C to identify knot C as the likeliest source of escaping Lyman-continuum radiation.

quotes.quote: The source of leaking ionizing photons from Haro11 -- Clues from HST/COS spectroscopy of knots A, B and C Based of observations with the NASA/ESA Hubble Space Telescope. Here we utilize HST/COS spectroscopy in the wavelength range 1100-1700 \ of the three knots (A, B, and C) of \ to study the presence of \ emission and the properties of intervening gas. We find that all knots have bright \ emission. UV absorption lines, originating in the neutral interstellar medium, as well as lines probing the ionized medium, are seen extending to blue shifted velocities of 500 km/s in all three knots, demonstrating the presence of an outflowing multiphase medium.

We find that knots A and B have large covering fractions of neutral gas, making LyC escape along these sightlines improbable, while knot C has a much lower covering fraction ( 50 ). Knot C also has the the highest \ escape fraction and we conclude that it is the most likely source of the escaping Lyman continuum detected in . Being the nearest known LyC emitter, \ offers unique opportunities to study the details of LyC escape and how indirect LyC diagnostics behave in a spatially resolved fashion.

sources.summary: The paper maps Haro 11 with ALMA and MUSE, finding molecular gas around knots B and C, complex merger kinematics, high star-formation efficiency, and late-stage dwarf-merger status.

quotes.quote: We use the archival data of ALMA (band 3, 8) and VLT/MUSE to map CO( J= 1-0), [ CI ] ( ^3 P _1 - ^3 P _0 ), and emission in one of the most extreme starburst merging dwarf galaxies, Haro 11. We find the molecular gas is assembled around the central two star-forming regions (knots B and C). The molecular/ionized gas and stellar components show complex kinematics, indicating that the gas is probably at a combined stage of collision of clouds and feedback from star formation. The peak location and distribution of [ CI ] (1-0) strongly resemble the CO(1-0) emission, meaning that it might trace the same molecular gas as CO in such a dwarf merger starburst galaxy.

Given the high SFE, sSFR, small stellar mass, low metallicity, and deficient gas, Haro 11 could be an analog of high- z dwarf starburst and the potential progenitor of the nearby less massive elliptical galaxies. The significantly smaller turbulent pressure and viral parameter will probably trigger the intense starbursts. We also predict that it will quench at M_* 8.5 10^9 \ . Galaxies:ISM -- Galaxies: starburst -- Galaxies: individual: Haro 11 -- Galaxies: interactions The physical causes of the starburst and quenching in galaxies are open questions. In the star formation rate (SFR) versus stellar mass ( M_* ) space, galaxies distribute as two broad categories. Star-forming galaxies (SFGs, also known as the blue clouds), most of which are spiral or late-type galaxies, lie on an almost linear relation main sequence, e.g., Brinchmann2004a, Daddi2007, Peng2010, Lilly2013, Speagle2014 . Starburst galaxies undergo an exceptionally intense phase of star formation, which are above the main sequence, representing an evolutionary phase in the life of a galaxy e.g., elbaz2018, Orlitova2020 . Quiescent galaxies (QGs, red sequences), most elliptical or early-type galaxies, have significantly lower SFRs than SFGs and thus lie below the main sequence. Some so-called ``green valley'' galaxies lie between the blue SFGs and red QGs, regraded as the transition zone or quenching galaxies e.g., Bell2003, Fang2012 . These galaxies above/below the main sequence imply that SFGs might enhance or shut down their star formation at some point during their lifetime. A variety of processes can induce starburst activities in galaxies. The strong tidal force caused by gravitational interactions between close pairs or merger galaxies will perturb the orbits of gas and stars, triggering the gas flowing toward the galaxy center efficiently e.g., Larson1978Jan, DiMatteo2007Jun, Ellison2008 . In spiral-barred galaxies, because of the gravitational instabilities of the stellar bar, the gas in the outer spiral can also be transferred into the central region and form new stars e.g., Hopkins2009, Elmegreen2009a, Emsellem2015, DiazGarcia2021 . Similarly, the star formation quenching is correlated with active galactic nuclei (AGN) feedback e.g., dimatteo2005a,kauffmann2007,fabian2012,Cheung2016May,kaviraj2017 , secular processes, such as stellar bars, oval disks and spiral structures e.g., kormendy2004,masters2011,cheung2013 . Meanwhile, some external processes, for example, the galaxy environment and the major mergers e.g., dimatteo2005a,hopkins2006,hopkins2008,knobel2015,peng2015,Weigel2017 can also affect the gas consumption and lead to the quenching of star formation. The merging of galaxies is one of the inescapable processes in galaxy evolution, for both the starburst and quenching activities Hopkins2009 . By perturbing gas rotation, mergers will lead to the rapid inflows of gas and power the intense starburst or feed the central massive black hole. The energetic feedback from AGN or starbursts will heat the interstellar medium (ISM), preventing the gas from cooling and sweep out gas from their host galaxies e.g., fabian2012, Cheung2016May,harrison2018 , which will suppress the star formation and convert the blue SFGs into quiescent red massive elliptical galaxies. In the local universe, major mergers are always observed between spiral galaxies, which could result in the ultraluminous infrared galaxies (U)LIRGs, e.g., Papadopoulos2007, Israel2015, Espada2018, Spence2018, Shangguan2019a . At high redshift universe, where galaxy mergers were more prevalent, most of the observed merger cases are bright and massive submillimeter galaxies (SMGs) e.g., alexander2005,shapiro2008,narayanan2010,brisbin2017,wardlow2017 , suggested to be the progenitors of massive quiescent elliptical galaxies in the local universe. However, the mechanisms of triggering or quenching star formation in low stellar mass or dwarf galaxies i.e, M_* < 5 10^9 \ , Stierwalt2015 are unclear. WHile a large fraction ( > 70 ) in the local universe are dwarf galaxies, only a few percent of them are starburst galaxies, including blue compact galaxies (BCGs), Lyman- reference sample LARS, hayes2013,ostlin2014 , Lyman-break analogs LBAs, heckman2001,heckman2005 , and Green Peas see the review in Orlitova2020 . Many BCGs are relatively isolated, while the morphologies and velocity fields implicate a recent interaction with neighbors. Furthermore, the stellar components in BCGs contain a mixture of young and old populations kunth2000 , perhaps indicating that interactions are responsible for the starburst activity in BCGs. However, because of the weak gravitational potential and strong turbulence by stellar winds, the conversion from molecular gas to stars is suggested to be inefficient in previous star formation models. Whether the merging of two dwarf galaxies can trigger starburst activity, is still an open question. Recently, reported that the star cluster formation rate ( SFR) in the galaxy VCC 848, a remnant of a gas-rich dwarf-dwarf merger, is enhanced 1.0 dex during the past 1 Gyr relative to its earlier times. Though the dwarf-dwarf mergers are quite rare 3 , KadoFong2020 within dwarf galaxies in local universe, they are expected to be frequent at high redshift e.g., Fensch2017,Husko2021,romano2021,Whitney2021 . The evolution results of these dwarf mergers are still uncertain. performed a systematic study about the quenched field galaxy fraction using the SDSS DR8 spectra, demonstrating that it is smaller than 0.06 when M_* < 10^9 \ , while are about 0.5 and 3 for less massive galaxies at M_* < 10^ 9.5 \ and M_* < 10^ 9.9 \ , respectively. Assuming the merger quenching scenario in dwarf galaxies is similar to more massive ones, dwarf mergers at high redshift would be the progenitors of these nearby dwarf elliptical galaxies. However, it is difficult to study the detailed starburst activities and gas consumption for these distant dwarf mergers because of the low brightness. Thus, analogs of these distant cases, metal-poor gas-rich dwarf mergers with high spatial resolution, multi-band observations, can provide the ideal laboratories to study the physical mechanisms of triggering and quenching the star formation in such dwarf galaxies. In this paper, we focus on the molecular/neutral gas properties of Haro 11, which is a merging system and one of the most extreme starburst BCDs in the nearby universe e.g., Cormier2012,Cormier2014,Oestlin2015,Pardy2016,Menacho2019,Menacho2021,Oestlin2021 . The redshift ( z ) is about 0.0206, collected from the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu (NED), corresponding to the distance of 87.2 Mpc and a scale of 420 pc per arc second. Haro 11 is a late-stage merger, showing similar morphology and kinematics of stars and ionized gas to the well-known Antennae (NGC 4038/4039) galaxy Oestlin2015 .

sources.summary: The paper directly images Haro 11 neutral gas, arguing merger-driven displacement forms a tidal-tail-like structure that may facilitate anisotropic Lyman-continuum escape.

quotes.quote: We have directly imaged the neutral gas in the nearby reionization-era analog galaxy Haro 11 with the line to identify the mechanism enabling ionizing radiation escape. We find that merger-driven interactions have caused a bulk offset of the neutral gas by about 6 kpc from the center of the galaxy, where LyC emission production sites are located. This could facilitate the escape of ionizing radiation into our line of sight. Galaxy interactions can cause both elevated LyC production and large-scale displacement of \ from the regions where these photons are produced.

The merger nature of Haro 11 had already been demonstrated by ionized gas kinematics studies Ostlin2001,Ostlin2015 , but the impact of the interaction on the neutral gas distribution was previously unknown. In fact, the neutral gas structure in Haro 11 strongly resembles a smaller version of the Southern tidal tail in the more massive Antennae galaxy merger Hibbard2001 , to which Haro 11 has been compared previously due to striking similarity in ionized gas kinematics Ostlin2015 . To further analyze how the merger impacts the neutral gas distribution and star formation in Haro 11 as a function of time, our team has developed simulations of the merger that will be presented in a forthcoming paper in prep.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Haro11 as a star-forming merger-dominated galaxy among four deep-diving systems with depressed metallicity, high SFR, late merger stage, and solar-like N/O.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, four galaxies namely Haro11, IRAS12112+0305, IRAS20551-4250 and IRAS23128-5919 strongly deviate from the MZR showing metallicities lower by a factor of 2 than expected for these deep-diving (U)LIRGs stellar masses according to the MZR. While Haro11 is not a ULIRG galaxy like the others, this star-forming galaxy is reported to be a well known merger dominated galaxy that shared many of properties observed in ULIRGs. Notably, IRAS12112+0305 was previously reported to have a super-solar abundance ( 2Z _ ) , while IRAS20551-4250 and IRAS23128-5919 where reported to have abundances close to the values expected from the MZR ar their masses . These high estimations of O/H are driven by the nearly solar N/O values obtained here. However, our O/H estimation -- independent of N/O-- is significantly lower, thus suggesting a more complex mixing process of the gas affecting their chemical composition.

We show in Figure fig:MZR that these deep-diving (U)LIRGs have the highest SFR values in our sample ( >100 M _ yr ^ 1 ) and they are in the final stages of their merges. Additionally, we find that the N/O ratios for these four galaxies are similar to the solar ratio, which suggests that they have reached a relatively mature stage of their chemical enrichment history .

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO 350 38 as an SED-identified AGN candidate undetected by NuSTAR, with WISE/PAH evidence for obscuration, estimated N_H=25.3, and possible heavy obscuration or starburst emission.

quotes.quote: SED-identified three AGN candidates Yamada2023 identified, on the basis of SED decomposition, three AGN candidates (ESO 350 38, ESO 374 032, and NGC 4418), which had been observed in X-rays, specifically with NuSTAR in the 8--24 keV band, but not been detected presumably due to insufficient exposures ( 50 ks) (see Section sub2-1-GOALS ). Their WISE color criteria Stern2012 and equivalent widths of the 6.2 m polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon features suggest that they are obscured AGNs e.g., Yamada2021 . Using Equation 7 in Asmus2015 in combination with their 12- m AGN fluxes and observed 2--10 keV fluxes, we estimate their N_ H to be 25.3 1.8 (ESO 350 38), 25.6 1.9 (ESO 374 032), and 26.2 1.9 (NGC 4418).

The 3 upper limits of intrinsic 2--10 keV luminosities are then estimated to be L_ 2-10 ^ (lim) = 42.42 , 42.69, and 41.49, respectively, from the 3 upper limit of their 8--24 keV luminosities under the assumption of N_ H = 25 Yamada2021 . Comparison between their X-ray and bolometric AGN luminosities yields 2--10 keV bolometric correction factors of 20--160. The values are roughly consistent with the typical one among local AGNs with a wide range of _ Edd = - 3--0 Vasudevan2007 . Thus, these three sources are expected to be heavily obscured AGNs with N_ H 25 , or pure starbursts.

sources.summary: The paper resolves Haro 11 LyC emission into Knots B and C, estimating local escape fractions and linking observed LyC strength to young stellar populations and ionizing luminosity.

quotes.quote: As the nearest confirmed Lyman continuum (LyC) emitter, Haro 11 is an exceptional laboratory for studying LyC escape processes crucial to cosmic reionization. Our new HST/COS G130M/1055 observations of its three star-forming knots now reveal that the observed LyC originates in Knots B and C, with 903 - 912 \ luminosities of 1.9 1.5 10^ 40 erg s^ 1 and 0.9 0.7 10^ 40 erg s^ 1 , respectively. We derive local escape fractions = 3.4 2.9 and 5.1 4.3 for Knots B and C, respectively. Our Starburst99 modeling shows dominant populations on the order of 1-4 Myr and 1-2 10^7 in each knot, with the youngest population in Knot B.

Thus, the knot with the strongest LyC detection has the highest LyC production. However, LyC escape is likely less efficient in Knot B than in Knot C due to higher neutral gas covering. Our results therefore stress the importance of the intrinsic ionizing luminosity, and not just the escape fraction, for LyC detection.

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 350-IG38 as a distorted, massive starburst galaxy with multiple nuclei, broad non-Gaussian H lines, slow outer rotation, and merger-triggered activity.

quotes.quote: ESO 350-IG38 ( Haro 11 ) This galaxy has a heart shaped morphology, with three bright starburst nuclei (Fig. contig38 ). The outer morphology is distorted out to very faint isophotal levels (see Fig. contig38 where the faintest visible structures are _R 26 mag/arcsec ^2 ). This is seen more clearly in broad bands that in H demonstrating that the light originates mainly in stars.

These properties indicate that the centre is not dynamically relaxed, while the outer velocity field shows a very slow rotation. The estimated stellar mass density exceeds by far what can be supported by the observed amount of rotation ( 30 km/s). Thus, the galaxy is either not in equilibrium, or it is not primarily supported by rotation. These properties strongly suggest that the starburst was triggered by a merger process.

sources.summary: The paper treats Haro 11 as a local UV-luminous LBG analog with LBG-like far-UV properties, extended soft X-ray emission, and a supernova-driven outflow shock.

quotes.quote: We have re-analyzed data and obtained new observations of , a local ( D_L =88 Mpc) UV luminous galaxy. has a similar far-UV luminosity ( 10^ 10.3 L _ ), UV surface brightness ( 10^ 9.4 L_ kpc^ 2 ), SFR, and metallicity to that observed in Lyman Break Galaxies (LBGs). We show that has extended, soft thermal ( kT 0.68 keV ) X-ray emission with a luminosity and size which scales with the physical properties (e.g. SFR, stellar mass) of the host galaxy. An enhanced ratio of 4 relative to solar abundance suggests significant supernovae enrichment. These results are consistent with the X-ray emission being produced in a shock between a supernovae driven outflow and the ambient material.
This is roughly what is observed in Haro 11.

sources.summary: The paper includes Haro 11 as a Lyman-break analog whose extended X-ray emission, weak nuclear contribution, and Spitzer line diagnostics argue against significant AGN power.

quotes.quote: Benefitting from the high angular resolution capability of , three of the six LBAs in our sample, VV 114, Haro 11, and J082355.0 + 280621.8 appear to have extended X-ray emission (see Figure fig:LBA3 ), and we can distinguish multiple X-ray point sources, all of which are luminous enough to qualify as ULXs ( >10^ 40 ). The nuclear regions, potentially associated with AGN, contribute only < 15 to the 2--10 keV emission in each case, immediately indicating that the elevated /SFR for those LBAs cannot be attributed to AGN activity. While VV114 may harbor an obscured AGN (in the Eastern component, VV114E), Grimes07 conclude that star formation dominates the 2--10 keV power in this galaxy. Based on Spitzer /IRS spectroscopic analysis of the [ OIV ] 25.9 m and [ NeV ] 14.3 m emission features, Cormier2012 conclude that Haro 11 does not contain an AGN.

In Figure fig:LBA3 , the images of extended X-ray emission in three LBAs (VV 114, Haro 11 and J082355.0 + 280621.8) display multiple off-nuclear point sources with 10^ 40 , presumed to be ULXs, associated with each galaxy. Based on their SFRs and the analysis of HMXBs in star-forming galaxies by , we would have expected fewer ULXs with >10^ 40 in these sources than what we observe (five, three and three off-nuclear sources with >10^ 40 in VV 114, Haro 11and J082355.0 + 280621.8, respectively).

sources.summary: The review describes Haro 11 as a local luminous blue compact galaxy whose Ly-alpha emission emerges mainly from knot C and a surrounding scattered halo.

quotes.quote: Resolved Analyses: A Case Study of Haro 11 Local luminous BCG \ is a Lyman break analog e.g. Grimes2007 , and emits \ with a total EW of 15 . As shown in Figure fig:h11 , it comprises three main star-forming knots (labeled in the FUV image), all of which are bright in the UV and , but only one of these condensations emits \ Hayes2007 . Following this breakdown of the galaxy, knots A and B (the west-most two) are by far the brightest in \ and must produce the bulk of the \ radiation, but both absorb at . In contrast it is only the single easterly knot C, which is the faintest of the three in \ but brightest in the UV, that locally emits its .

\ also emits a halo of \ emission, centred around knot C, which can most easily be explained if \ is re-radiated after scattering in the surrounding neutral gas. Diffuse emission also surrounds the two easterly knots, but at the positions of the clusters absorption outweighs the emission,

sources.summary: The paper uses Haro 11 in its low-metallicity sample, adopting published metallicities, scaled CO measurements, dust-based gas estimates, and a power-law molecular-gas model.

quotes.quote: Guseva12 estimated the value of \ in the two H II regions, Haro 11B and Haro 11C as 8.1 and 8.33, respectively hence, we adopt the average value for Haro 11.

We adopted the literature values for ^ 12 CO(1--0) line intensities for CGCG 007-025 and N66, while for Haro 11, UM 311 and Hubble V region ^ 12 CO(3--2) line intensities were scaled using the relation I_ CO(3-2) /I_ CO(1-0) = 0.60 (in temperature units) due to unavailability of ^ 12 CO(1--0) line intensities. Calculating L _ CO , (area integrated luminosity) the molecular gas masses are estimated using _ CO,Gal and were further scaled using the 8 map, to account for the difference in the extracted IRS spectrum and the CO beam regions for each galaxy.

The flux of \ rotational lines for Haro 11 are from Cormier14 , while for UM 311, and CGCG 007-025 Hunt10 measured the \ rotational line fluxes. For Hubble V region, Roussel07 derived the flux of \ rotational lines. Figure fig:Haro is an excitation diagram for low metallcity galaxy Haro 11, with the power law model fit. Our model prediction for the unobserved S(0) line is included, and is consistent with the estimated upper limit.

sources.summary: Haro 11 is treated as a blue compact merger with clumps visible at high redshift and little detectable neutral or molecular gas around its main clumps.

quotes.quote: For this paper, we used multi-wavelength observations of 7 merging systems in the survey: Arp 220, ESO 185--IG13, Haro 11 (ESO 350--IG038), NGC 1614, NGC 2623, NGC 3256, and NGC 3690. Arp 220 is an Ultraluminous Infrared Galaxy (ULIRG), NGC 1614, NGC 2623, NGC 3256, and NGC 3690 (Arp 299) are LIRGS, and ESO 185--IG13 and Haro 11 are blue compact galaxies. Their distances range from 38 to 90 Mpc.
Haro 11, for example, has the appearance of a clumpy galaxy at higher z; NGC 2623 would just be a double galaxy at z=2.

Haro 11, a low metallicity BCD, has no detectable HI or CO, with upper limits of 10^8 M_ for each component berg . Its ionized and diffuse gas component is 5.8 10^8 M_ based on mid- and far-infrared fine-structure cooling lines observed with the Spitzer Infrared Spectrograph (IRS) and Herschel Photodetector Array Camera and Spectrometer (PACS) by corm , who describe the photodissociation region and compact HII regions associated with the three main clumps.

sources.summary: The paper says ESO 350-IG038 has Ly emission on a broad structure, complicated velocity components, and a nonstandard profile with damped absorption extending redward.

quotes.quote: Most spectra show Ly \ emission with a broad absorption on their blue side except for ESO 350-IG038 in which the emission is seen atop of a broad structure requiring several filaments and T1214-277 that shows pure emission. When metallic lines are detected, they are always blueshifted with respect to the ionized gas, further supporting the interpretation. In the case of ESO 350-IG038 the velocity structure seems to be more complicated and several components at different velocities are identified on the metallic lines.

This will produce the fast moving layer of HI at the superbubble shell, here thought to be responsible for the partial absorption observed in sources such as Haro 2, ESO B400-G043 (which probably exhibits a secondary blueshifted \ emission) and ESO 350-IG038 ( ). In the latter case however, the profile is not typical of a clear P Cygni one. Instead the underlying damped \ absorption extends beyond the red of the line emission.

sources.summary: The paper presents deeper V and K photometry of Haro 11, revising its host color to V-K=2.3 and removing the need for an anomalous stellar population.

quotes.quote: We have presented deep new optical/near-IR data of the BCG Haro 11 and performed surface photometry on the faint outskirts of the underlying LSB host galaxy. We have obtained a total color of V-K=2.3 0.2 for the host over the range =5 - 10 kpc. This is 2.3 off from the previously published measurement of V-K=4.2 0.8 , which was measured out to =12.3 kpc. Increasing the radial range of the total color measurement out to =12.3 kpc gives V-K=2.4 0.5 which has a 2 discrepancy to previous results. The new color places Haro 11 on the blue end of the V-K range occupied by the other BCGs in the sample and reconciles the LSB host of Haro 11 with a metal-poor ( Z=0.001 ) stellar population with a standard Salpeter IMF.

sources.summary: The paper treats Haro 11 as a Herschel-sample galaxy with unusually high molecular-to-atomic gas ratio and a sub-mm excess plausibly produced by free-free emission.

quotes.quote: The mass of molecular hydrogen M_ H2 for three galaxies from the Herschel sample with detected CO emission (Haro 11, Mrk 1089, Mrk 930) was taken from Co14 and added to the H  i mass. The M_ H2 / M_ HI ratios for Mrk 1089 and Mrk 930 are low, of 0.2 -- 0.3. However, this ratio of 5 is much higher for Haro 11.

Regarding Haro 11 and II Zw 40, _ ff are 700 m and 960 m, respectively (Figs. fig6 a1 and fig6 b1). Therefore, a sub-mm excess in Haro 11 due to the free-free emission is expected at shorter wavelengths, and it is seen by Herschel at 500 m, while it is not in II Zw 40.

sources.summary: The paper compares Haro 11 with Tol1247 as a nearby LyC-emitting galaxy with luminous Chandra X-ray sources and similar young clusters and high central SFR surface density.

quotes.quote: There are only three known nearby (closer than 1000 Mpc) galaxies from which LyC emission has been directly detected: Haro 11 Bergvall2006 , Tololo 1247-232 hereafter Tol1247 Leitet2013 , and Mrk 54 Leitherer2016 . Remarkably, the two that have been observed in X-rays are very luminous with L_X 10^ 41 erg/s in the 0.5--10 keV band for both Tol1247 Rosa2009 and Haro 11 Prestwich2015 . High resolution Chandra imaging of Haro 11 shows diffuse thermal emission ( kT 0.7 keV) and two bright point sources: one with a luminosity of 10^ 41 erg/s and a very hard spectrum ( = 1.2 0.3 ) located in a star-forming knot and second with a luminosity of 5 10^ 40 erg/s and a softer spectrum ( 2.2 ) located 3 arcseconds away in another star-forming knot Prestwich2015 .

The presence of one or more highly luminous accreting sources and the similarity of the X-ray emission from Tol1247 to that found from Haro 11 Prestwich2015 , the only other LyC-emitting galaxy that has been resolved in X-rays, may suggest a relation between accreting sources and LyC escape. Many properties of Tol1247 and Haro 11 are similar, including the young age of their star clusters and their high central SFR surface density.

sources.summary: The paper describes Haro 11 as a nearby LyC emitter with luminous X-ray emission, two X-ray-bright star-forming regions, and BPT-region evidence near composite AGN/star-forming values.

quotes.quote: Haro 11 is the first galaxy from which LyC emission was detected, using the Far Ultraviolet Spectroscopic Explorer (FUSE) Bergvall2006 . Haro 11 and Tol1247 are known X-ray emitters. Haro 11 has a total X-ray luminosity 10^ 41 erg s^ 1 and contains two X-ray emitting regions, each coincident with a star-forming region Grimes2007,Prestwich2015 . Haro 11 contains two X-ray bright regions and there is evidence for multiple sources in each Gross2021 . Spatially-resolved emission line measurements of the regions surrounding the X-ray sources in Haro 11 lie at the border of the composite AGN/starforming region of the BPT diagram, while the full galaxy lies well within the starforming region Gross2021 .

sources.summary: The paper revisits Haro11 as a high-SFR blue compact dwarf with three bright knots and limited dust, deriving a lower young-cluster formation fraction than earlier work.

quotes.quote: The three blue compact dwarf (BCD) galaxies ESO185-IG13, ESO338-IG04, and Haro11 have played a central role on the observational side because they have some of the highest known _ SFR and published values of . We present new estimates of for these BCDs in three age intervals (1-10 Myr, 10-100 Myr, 100-400 Myr), based on age-dating which includes H photometry to better discriminate between clusters younger and older than 10 Myr.
Haro11, which has three bright 'knots' (labeled A, B, C), is the only one of the three galaxies to show limited dusty regions.

The fraction of stars formed in 1-10 Myr clusters, or _ 1-10 Myr , was previously estimated to be 50 for the BCD galaxies ESO338 and Haro11, and 26 for ESO185 Adamo10, Adamo11a . In this work, we found significantly lower values of 15 4 (ESO185), 39 10 (ESO338), and 18 5 (Haro11), based on deeper HST observations which include narrow-band H imaging taken as part of the Clusters, Clumps, Dust and Gas (CCDG) survey.

sources.summary: The paper uses Haro11 as a good-quality dwarf-galaxy [CII] spectrum, finding high-velocity wings and a broad component consistent with outflowing gas.

quotes.quote: For comparison, we also show at the bottom of Fig. fig:bad_spec , the [ CII ] spectra of Haro11, one of the 29 sources of our sample. Contrarily to HS1319+3224, this represents an example of the good quality of our data, that was not possible to reach for the above-mentioned excluded DGS galaxies. Haro11 is placed at the center of the PACS footprint, where the signal is larger than in the outskirts. The 3 3 inner spectrum resulted to be the one with the largest flux and S/N (see Table tab:technical ) and we used it to search for broad wings in its high-velocity tails, clearly visible in the figure as a flux excess with respect to the single Gaussian component fitted to the emission profile.

On the other hand, the middle panel reports the [ CII ] spectrum of Haro11 (the same spectrum is also shown in the bottom panel of Fig. fig:bad_spec as a green line), in which the presence of the broad component is clearly evidenced by the large residuals at 400 km s^ 1 , obtained with a single-component Gaussian fit of the line.

sources.summary: The paper uses Haro 11 as a local LBG-like comparison galaxy, describing it as a knotty blue compact dwarf in merger, whose redshifted UV morphology is dominated by bright clustered nuclei.

quotes.quote: The two local comparison objects are Haro 11 and VV 114, both of which lie at z=0.02 for details see and references therein knop94,scoville00,goldader02,bergvall02,kunth03,grimes06,grimes07 . Haro 11 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy consisting of a number of UV bright knots believed to be in the process of merging. In this paper, we make use of an ACS/HRC image of Haro 11 taken through the filter F220W ( _c 2255 ) obtained from the HST archive (Program 10575, PI: G oran Ostlin). Both Haro 11 and VV 114 possess multiple bright nuclei ( 1 \ apart) when observed in \ at z=0.15 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Haro 11 as the highest-SFR DGS object and a luminous infrared outlier whose warm, short-wavelength SED makes MIPS 24 microns overestimate the SFR.

quotes.quote: The extension of the subsample of spatially resolved galaxies to the entire DGS sample broadens the range covered in metallicity from 0.03 Z _ (I Zw 18) to 0.5 Z _ (HS 2352+2733) and in SFR from 0.001 M _ yr ^ 1 (UGC 4483) to 43 M _ yr ^ 1 (Haro 11) as traced by FUV +24 m.

The best fitting line and dispersion (0.28 dex) in this plot is mainly dominated by three galaxies (SBS 0335-052, Tol 1214-277, Haro 11) which have the peak of their SED at very short wavelengths and, therefore, the 24 m band will overestimate the SFR while the 70 m emission will underestimate the SFR.

Another outlier is Haro 11, which shows the highest ratio based on the composite tracer FUV +24 m. We believe that the warmer dust temperatures with a peak in its SED at very short wavelengths ( 40 m, 2009A&A...508..645G ) in this Luminous InfraRed Galaxy (LIRG) causes an overestimation of the SFR based on MIPS 24 m.

sources.summary: The paper treats Haro 11 as a local LyC-emitting candidate, noting its FUSE-based detection, COS data handling, and relatively extended outflow-profile behavior.

quotes.quote: We acquired the restframe UV spectra of the leaker sample from four recent COS studies that measured the LyC escape fraction: five from HST proposal ID 13744 Izotov16, Izotov16b , two from HST proposal ID 13325 Leitherer16 , one from HST proposal ID 12886 Borthakur14 , and Haro 11 from HST proposal ID 13017 Alexandroff15, Heckman15 . However, the LyC detection for Haro 11 comes from FUSE observations Leitet13 , and the has not been confirmed with COS.
In addition to the eight LyC leakers detected by COS, we include Haro 11, which has a measured by FUSE leitet11, Leitet13 , in the sample as a LyC emitting candidate.
Correspondingly, Haro 11, J0921 + 4509, and Tol 1247 381 are all fairly extended and have / ratios above 2.5, while the more compact galaxies have smaller / ratios.

sources.summary: The paper treats Haro 11 as a LyC source with a hard X-ray point source, arguing that its ionizing radiation cannot be produced entirely by the central AGN.

quotes.quote: Another LyC source, Haro 11 with f_ esc,abs 3 (Leitet et al. 2011), has been detected in X-ray as a bright point source with a very hard spectrum (Prestwich et al. 2015).

The total UV absolute magnitudes of Tol 1247-232 and Haro 11 are 20/-21 , which translate into effective magnitudes of 15/-16 at 900 rest frame, given the observed values for their LyC escape fraction. Starting from the X-ray luminosity of Lx 10^ 41 erg/s measured by Kaaret et al. (2017) for their central sources, and assuming that the optical luminosities are scaling as Lx L_ UV ^ 0.6 (Lusso Risaliti 2016), we derive an absolute magnitude of M_ 1450 12 for the central AGNs in Tol 1247-232 and Haro 11. This implies that the ionizing radiation by these two galaxies cannot be entirely produced by the central engines, even assuming an escape fraction of 100 for the central AGN, but it is possibly emitted also by the surrounding stars, once the AGN has cleaned the surrounding ISM.

sources.summary: The paper describes Haro 11 as a starburst interacting Ly-alpha emitter with a bright compact X-ray source, spatial offsets between Ly-alpha and X-rays, and a possible LLAGN candidate in Haro 11-B.

quotes.quote: For example, the starburst interacting galaxy system Haro 11 is an LAE with a bright, hard, compact X-ray source coinciding with the galaxy's only strong \ source. This X-ray emission appears to be due to an ultra-luminous X-ray source ULX; prestwich15 .

In Haro 11, the LAE Haro 11-C does not correspond to the dominant X-ray source, Haro 11-B (Figure f_img1 a; Table chartable ). The former is a ULX, while the latter could be a faint LLAGN ( L_X = 1.1 10^ 41 \ ) prestwich15 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Haro 11 as a well-studied LyC system and finds its model probability for high escape fraction is low, consistent with the directly observed 3.3 percent escape fraction.

quotes.quote: One of the more well studied galaxies in the context of escaping LyC photons is Haro 11. Our model is fairly confident that Haro 11 has f_ esc <10 , assigning it a 22 probability of having f_ esc >10 . Interestingly, LyC radiation has been directly observed for this system and the estimate is that f_ esc =3.3 Leitet2011 , confirming the prediction from our model. The model similarly predicts minimal Lyman continuum leakage in both Haro 2 and also Haro 3.

sources.summary: The paper notes that Haro 11 had a reported Lyman-continuum detection implying a 1-10 percent escape fraction, but that a later analysis challenged the detection.

quotes.quote: Bergvall \ (2006) have reported the first positive detection of Lyman continuum photons in the spectra of the starburst galaxy Haro 11 using the Far-UV Space Explorer . Their analysis suggests an escape fraction in this galaxy of =1-10 . This finding has, however, been challenged by Grimes \ (2007), who cannot confirm the detection of Lyman continuum in the same data set.

sources.summary: The paper describes Haro 11 as a clumpy luminous blue compact dwarf Lyman break analog whose high-redshift UV appearance resembles double-nucleated galaxies.

quotes.quote: An example of a particularly clumpy LBA is the luminous blue compact dwarf galaxy Haro 11 at z=0.02 ostlin09 , which lies close to the edge of our LBA selection criteria based on FUV luminosity and surface brightness grimes07 . It is similar to the LBAs in most aspects but with a relatively high degree of clumpiness due to three strong light concentrations in an otherwise amorphous, forming galaxy. Its UV morphology at high redshift is similar to that of double nucleated galaxies identified on the basis of a large M_ 20 or even a ``by eye'' classification overzier08 .

sources.summary: Haro 11 is treated as a luminous blue compact host whose starburst began within 30-40 Myr; its optically bright young clusters include partially embedded systems with near-infrared excess.

quotes.quote: The age distributions of the YSCs suggest that the starburst episode in Haro 11, ESO 185-IG13, and Mrk 930 started not more than 30-40 Myr ago.

In all the three host galaxies, the analysis is limited to the optically brightest objects, i.e., systems that are only partially embedded by their natal cocoons (since deeply embedded clusters are probably too faint to be detected).

A significant fraction of young star clusters in Haro 11 2010MNRAS.407..870A , ESO 185-IG13 2011MNRAS.414.1793A , and Mrk 930 2011MNRAS.tmp..739A shows a clear signature of a flux excess at near-IR wavebands.

sources.summary: Haro11 is cited as a nearby ionizing-radiation emitter: a blue, metal-poor galaxy with young star-forming clumps and an estimated 17 percent LyC escape fraction.

quotes.quote: One is Haro11, a blue compact and metal poor (oxygen abundance 12+log(O/H)=7.9) galaxy at z=0.02 , characterized by M(HI) <10^8 M _ Bergvall2006 . It is composed of three main young ( <40 Myr old) star-formation clumps, probably experiencing a merging event. Leitet2011 estimated that 17 of the intrinsic LyC flux escapes Haro11 see also for the method Bergvall2013 .

sources.summary: The CLASSY notes identify J0036-3333 with Haro 11, describe multiple stellar populations, and state that the coadded spectrum focuses on UV-luminous knot C.

quotes.quote: J0036-3333 : Also commonly known as the galaxy Haro 11, which contains multiple stellar populations. The CLASSY coadded spectrum only focuses on knot C vader93 . Knot C is the most UV luminous source and main contributor to the Lyman alpha emission from Haro 11 hayes07 .

sources.summary: The paper cites Haro 11 as a complex local LyC leaker with three clumps, uneven LyC escape, strong Ly emission, and a velocity field suggesting a possible merger.

quotes.quote: A prominent example of a local LyC leaker with a complex morphology is Haro 11 (e.g. Bergvall2006,Keenan2017,Rivera-Thorsen2017 ), which has three distinct clumps, two of which show no LyC escape. However, all three show strong Ly emission, with the highest Ly escape coming from the clump which is likely to be the one leaking the LyC emission as well.
