Fits of single-component spectral energy distribution (SED) models to the photometry result in a moderately young age, t=80 40 Myr, small amounts of dust, E(B-V) 0.11 , and an exponentially declining star formation history with e folding time = 10-50 Myr. After correcting for the lensing magnification, we find a stellar mass of M _* 10^ 10 M _ and a current star formation rate SFR 77 M _ yr ^ 1 .

RCSGA 032727-132609 is typical of the known population of star-forming galaxies near this redshift in terms of its age and stellar mass. Its large magnification and spatial extent provide a unique opportunity to study the physical properties of an individual high-redshift star-forming galaxy in great detail, opening up a new window to the process of galaxy evolution between z=1.7 and our local Universe.

sources.summary: The paper studies RCSGA 032727-132609 as a strongly lensed z=1.70 starburst resolved to 100 pc, finding disturbed kinematics, a tidal tail, clump outflows, and evidence for an equal-mass mixed major merger.

quotes.quote: Ellis Av., Chicago, IL 60637 4 Kavli Institute for Cosmological Physics, University of Chicago, 5640 South Ellis Avenue, Chicago, IL 60637 5 Department of Astronomy and Astrophysics, University of Michigan We present a detailed analysis of multi-wavelength HST/WFC3 imaging and Keck/OSIRIS near-IR AO-assisted integral field spectroscopy for a highly magnified lensed galaxy at z=1.70 . This young starburst is representative of UV-selected star-forming galaxies (SFG) at z 2 and contains multiple individual star-forming regions. Due to the lensing magnification, we can resolve spatial scales down to 100 pc in the source plane of the galaxy. The velocity field shows disturbed kinematics suggestive of an ongoing interaction, and there is a clear signature of a tidal tail.

With strong star formation driven outflows in four clumps, RCSGA0327 is the first high redshift SFG at stellar mass <10^ 10 M _ with spatially resolved stellar winds. We compare the H luminosities, sizes and dispersions of the star-forming regions to other high-z clumps as well as local giant H II regions and find no evidence for increased clump star formation surface densities in interacting systems, unlike in the local Universe. Spatially resolved SED modelling unveils an established stellar population at the location of the largest clump and a second mass concentration near the edge of the system which is not detected in H emission. This suggests a picture of an equal-mass mixed major merger, which has not triggered a new burst of star formation or caused a tidal tail in the gas-poor component. galaxies: high-redshift, strong gravitational lensing, galaxies: kinematics and dynamics, galaxies: structure High redshift star-forming galaxies (SFG) show increasingly irregular and clumpy morphologies compared to the local Universe.

sources.summary: The paper images RCSGA 032727-132609 as a lensed low-metallicity star-forming galaxy at z 1.703, finding patchy, asymmetric resonant emission from an outflow extending to about 39 kpc.

quotes.quote: We image the spatial extent of a cool galactic outflow with resonant \ emission in a gravitationally lensed star-forming galaxy at z 1.7 using VLT/MUSE observations. We observe \ residual (continuum-subtracted) emission out to an observed radial distance of 26.5_ 0.4 ^ +0.5 kpc from the galaxy, with an observed maximum spatial extent of 39_ 0.6 ^ +0.8 kpc ( 30_ 0.5 ^ +0.7 kpc after correcting for seeing). \ residual emission is patchy and covers a total area of 184_ 10 ^ +5 kpc^2 , constraining the minimum area covered by the outflowing gas to be 13.27_ 1.02 ^ +0.55 of the total area. The spatial extent of the \ emission is asymmetric and shows an observed 27.6_ 0.7 ^ +0.8 ( 20.9_ 0.6 ^ +0.7 after seeing correction) larger extent along the declination direction.

The galaxy RCSGA 032727-132609 is a low-metallicity star-forming galaxy at z 1.703 gonzalez2017alma,rigby2018magellanI and is lensed by the galaxy cluster RCS2 032727-132623 at z = 0.564 wuyts2010bright, wuyts2014magnified . It was discovered in the Second Red Sequence Cluster Survey gilbank2011red . The apparent shape of the galaxy consists of the main arc north of the cluster subtending 38'' and a smaller counter arc south of the cluster subtending 7'' on the sky see fig:muse_hst ; wuyts2014magnified . The main arc consists of three images of the galaxy (images 1, 2, and 3 are denoted by yellow rectangles and yellow numbers in fig:muse_hst ) and the counter arc is a 4^ th image.

sources.summary: The paper treats RCSGA 032727-132609 as a bright strongly lensed high-redshift galaxy whose SED and spectra indicate a young, lightly reddened, actively star-forming stellar population.

quotes.quote: We present a comprehensive analysis of the rest-frame UV to near-IR spectral energy distributions and rest-frame optical spectra of four of the brightest gravitationally lensed galaxies in the literature: RCSGA 032727-132609 at z=1.70 , MS1512-cB58 at z=2.73 , SGAS J152745.1+065219 at z=2.76 and SGAS J122651.3+215220 at z=2.92 . This includes new Spitzer imaging for RCSGA0327 as well as new spectra, near-IR imaging and Spitzer imaging for SGAS1527 and SGAS1226. Lensing magnifications of 3-4 magnitudes allow a detailed study of the stellar populations and physical conditions.

We compare star formation rates as measured from the SED fit, the H and [ O II ] 3727 emission lines, and the UV+IR bolometric luminosity where 24 m photometry is available. The SFR estimate from the SED fit is consistently higher than the other indicators, which suggests that the Calzetti dust extinction law used in the SED fitting is too flat for young star-forming galaxies at z 2 . Our analysis finds similar stellar population parameters for all four lensed galaxies: stellar masses 3-7 10^9 M _ , young ages 100 Myr, little dust content E(B-V) =0.10-0.25, and star formation rates around 20-100 M _ yr ^ 1 . Compared to typical values for the galaxy population at z 2 , this suggests we are looking at newly formed, starbursting systems that have only recently started the build-up of stellar mass.

sources.summary: The paper describes RCSGA 032727-132609 as a bright z=1.7037 lensed galaxy with strong magnification, measured stellar mass, high SFR estimates, no AGN contribution, and metallicity measurements.

quotes.quote: RCSGA 032727-132609 is a bright lensed galaxy (at the time of its discovery, it was the most luminous lensed galaxy ever known) at redshift z=1.7037 , magnified by a factor =17.2 1.4 by RCS2 032727-132623 galaxy cluster at z=0.564 . From spectral energy distribution (SED) fitting, the stellar mass was found to be 2 10^ 10 M_ Wuyts10 . Different methods give SFR values between 130 M_ yr^ 1 Wuyts10 and 360 M_ yr^ 1 Rigby11 . As noted in Rigby11 , the highest SFR value is to be regarded as an upper limit. The BPT diagram for this object is consistent with no AGN contribution.
The abundances in Table t:abundance_data are taken from Keck II/NIRSPEC emission spectrum Rigby11 . The (O/H)+12 values reported in Table t:abundance_data refer to the direct, R_ 23 and N_ 2 methods.

sources.summary: The paper studies RCSGA032727-132609 as a strongly lensed star-forming galaxy with spatially varying cool outflows whose energetics suggest energy-driven feedback.

quotes.quote: In this work, we study the spatial variation of the cool ( T 10^4 K) outflows in the strong gravitationally lensed galaxy RCSGA032727-132609 (hereafter referred to as ) at z= 1.70347 wuyts2010bright, wuyts2014magnified, Shaban202230kpc . In Shaban202230kpc , (hereafter Shaban202230kpc ), we presented the spatial extent of the outflowing gas in this galaxy using resonant emission maps. In this work, we present the spatially resolved outflows kinematics and gas properties from blueshifted and absorption lines.

The outflow velocities from different regions of the same galaxy vary by 80 km s ^ 1 , which is comparable to the variation seen in a large sample of star-burst galaxies in the local Universe. Using multiply lensed images of , we probe the same star-forming region at different spatial scales (0.5 kpc ^2 –- 25 kpc ^2 ), we find that outflow velocities vary between 120 to 242 km s ^ 1 , and the mass outflow rates vary between 37 to 254 . The outflow momentum flux in this galaxy is 100 of the momentum flux provided by star-formation in individual regions, and outflow energy flux is 10 of the total energy flux provided by star-formation. These estimates suggest that the outflow in is energy driven.

sources.summary: The paper uses the lensed z=1.7 galaxy RCSGA 032727-132609 to map spatially resolved outflows from star-forming knots and infer locally sourced galactic winds.

quotes.quote: In this work, we perform the first spatially-resolved down-the-barrel study of galactic outflows using the lensed, z = 1.7 spatially resolved galaxy RCSGA 032727-132609 as the background source Wuyts2010 . We observe the and absorption and emission lines for four spatially resolved star-forming knots in this galaxy. These knots are physically separated by 2 to 6 kpc Wuyts2014 .

Almost 20 to 50 of the blueshifted absorption probably escapes the gravitational potential of the galaxy. In this galaxy, the outflow is ``locally sourced'', that is, the properties of the outflow in each line of sight are dominated by the properties of the nearest clump of star formation; the wind is not global to the galaxy. The mass outflow rates and the momentum flux carried out by outflows in individual star forming knots of this object are comparable to that of starburst galaxies in the local Universe.

sources.summary: The paper treats RCS0327 as a precisely measured lensed z=1.70 galaxy, reporting its ionization parameter, stellar mass, star-formation rate, elevated sSFR, and relatively high metallicity.

quotes.quote: Since the physical conditions of the lensed galaxy RCS0327 at z=1.70 have been measured precisely, we compare them to the derived physical parameters of the Stark:2014fa sample. RCS0327 has a measured ionization parameter U = -2.84 0.06 ( Rigby:2011il , using the average extinction from Whitaker:2014jy .)

RCS0327 has a measured stellar mass of 6.3 0.7 10^9 , and a star formation rate of 30--50 yr ^ 1 Wuyts:2012ej . Its specific star formation rate (sSFR) is 5 10^ 9 yr ^ 1 , such that it lies a factor of 3 above the correlation between star formation rate and stellar mass at that redshift.
Thus, RCS0327 is similar to the Stark:2014fa sample in terms of sSFR, but has a higher metallicity and lower ionization parameter than inferred for that sample.

sources.summary: The paper identifies RCS0327 as a very bright z=1.7 lensed galaxy and uses Keck/NIRSPEC spectroscopy to determine physical conditions of star formation at z~2.

quotes.quote: Among these new discoveries is , hereafter RCS0327, at z = 1.7 wuyts , discovered from the second Red Sequence Cluster Survey (RCS2; Gilbank \ in prep.). With an integrated g band magnitude of 19.15, we believe \ to be the brightest high-redshift lensed galaxy yet found. wuyts present the discovery, spectroscopic confirmation, deep-follow-up imaging, spectral energy distribution modeling, and preliminary lensing analysis. In this paper, using 1.3 hr of integration with NIRSPEC on Keck, we determine with unprecedented precision the physical conditions of star formation in this hopefully--typical galaxy at the crucial epoch of z 2.

sources.summary: The paper describes RCS0327 as a merging lensed galaxy with multiple knots, using Knot E for redshift and Mg II-profile analysis because it has the best signal-to-noise.

quotes.quote: RCS0327 has a complex velocity structure because it is undergoing a major merger Wuyts14 . For Knot E of RCS0327 the C III ] emission is blended with Fe II 2600 absorption from an intervening system at z = 0.98295; we therefore instead use Si II* 1264, Si II* 1309, Si II* 1533, and Si III ]  1882, finding a weighted-average emission line redshift of 1.7034 0.00014 for Knot E.

While the five galaxies in this paper were merely selected from our larger sample as spectral coverage of Mg II and , in fact, all five show detected Mg II emission and absorption in a P Cygni profile.
In each galaxy, the Mg II emission is broader than the C III ] and [ O II ] lines, and the peak is redshifted with respect to systemic by 100 to 200 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00001584.name: NGC 337A
gmw_00001584.overview: NGC 337A is classified as an irregular galaxy [arXiv:1801.07750]. It features a sparsely sampled velocity map, yet its rotation curve can still be modeled out to a radius of 2.5 kpc [arXiv:1801.07750].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 337A as an irregular RINGS galaxy with a sparse velocity map, measurable rotation curve, and photometric-kinematic projection mismatch.

quotes.quote: NGC 337A has one of the most sparsely sampled velocity maps in the RINGS medium-resolution \ kinematic data, as seen in Figure fig:N337A . It is also one of the two galaxies in this work (along with NGC 4517A) that are classified as Irregular. Despite this, our model is able to sample the rotation curve over a wide range of radii (Figure fig:N337A ) extending out to 2.5 kpc.

gmw_00001585.name: NGC 578
gmw_00001585.overview: NGC 578 is a barred spiral galaxy that features one of the strongest visible bars in its sample [arXiv:1801.07750]. However, its bar region lacks sufficient velocity data, requiring an axisymmetric fit to estimate its rotation curve [arXiv:1801.07750].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 578 as a strongly barred RINGS galaxy whose sparse bar-region velocity data require an axisymmetric rotation-curve fit.

quotes.quote: Even though NGC 578 exhibits one of the strongest visible bars among this sample of galaxies, we were disappointed to find that the velocity map (Figure fig:N578 ) lacks sufficient data in the bar region to be able to separate a non-circular flow from the axisymmetric part. Note the absence of velocity information immediately to the N and S of the bar. We therefore derive an estimate of the rotation curve from an axisymmetric fit only.

gmw_00001586.name: NGC 908
gmw_00001586.overview: NGC 908 is a spiral galaxy characterized by a single large spiral arm on its northeastern side [arXiv:1801.07750]. This asymmetric structure is unmatched on the opposite side and may be responsible for a warped disk at large radii [arXiv:1801.07750].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 908 has an asymmetric large spiral arm, correlated residual velocities, and a rotation-curve rise possibly caused by that asymmetry or a warped disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 908 has a single large spiral arm towards the north-east side of the galaxy (see the top left panel of Figure fig:N908 ) which is unmatched by a corresponding spiral arm on the opposite side. We have fitted an axisymmetric model, which therefore leads to a corresponding region of large correlated residual velocity. This feature is probably responsible for the sudden increase in the derived rotation curve beyond 120 , which could also be indicative of a warped disk at large radii.

gmw_00001587.name: NGC 1325
gmw_00001587.overview: NGC 1325 is an SAbc spiral galaxy that forms a pair with NGC 1325A within the NGC 1332 group [arXiv:2108.04410]. It exhibits a regular projected flow pattern with a flat rotation curve [arXiv:1801.07750] and shows evidence of interaction with gas extending from ESO 548-G011 [arXiv:2108.04410].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1325 as SN 2021yja's host, notes a previous supernova there, and adopts host redshift and distance information for the supernova analysis.

quotes.quote: Its J2000 coordinates, as measured by Gaia Photometric Science Alerts, are = 03 h 24 m 21 180 , = -21 33'56 20 hodgkin_gaiaalerts_2021 , 99'' southwest of the center of its host galaxy, NGC 1325. NGC 1325 previously hosted SN 1975S, which was not spectroscopically classified wegner_photometric_1977 .

NGC 1325 has a heliocentric redshift of z = 0.005307 0.000005 springob_digital_2005 . However, we observe a weak, narrow H emission line in our Keck HIRES spectrum at a redshift of z = 0.00568 , corresponding to a line-of-sight velocity of z = +112 \ km \ s ^ 1 with respect to the galaxy core. This velocity is reasonable given the position of the SN and a typical galaxy rotation curve falcon-barroso_stellar_2017,guidi_selgifs_2018 , so we adopt this as the SN redshift.

Distance estimates for NGC 1325 based on the method of listed on the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database range from 17.7 to 26.1 Mpc ( =31.24 32.09 mag). For comparison, the redshift-dependent distance of NGC 1325 is 23.6 4.5 Mpc, assuming the cosmological parameters of the planckcollaboration_planck_2020 and an uncertainty of cz 300 \ km \ s ^ 1 due to the galaxy's peculiar velocity.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 1325 has a regular projected flow pattern, a broadly flat rotation curve, and sparse uncertain central velocity estimates.

quotes.quote: Our data on NGC 1325 (Figure fig:N1325 ) indicate that this galaxy has a regular projected flow pattern. We derive a rotation curve that is approximately flat over a wide range of radii. Notably, we detect very little \ emission in the innermost 25 \ of the map, where our velocity estimates are correspondingly sparse and uncertain.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1325 as an SAbc galaxy paired with NGC 1325A and notes interaction evidence from gas extending toward NGC 1325.

quotes.quote: Within the NGC 1332 group, NGC 1325 ( 38) is a SAbc galaxy that forms a pair along with NGC 1325A. There is no \ detection of NGC 1325A in our study but there is in W15. Evidence of interaction is seen with gas extending from the north corner of LSB galaxy, ESO 548-G011 ( 39), towards NGC 1325.

gmw_00001588.name: NGC 1964
gmw_00001588.overview: NGC 1964 is a spiral galaxy characterized by an almost regular flow pattern [arXiv:1801.07750]. Its rotation curve is consistent with previous measurements, and its disk has an estimated inclination of 74 degrees [arXiv:1801.07750].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1964 as having an almost regular flow pattern, consistent fitted projection parameters, and a rotation curve similar to previous measurements.

quotes.quote: We find, Figure fig:N1964 , an almost regular flow pattern for NGC 1964. Our fitted center position and projection angles agree, within the estimated uncertainties (see Figure fig:projcomp ), with those derived from the I band image by RINGSPhot . As shown in Figure fig:rc_comp , our derived rotation curve is similar to that measured previously by 1996ApJS..107...97M , who adopted an inclination of 68 , compared to our 74 .

gmw_00001589.name: NGC 2280
gmw_00001589.overview: NGC 2280 is a disk galaxy that exhibits a regular velocity field typical of a rotating disk seen in projection [arXiv:1801.07750]. However, its kinematic structure is more complex than simple axisymmetric models allow, showing large regions of correlated residuals [arXiv:1511.01066].

sources.summary: The paper presents SALT and VLA velocity-field observations of NGC 2280, finding axisymmetric models inadequate in places but overall agreement with prior rotation measurements.

quotes.quote: Here we present our observations of one of these galaxies, NGC 2280. We also present 21 cm kinematic observations of this galaxy taken with the Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) for the purposes of comparison. Our kinematic observations of NGC 2280 were taken on 1 Nov 2011 and 28 Dec 2011 in the medium-resolution mode of the SALT interferometer. On each of these nights, we obtained 25 one-minute exposures of the galaxy, scanning over the line of excited hydrogen from 6565.5 to 6643.5 in 2 steps.

The software is also capable of fitting for asymmetries such as bars and warps, though we find that allowing such flexibility is unnecessary for NGC 2280; such non-axisymmetric models do not significantly improve the fits. In modeling these data, we have added an additional 8 km/s in quadrature to our uncertainty map to account for the turbulent motions typical of the interstellar medium Gunn79 . After accounting for this additional 8 km/s, the reduced chi-squared value for our best-fitting model is 2.33, indicating a poor formal fit to the data. The left panel of figure fig:resmaps shows a map of data-minus-model residuals for our best-fitting model. This residual map shows large regions (several effective beams across) of correlated residuals with magnitudes larger than our expected uncertainty. This suggests that the kinematic structure of NGC 2280 is more complicated than is permitted by our axisymmetric model.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 2280 as a rotating disk with a regular velocity field and one of the sample's most complete rotation curves.

quotes.quote: Our derived velocity map for NGC 2280, presented in Figure fig:N2280 , again reveals a regular flow pattern that is typical of a rotating disk galaxy seen in projection. Unlike many of the other galaxies in our sample, we have been able to extract reliable velocities at both very small and very large radii, producing one of the most complete rotation curves in this sample. Aside from the inermost point, which is very uncertain, the measured orbital speed agrees well with that in our previous paper, where we also demonstrated general agreeement with the H 1 rotation curve.

gmw_00001590.name: NGC 3705
gmw_00001590.overview: NGC 3705 is a spiral galaxy with a disk structure [arXiv:1801.07750]. The galaxy lacks detectable emission in its central 20 arcseconds, but it exhibits an approximately flat rotation curve over a broad range of outer radii [arXiv:1801.07750].

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 3705, notes missing central emission and uncertain inner velocities, and finds an approximately flat outer rotation curve.

quotes.quote: We have derived the maps shown in Figure fig:N3705 from our data on NGC 3705. We detect no \ emission in the central 20 of the galaxy, and the innermost fitted velocities have large uncertainties. For R 80 , the rotation curve appears to be approximately flat over a broad range of radii.

gmw_00001591.name: NGC 4517A
gmw_00001591.overview: NGC 4517A is morphologically classified as an irregular galaxy [arXiv:1801.07750]. Its velocity map is very sparsely sampled, which contributes to large uncertainties in its axisymmetric rotation curve [arXiv:1801.07750].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4517A as an irregular galaxy with sparse velocity data, an uncertain axisymmetric rotation curve, and inclination-driven uncertainties.

quotes.quote: Our velocity map for NGC 4517A, Figure fig:N4517A , like that for NGC 337A, is very sparsely sampled, and both galaxies are morphologically classified as Irregular. Our rotation curve extracted from an axisymmetric model of this galaxy is sparsely sampled and has large uncertainties. These uncertainties also reflect the uncertainty in the inclination.

gmw_00001592.name: NGC 5364
gmw_00001592.overview: NGC 5364 is an intermediate to late-type grand-design spiral galaxy classified as SA()bc [arXiv:1004.5063]. It features an off-center inner ring [arXiv:1004.5063], with ionized gas emission that strongly traces its spiral arms but is absent within the innermost 15 arcseconds [arXiv:1801.07750].

sources.summary: A grand-design spiral with an off-center inner ring, strong HII regions along the arms, and asymmetric ionized gas possibly influenced by NGC 5363.

quotes.quote: NGC 5364 (Figure img5 , middle row). This galaxy is classified as an SA()bc. It is an intermediate to late-type galaxy with bright grand design spiral structure. An off-center inner ring prominently stands out against the underlying disk and the surrounding spiral arms in the B band image. The arms are traced strongly by HII regions of ionized gas.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5364's emission traces spiral arms, lacks inner emission, and yields a rotation curve with inclination-related normalization uncertainty.

quotes.quote: The \ emission in NGC 5364 very strongly traces its spiral arms and we detect no \ emission within the innermost 15 . The rotation curve is rising roughly linearly outside this radius before becoming approximately flat for R 80 . Because the kinematic data are somewhat sparse, the galaxy's inclination has a moderately large uncertainty, leading to a large uncertainty in the overall normalization of the rotation curve.

gmw_00001593.name: NGC 6118
gmw_00001593.overview: NGC 6118 is a spiral galaxy with a velocity map that reveals a continuously rising rotation curve from its center to approximately 100 arcseconds [arXiv:1801.07750]. Photometric studies of the galaxy indicate an H-alpha scale length of 4.2 to 4.5 kiloparsecs [arXiv:astro-ph/0510374].

sources.summary: The paper finds a much shorter H-alpha scale length for NGC 6118 than RD94 and suggests the older outer profile may be sky-contaminated.

quotes.quote: There are only four galaxies in common between our sample and the RD94 sample. We agree on the R--band scale lengths for NGC 1637, but are our values for NGC 4192, NGC 4548, and NGC 6118 are 6-16 longer RD94 are able to measure H scale lengths for all of these galaxies except NGC 1637. We find the largest H scale length discrepancy between our results for NGC 6118.

While RD94 measure an H scale length of 10.7 kpc, we find 4.2 kpc (1-3 r_R ) or 4.5 ( r_R-r_s ) kpc. We do not reproduce the outer extent of the H profile given in Ryder Dopita (1993) and examination of their image and profile suggests that their outer profile may be contaminated by sky. We note that NGC 6118 has the third longest H scale length measured in the RD94 sample.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 6118's velocity map and an axisymmetric-model rotation curve that rises to R 100 before uncertain outer declines.

quotes.quote: Our velocity map for NGC 6118 is presented in Figure fig:N6118 . The rotation curve extracted from our axisymmetric model rises continuously from the center to R 100 . The decreasing values beyond this radius have large uncertainties.

gmw_00001594.name: NGC 6384
gmw_00001594.overview: NGC 6384 is a spiral galaxy similar to the Milky Way [arXiv:1901.06350]. Its central region contains a nuclear star cluster embedded within a boxy/X-shaped bulge [arXiv:1901.06350], while its outer emission closely traces the spiral arms [arXiv:1801.07750].

sources.summary: The paper uses high-resolution NIR imaging to model NGC 6384's nuclear star cluster, central kiloparsec, bulge, disk, and stellar populations from ARGOS and HST photometry.

quotes.quote: We analyse high spatial resolution near infra-red (NIR) imaging of NGC 6384, a Milky Way like galaxy, using ARGOS commissioning data at the Large Binocular Telescope (LBT). ARGOS provides a stable PSF _ FWHM = 0 2 0 3 AO correction of the ground layer across the LUCI 2 NIR camera 4 4 field by using six laser guide stars (three per telescope) and a na tural guide star for tip-tilt sensing and guiding. Enabled by this high spatial resolution we analyse the structure of the nuclear star cluster (NSC) and the central kiloparsec of NGC 6384. We find via 2D mo dell ing that the NSC ( r_ eff 10 pc) is surrounded by a small ( r_ eff 100 pc) and a larger Sersi ( r_ eff 400 pc), all embedded within the NGC 6384 large-scale boxy/X-shaped bulge and disk.

SED-fitting to the NIR and optical HST photometry allowed to leverage the age-metallicity-extinction degeneracies and derive the effective NSC properties of an young to old population mass ratio of 8 with M _ ,old 3.5 10^7M_ , Age _ old, \ young = 10.9 1.3 Gyr and 226 Myr 62 , metallicity [ M/H ] = 0.11 0.16 and 0.33 39 dex, and E(B V) = 0.63 and 1.44 mag.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 6384, showing emission along spiral arms and no detected emission in the innermost 25 arcseconds.

quotes.quote: We present our velocity map for NGC 6384 in Figure fig:N6384 . As in NGC 5364, the \ emission closely traces the spiral arms. We detect no \ emission within the innermost 25 .

gmw_00001595.name: NGC 7606
gmw_00001595.overview: NGC 7606 is a spiral galaxy characterized by a typical spiral-disk velocity flow pattern [arXiv:1801.07750]. It is a fast-rotating system with a rotation curve that rises from the innermost region, flattens around 30 arcseconds, and declines slightly further out [arXiv:1801.07750].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7606 as the sample's fastest rotator, with a typical spiral-disk flow pattern and a sparsely constrained rotation curve.

quotes.quote: NGC 7606 is the fastest-rotating galaxy in this sample and the second most-luminous. Again the velocity map, Figure fig:N7606 , displays the flow pattern of a typical spiral disk, and again we detect no \ emission in the innermost 15 . Our fitted rotation curve appears to be rising from our innermost point, becoming roughly flat from R 30 , before declining somewhat from 50 - 120 with a hint of an outer increase, although the uncertainties are large due to the sparseness of our data at these radii.

gmw_00001596.name: AM 1353-272
gmw_00001596.overview: AM 1353-272 is an interacting galaxy pair consisting of an integral-sign spiral galaxy and a disturbed disk companion [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405, arXiv:astro-ph/0601250]. The system features two 30-kiloparsec-long tidal tails that host blue knots and candidate tidal dwarf galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405, arXiv:astro-ph/0601250].

sources.summary: AM 1353-272 contains two same-redshift interacting galaxies, including an integral-sign spiral with long tidal tails hosting blue knots and detached possible associated knots.

quotes.quote: AM 1353-272 Four galaxies are seen in the field of AM 1353-272 (Fig. AM1353-272 ). The two brightest are at the same redshift. ``A'' is an integral-sign spiral ( V_ A = 12145 km s ^ 1 ) interacting with a disturbed disk galaxy ``B'' ( V_ B = 11791 km s ^ 1 ).

The long tidal tails of ``A'' host a number of blue knots (``a'' to ``n''). Three detached small knots (``o'' to ``q'') are visible east of the northeast tail. Given their colors, these knots could be physically linked with ``A'' but without redshift information available this remains speculative. The knots have absolute magnitudes in the range of 12.1 < M_B < -15.7 mag. Their colors do agree with the Z_3 models and intermediate strength burst.

sources.summary: The paper describes AM 1353-272 as an interacting galaxy pair with long tidal tails, candidate tidal dwarf galaxies, concentrated central line emission, and unresolved kinematic discrepancies in one outer candidate.

quotes.quote: The galaxy pair AM 1353-272 nicknamed ``The Dentist's Chair'' shows two 30 kpc long tidal tails. Previous observations using multi-slit masks showed that they host up to seven tidal dwarf galaxies. The kinematics of these tidal dwarfs appeared to be decoupled from the surrounding tidal material. New observations of the tip of the southern tidal tail with the VIMOS integral field unit confirm the results for two of these genuine tidal dwarfs but raise doubts whether the velocity gradient attributed to the outermost tidal dwarf candidate is real.

sources.summary: Component `A' is described as the tailed member of AM 1353-272, with central line emission, bar-end knots, and a bar-like structure whose ends show opposite velocity signs.

quotes.quote: Fig. PmW:Fig:System shows the two components of the system, `A', a galaxy with 30 kpc long tidal tails, and `B', a disturbed disk galaxy, have a distance of D 160 Mpc ( H_0 = 75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ).

The center of galaxy `A', much of the southern tidal tail, and the companion `B' were targeted with low-resolution (blue grism, 54 ^ 54 ^ ), and observed for 1 hour in mediocre conditions with 2 . ^ 0 seeing.

The southern tidal tail in undetected in this exposure and the strongest line emission appears to be in the center of `A' and in the two knots at the end of its bar-like central structure designated `g' and `j' in WFDF02 . Galaxy `B', despite being strongly reddened, also is a strong source of H line emission. As the velocity resolution is on the order of 100 km s ^ 1 , in this mode of VIMOS we cannot resolve the velocity structures in individual knots, but the bar-like structure in `A' seems to rotate (the eastern end near knot `j' is receding, the western end near knot `g' is approaching). The same is true for the companion `B'.

gmw_00001597.name: `B'
gmw_00001597.overview: The object 'B' is a disturbed disk companion galaxy located in the AM 1353-272 system [arXiv:1502.06548, arXiv:astro-ph/0601250]. Despite being strongly reddened, it is a low-luminosity starburst galaxy that exhibits strong H-alpha line emission and a rotation-like velocity structure [arXiv:1502.06548, arXiv:astro-ph/0601250].

sources.summary: The paper uses AM 1353-272 B, a disturbed low-luminosity starburst companion, to demonstrate spatially resolved DIB mapping beyond the Local Group and finds DIB strength follows extinction radially.

quotes.quote: This work presents the first spatially resolved detection of a DIB in a galaxy outside the Local Group. By using 568 MUSE spectra sampling a total projected area of 13.5 kpc ^2 , we were able to measure the equivalent width of the DIB at 5780.5 in six locations of AM 1353-272 B. Our strategy constitutes an alternative approach to extragalactic DIB studies different from using targets in the Local Group and individual sightlines outside the Local Group.

We found decreasing radial profiles for EW( 5780.5), both in the receding and approaching sides of AM 1353-272 B up to distances of 4.6 kpc from the galaxy centre. The interstellar extinction displays a similar trend, with decreasing values towards the external parts. This translates into a correlation between the strength of the DIB and the extinction within AM 1353-272 B.

sources.summary: Component `B' is described as a disturbed disk companion in AM 1353-272; the observations target it, detect strong H emission, and find rotation-like velocity structure.

quotes.quote: Fig. PmW:Fig:System shows the two components of the system, `A', a galaxy with 30 kpc long tidal tails, and `B', a disturbed disk galaxy, have a distance of D 160 Mpc ( H_0 = 75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ).

The center of galaxy `A', much of the southern tidal tail, and the companion `B' were targeted with low-resolution (blue grism, 54 ^ 54 ^ ), and observed for 1 hour in mediocre conditions with 2 . ^ 0 seeing.

Galaxy `B', despite being strongly reddened, also is a strong source of H line emission. As the velocity resolution is on the order of 100 km s ^ 1 , in this mode of VIMOS we cannot resolve the velocity structures in individual knots, but the bar-like structure in `A' seems to rotate (the eastern end near knot `j' is receding, the western end near knot `g' is approaching). The same is true for the companion `B'.

gmw_00001598.name: Object A
gmw_00001598.overview: Object A is a radio-loud active galactic nucleus monitored by the Kepler satellite [arXiv:1303.1147]. Over a 2.8-year observation period, the object exhibited moderate-amplitude variations and multiple flares [arXiv:1311.4838], showing a steady increase in both its overall activity and flare amplitudes [arXiv:1311.4838].

sources.summary: Object A showed the strongest Kepler variability, including Q14-Q16 flares and a 2.8-year light curve with increasing activity and flare amplitudes.

quotes.quote: Through examination of the light curves we note that during Q14 object A showed a large scale flare of 15 from the baseline flux centered around Barycentric Kepler Julian Date (BKJD) 1320; BKJD is defined as BJD 2454833.0 days, and used so as to store smaller time stamps on the satellite. Throughout the quarter, multiple flares on the 5 scale are noted. This flaring activity continued into Q15; however, object A unfortunately then fell on a Moir e affected CCD, preventing us from making any claims on the amplitude of the flaring activity then. During Q16, object A showed a few small variations, with two flares at the 6 level.

Overall, objects A and C show the most true variations, with object A the most active. Object A shows moderate amplitude variations at the 5-20 level throughout the 2.8 years of collected data. While some quarters exhibit variations due to the Moir e effect (see 2), unaffected quarters clearly show moderate and rapid changes in baseline flux. Overall this object has been steadily increasing over time in both overall activity, and amplitude of the noted flares.

sources.summary: Object A is one of the Kepler-monitored radio-loud AGN; it showed several 4-6 percent flares over ten days, while no significant Palomar-era variability was found.

quotes.quote: Object A (MG4 J192325+4754) showed several significant flares of 4-6 size over a period of 10 days in Q12. This quarter is one of the two for that AGN that should not have been affected at all by Moir e problems but this quarter was affected by solar coronal mass ejections kepler2012 ; however, the interval in question was well away from those disruptions and also well away from the monthly gaps and glitches.

Flares and the Overall Shape of the Light Curves In 30 quarters of data, the strongest flare we saw was an excursion of 7 amplitude in Object C during Q10, and lasting about 5 days. Object A also several significant flares of 4-6 size over a period of 10 days in Q12.

gmw_00001599.name: Object B
gmw_00001599.overview: Object B is a galaxy tentatively classified as a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy at a redshift of 0.513 [arXiv:1303.1147]. Ground-based imaging resolves a faint host galaxy surrounding its central point source [arXiv:1303.1147], which exhibits very little intrinsic optical variability [arXiv:1311.4838].

sources.summary: Object B showed little intrinsic variability: an apparent Q14 dip was likely instrumental, Q15 was dominated by velocity aberration, and later data had module gaps.

quotes.quote: During Q14 object B was noted to show an 8 dip in brightness consisting of four contiguous data points, centered on BKJD 1360. No loss of fine pointing or other instrumental glitch could be correlated with this peculiar event. Nonetheless, given the paucity of apparently intrinsic variations of this object, it is likely that this was an undetermined instrumental effect. During Q15 the primary observed variation for object B is due to velocity aberration. There is no data for object B during Q16 as that is one of the four quarters each year where it falls on dead CCD Module 3.

Object B generally shows the least variation of all four objects, with the only clear variation due to differential velocity aberration. There are two major gaps in the data during Quarters 8 and 12, and an early end to the data train (no Quarter 16), since object B fell on the dead CCD Module 3 at those times.

sources.summary: Object B is the lowest-redshift target; Palomar imaging resolved host-galaxy emission around its point source, and the paper links this contribution to flatter Kepler power spectra.

quotes.quote: Object B, the lowest redshift target, was resolved by g' band imaging into a central point source embedded in diffuse emission typical of host galaxies. With ground based seeing of 1.^ 5 and with the target at redshift 0.513, no structural details were expected or revealed; the fuzzy host galaxy emission in the g' band image was estimated at approximately 6 arcsec extent using IRAF tasks. In the other Sloan bands, Object B was much more strongly dominated by the central nuclear source with comparatively weak diffuse emission detected in radial profile plots. It was tentatively classed as a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy based on observations of a spectrum with a single emission line, H , by hen97 .

Object B, tentatively classed as a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy, is the object in which our Palomar imaging detected the faint host galaxy surrounding the bright central point source, hence, the Kepler-detected optical emission had a small extra contribution from the host galaxy which could damp down any variation and flatten the resulting PSD.

gmw_00001600.name: Object C
gmw_00001600.overview: Object C is an active galactic nucleus that exhibits a nearly flat optical spectrum, which may indicate a thermal Big Blue Bump contribution [arXiv:1303.1147]. Its light curve shows continual small-scale variations alongside a major flare lasting several days [arXiv:1303.1147, arXiv:1311.4838].

sources.summary: Object C showed a real 7 percent flux rise, additional 3 percent fluctuations, and a flat optical spectrum suggesting possible thermal Big Blue Bump contribution.

quotes.quote: We found a 7 rise in the flux of CGRaBS J1918+4937 (object C) during Q10 which lasted about five or six days around BJD=2455755 (Fig. 2). We confirmed that there was no plausible contamination source, e.g., a nearby star that might have flared and been incorporated into the pixels included in its photometry. This variation shares no characteristics with known artifacts, hence, we conclude that it is real. Object C also showed three consecutive fluctuations of 3 amplitude during a span of about 25 days during Q9. Other variations in Object C during Q7 and Q11 might be real, but because there was some Moire effect present in those quarters, we cannot make that attribution.

In contrast, Object C has a nearly flat spectrum (bluer than the other three objects) consistent with a blue continuum spectrum showing a single emission line, MgII at 2798 , ( healey2008 ; FITS spectrum provided by M. S. Shaw, personal communication). In Object C, therefore, the optical emission could have a significant contribution from thermal Big Blue Bump emission, but disentangling the relative contributions of nonthermal and thermal emission requires high quality spectrophotometry which is beyond the scope of this paper.

sources.summary: Object C showed continual small-amplitude Kepler variations, minor Q16 changes, and a high-duty-cycle 2.8-year light curve with one several-day flare.

quotes.quote: During Q14 and Q15, object C showed continual variation at the 1-2 level on a timescale of several days. During Q15 some of the variation may be due to the Moir e effect described in 2. Throughout Q16 object C continued to show minor variations at the 1-2 level.

Object C shows small scale variations at the few percent level with a high duty cycle. There is one major flare lasting for several days noted around KBJD 925. See Fig. \ 2 of Paper I for more details.

gmw_00001601.name: Object D
gmw_00001601.overview: Object D is a flat-spectrum radio quasar characterized by a compact radio structure [arXiv:1303.1147]. Its optical spectrum peaks at the i' band, and the source exhibited no short-term variability during any Kepler quarter [arXiv:1303.1147].

sources.summary: Object D is treated as a compact-radio-structure FSRQ target with a peaked optical spectrum and no detected short-term variability during any Kepler quarter.

quotes.quote: Our 4 FSRQ targets are all VLBA calibrators with compact radio structures (e.g., [ HB89 ] 1924+507=4C50.47, a member of the CJF survey in ). A leading model for any jet-based quasi-periodic variations is the intersection of an outward propagating shock with a helical structure within the jet rani09 .
Object D has a spectrum peaked at i' band, but the large uncertainty at z' band is sufficient to encompass a power law similar to those seen in Objects A and B.
Neither object B nor D showed any short-term variability of any type during any quarter.

gmw_00001602.name: XI
gmw_00001602.overview: Andromeda XI is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy and a satellite of Andromeda [arXiv:2305.13360, arXiv:1302.6590]. It experienced a dominant star-formation episode before z=5, followed by a minor period of later star formation that produced approximately 15 percent of its stellar mass [arXiv:2305.13360].

sources.summary: The paper identifies XI as an M31 ultra-faint dwarf and reports an early dominant star-formation episode before z=5, followed by about 15 percent later star formation.

quotes.quote: We select every galaxy targeted by the survey that has an absolute magnitude M_V>-7.0 , which leaves no ambiguity about their status as UFDs. We use absolute magnitudes from Savino22 , which are based on the PandAS structural parameters of Martin16 and are recalibrated on the basis of updated RR Lyrae distances. This selection yields six targets: XI ( M_V = -6.4 0.4 , 11 orbits of imaging), XII ( M_V = -6.6 0.5 , 11 orbits), XIII ( M_V = -6.8 0.4 , 9 orbits), XX ( M_V = -6.4 0.4 , 11 orbits), XXII ( M_V = -6.4 0.4 , 18 orbits), and XXVI ( M_V = -6.0^ +0.7 _ 0.5 , 16 orbits).

Figure fig:SFHs shows the lifetime SFHs for our six UFDs. Five of the UFDs in our sample ( XI , XII , XX , XXII and XXVI ) have a prominent episode of star formation in the earliest time bin ( t>12.6 Gyr), which drastically decreased in intensity by z 5 . Taken at face value, the best-fit SFHs indicate that all five galaxies formed 50 of their stellar mass in the oldest time bin. This initial strong episode of star formation is followed by varying degrees of star formation at later times ranging from 15 ( XI ) to 35 ( XXII ) of the total stellar mass.

sources.summary: And XI is described as the most luminous of the three new M31 dwarf galaxies, a faint stellar overdensity with an RGB population, uncertain distance, and measured center.

quotes.quote: And XI This satellite is the most luminous of the three new dwarf galaxies found around M31. It is however so faint that the MegaCam image observed in the g band does not show any visible dwarf, although it does correspond to an overdensity of stars (bottom right panel of Figure M31dwarf_fig03 ). The CMD of the region within 3 arcminutes of the central position of the dwarf is constructed in the left panel of Figure M31dwarf_fig03 and shows a RGB, composed of 40 stars with an implied metallicity of 1.3 . The metallicity distribution of stars with i_0<24.0 (top right panel of the same Figure) indeed shows a higher number of stars around the median value of 1.3 . Only stars within this peak, that is with 1.8< <-0.8 that populate the RGB (thick histogram) are considered as And XI stars and used to determine the parameters of the dwarf.

The center of the dwarf, determined as the mean of the stars within 3 arcminutes and with 1.8< <-0.8 is ( , )=(0^ h 46^ m 20^ s ,+33 48'05'') . The luminosity profiles of the dwarf in both the g and i bands are constructed from this center by adding the flux contribution of each star, corrected from the background contaminating flux that is determined from stars within an annulus between 4 and 10 arcminutes.

sources.summary: The paper identifies five probable Andromeda XI members, revises its velocity and dispersion upward relative to Collins et al., and argues the new dataset is more robust than T12.

quotes.quote: The kinematic properties for Andromeda XI as measured from this DEIMOS data set were first published in collins10 . Here we identify 5 stars as probable members. We determine most-likely parameters of v_r=-427.5^ +3.5 _ 3.4 and _v=7.6^ +4.0 _ 2.8 . In collins10 we measured v_r=-419.4^ +4.4 _ 3.8 and we were unable to resolve the velocity dispersion for the dSph, measuring _v=0.0^ +4.6 (where the upper bound represents the formal 1 uncertainty on the unresolved dispersion), which implied a higher systemic velocity and lower velocity dispersion for And XI.

gmw_00001603.name: XII
gmw_00001603.overview: Andromeda XII is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:2305.13360, arXiv:1406.1243] and a satellite of M31 [arXiv:1211.2638, arXiv:1302.6590]. It exhibits extreme kinematics [arXiv:1211.2638, arXiv:1302.6590] and is likely on its first infall into the Local Group [arXiv:1211.2638, arXiv:1406.1243].

sources.summary: The paper identifies XII as an M31 ultra-faint dwarf and reports an early dominant star-formation episode before z=5, followed by a later-time tail.

quotes.quote: We select every galaxy targeted by the survey that has an absolute magnitude M_V>-7.0 , which leaves no ambiguity about their status as UFDs. We use absolute magnitudes from Savino22 , which are based on the PandAS structural parameters of Martin16 and are recalibrated on the basis of updated RR Lyrae distances. This selection yields six targets: XI ( M_V = -6.4 0.4 , 11 orbits of imaging), XII ( M_V = -6.6 0.5 , 11 orbits), XIII ( M_V = -6.8 0.4 , 9 orbits), XX ( M_V = -6.4 0.4 , 11 orbits), XXII ( M_V = -6.4 0.4 , 18 orbits), and XXVI ( M_V = -6.0^ +0.7 _ 0.5 , 16 orbits).

Figure fig:SFHs shows the lifetime SFHs for our six UFDs. Five of the UFDs in our sample ( XI , XII , XX , XXII and XXVI ) have a prominent episode of star formation in the earliest time bin ( t>12.6 Gyr), which drastically decreased in intensity by z 5 . Taken at face value, the best-fit SFHs indicate that all five galaxies formed 50 of their stellar mass in the oldest time bin. This initial strong episode of star formation is followed by varying degrees of star formation at later times ranging from 15 ( XI ) to 35 ( XXII ) of the total stellar mass.

sources.summary: And XII is the least-populated new dwarf, with a sparse RGB, a stellar overdensity centered near 0h47m27s, and derived parameters consistent with a very faint dwarf.

quotes.quote: And XII Among the three dwarf galaxies presented in this paper, And XII is the one with the least populated RGB with only 20 stars. The CMD of stars within 3' of its central position is shown in the left panel of Figure M31dwarf_fig04 and contains a well defined RGB between the isochrones of metallicities 2.3 and 1.3 . The metallicity distribution once again contains a strong peak, with a median value of =-1.5 . In the following only stars within the 1.8< <-1.0 metallicity range are considered to belong to the dwarf.

The distribution of these stars on the sky (bottom right panel of the same Figure) shows that, even though there are not many stars in this region of the sky, they do correspond to an overdensity, centered on ( , )=(0^ h 47^ m 27^ s ,+34 22'29'') . The probability that a group of stars are at the same time clustered on the sky and along a RGB is very low (see section 4), strengthening the hypothesis that And XII is a dwarf even though it is not directly visible on the MegaCam images. The derived parameters are also consistent with those of a dwarf, though a very faint one.

sources.summary: The paper identifies And XII as an extreme-velocity M31 satellite poorly fit by the smooth model; timing-argument solutions rule out prior pericentric passage, implying first infall.

quotes.quote: Two of the entourage, And XII martin2006, chapman2007, collins2010 and And XIV majewski2007, kalirai2010 , are unusual in that they have extreme kinematics with high line-of-sight velocities. And XII may have been formed outside of the virial radius of the Local Group chapman2007 and only recently been accreted.

The period of And XII is estimated at T 3.5 Gyr and the period of And XIV is estimates at T 7 Gyr. Thus, for these models, And XIV is predicted to be on its second or third orbit, and And XII on its fourth; i.e. neither satellite is on its first infall into the M31 system. However, from fig:psat , we conclude that these satellites are unlikely for all of the models that we have considered, so it is unwise to put too much faith into these periods.
For And XII, orbits which assume at least one pericentric passage can be ruled out and it must be on its first infall into the M31 system.

sources.summary: The paper describes Andromeda XII as an extreme-velocity satellite, measuring an unresolved dispersion and finding consistency with earlier studies.

quotes.quote: Kinematic properties for Andromeda XII as determined from the DEIMOS data set presented here were previously published in chapman07 and collins10 , and in both cases, membership was largely determined using hard cuts in velocity. This object has been of particular interest as it possesses an extremely negative systemic velocity with respect to Andromeda, suggesting that it is on its first infall into the local group (see chapman07 for a full discussion). Using our new algorithm, we measure v_r=-557.1 and an unresolved velocity dispersion of _v=0.0^ +4.0 , where the upper bound represents the formal 1 uncertainty on the measurement. As the dispersion is unresolved, the lower error bound is undefined, as having a negative velocity dispersion is unphysical. These values are completely consistent with the results of chapman07 and collins10 ( v_r=-558.4 3.2 and _v=2.6^ +5.1 _ 2.6 ).

sources.summary: The review presents Andromeda XII as a contrasting infalling dSph that may be entering the Local Group for the first time after earlier gas-stripping interactions.

quotes.quote: Andromeda XII: untouched by the LG? An interesting, opposite case, has been reported a few years ago. This dSph galaxy seems to be infalling at very high speed into the LG (Chapman et al. 2007). Chapman et al. (2007) argue that, because of the large velocity, this galaxy is entering the LG for the very first time, and therefore represents the best example of a late infall in the LG. Being depleted of gas, this dSph must have experience strong tidal interactions before joining the LG.

gmw_00001604.name: XX
gmw_00001604.overview: Andromeda XX is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:2305.13360] that is one of the faintest companions of Andromeda [arXiv:1302.6590]. It experienced a dominant episode of star formation before z=5 [arXiv:2305.13360], followed by a lower level of star formation at later times [arXiv:2305.13360].

sources.summary: The paper identifies XX as an M31 ultra-faint dwarf and reports an early dominant star-formation episode before z=5, followed by a later-time tail.

quotes.quote: We select every galaxy targeted by the survey that has an absolute magnitude M_V>-7.0 , which leaves no ambiguity about their status as UFDs. We use absolute magnitudes from Savino22 , which are based on the PandAS structural parameters of Martin16 and are recalibrated on the basis of updated RR Lyrae distances. This selection yields six targets: XI ( M_V = -6.4 0.4 , 11 orbits of imaging), XII ( M_V = -6.6 0.5 , 11 orbits), XIII ( M_V = -6.8 0.4 , 9 orbits), XX ( M_V = -6.4 0.4 , 11 orbits), XXII ( M_V = -6.4 0.4 , 18 orbits), and XXVI ( M_V = -6.0^ +0.7 _ 0.5 , 16 orbits).

Figure fig:SFHs shows the lifetime SFHs for our six UFDs. Five of the UFDs in our sample ( XI , XII , XX , XXII and XXVI ) have a prominent episode of star formation in the earliest time bin ( t>12.6 Gyr), which drastically decreased in intensity by z 5 . Taken at face value, the best-fit SFHs indicate that all five galaxies formed 50 of their stellar mass in the oldest time bin. This initial strong episode of star formation is followed by varying degrees of star formation at later times ranging from 15 ( XI ) to 35 ( XXII ) of the total stellar mass.

sources.summary: The paper describes Andromeda XX as one of Andromeda’s faintest companions, with four probable members giving a tentative velocity dispersion that needs confirmation.

quotes.quote: And XX was the third of three dSphs discovered by mcconnachie08 , and is notable for being one of the faintest dSph companions detected surrounding Andromeda thus far. With M_V=-6.3 and r_ half =114^ +31 _ 12  pc, it is a challenging object to study spectroscopically as there are very few stars available to target on its RGB, as shown in the top left subplot in Fig.  fig:And20 . As a result, our algorithm is only able to find 4 stars for which P_i>10^ 6 . These are found to cluster around v_r=-456.2^ +3.1 _ 3.6 , with a dispersion of _v=7.1^ +3.9 _ 2.5 .

gmw_00001605.name: XXVI
gmw_00001605.overview: Andromeda XXVI is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy associated with M31 [arXiv:1102.2902, arXiv:2305.13360]. It features a sparsely populated, metal-poor red giant branch [arXiv:1102.2902] and experienced a dominant star-formation episode before redshift z=5 [arXiv:2305.13360].

sources.summary: The paper identifies And XXVI as a newly discovered M31 dwarf spheroidal with sparse RGB stars but a discernible metal-poor RGB sequence.

quotes.quote: The five new dwarf galaxies were straightforward to detect as over-densities in the matched-filter surface density maps of metal-poor RGB stars. There are no other obvious candidate satellites visible in this map or in our equivalent average metallicity and metal-rich maps. For simplicity and following convention, we have named them Andromeda XXIII - XXVII (And XXIII - XXVII) after the constellation they are found in. As noted earlier the locations of all previously known dSphs (blue circles) and the five new dwarfs (red circles) are readily visible in Fig. fig_map . Moving from East to West (left to right in the Figure) the new galaxies are And XXIII, And XXIV, And XXVII, And XXV and And XXVI.

And XXIV and And XXVI have the most sparsely populated RGBs of the dSphs resulting in much noisier LFs. However, in both cases a metal-poor RGB sequence can still be clearly discerned, which is manifestly absent in the companion reference fields.

sources.summary: The paper identifies XXVI as an M31 ultra-faint dwarf and reports an early dominant star-formation episode before z=5, followed by a later-time tail.

quotes.quote: We select every galaxy targeted by the survey that has an absolute magnitude M_V>-7.0 , which leaves no ambiguity about their status as UFDs. We use absolute magnitudes from Savino22 , which are based on the PandAS structural parameters of Martin16 and are recalibrated on the basis of updated RR Lyrae distances. This selection yields six targets: XI ( M_V = -6.4 0.4 , 11 orbits of imaging), XII ( M_V = -6.6 0.5 , 11 orbits), XIII ( M_V = -6.8 0.4 , 9 orbits), XX ( M_V = -6.4 0.4 , 11 orbits), XXII ( M_V = -6.4 0.4 , 18 orbits), and XXVI ( M_V = -6.0^ +0.7 _ 0.5 , 16 orbits).

Figure fig:SFHs shows the lifetime SFHs for our six UFDs. Five of the UFDs in our sample ( XI , XII , XX , XXII and XXVI ) have a prominent episode of star formation in the earliest time bin ( t>12.6 Gyr), which drastically decreased in intensity by z 5 . Taken at face value, the best-fit SFHs indicate that all five galaxies formed 50 of their stellar mass in the oldest time bin. This initial strong episode of star formation is followed by varying degrees of star formation at later times ranging from 15 ( XI ) to 35 ( XXII ) of the total stellar mass.

gmw_00001606.name: XIII
gmw_00001606.overview: Andromeda XIII is an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2305.13360] located in the halo of the Andromeda Galaxy [arXiv:1302.6590]. It features a stellar population with a peaked metallicity distribution [arXiv:astro-ph/0607472] and experienced a delayed, rapid burst of star formation approximately 11 billion years ago [arXiv:2305.13360].

sources.summary: The paper flags Andromeda XIII as an outlier, deriving a shorter RR Lyrae distance than earlier HST/WFPC2 work and attributing differences to metallicity, extinction, and calibration choices.

quotes.quote: Based on HST/WFPC2 RR Lyrae data, yang_hst/wfpc2_2012 report a distance of =24.62 0.05 . We find =24.44 0.06 using the same data, a 2 disagreement. There are a few subtle differences in the analyses, which may reconcile the difference.

First, yang_hst/wfpc2_2012 adopt a metallicity of [ Fe/H ] = -1.6 based on the observed color of the RGB, whereas we assume [ Fe/H ] = -2 . Second, yang_hst/wfpc2_2012 derive their own extinction, A_V=0.341 , which is 0.12 mag larger than schlafly2011 . We use the reddening-free PWZ.

Finally, they anchor their distances to the Chaboyer1999 RR Lyrae calibration, which is ultimately based on Hipparcos . As we discuss in sec:caretta , this results in an average offset of 0.05 mag compared to Gaia eDR3.

sources.summary: The paper identifies XIII as an M31 ultra-faint dwarf with an unusual delayed history: little early star formation, then a rapid burst around z~2-3 forming most of its stellar mass.

quotes.quote: We select every galaxy targeted by the survey that has an absolute magnitude M_V>-7.0 , which leaves no ambiguity about their status as UFDs. We use absolute magnitudes from Savino22 , which are based on the PandAS structural parameters of Martin16 and are recalibrated on the basis of updated RR Lyrae distances. This selection yields six targets: XI ( M_V = -6.4 0.4 , 11 orbits of imaging), XII ( M_V = -6.6 0.5 , 11 orbits), XIII ( M_V = -6.8 0.4 , 9 orbits), XX ( M_V = -6.4 0.4 , 11 orbits), XXII ( M_V = -6.4 0.4 , 18 orbits), and XXVI ( M_V = -6.0^ +0.7 _ 0.5 , 16 orbits).

XIII exhibits a very different SFH. From the best-fit SFH, we see that only 10 of its stellar mass formed in the oldest time bin, followed by a 1 Gyr quiescent period. The galaxy then experienced a strong burst of star formation beginning 11 Gyr ago, in which it formed 75 of its total stellar mass in the span of 1 Gyr. As we discuss below, the SFH of XIII is unlike any other known UFD. Its unusual nature, and the observational challenges linked to this target (being the most distant system in our sample, one of the most extincted, and with relatively shallow photometry), motivated a number of tests for robustness (e.g., sensitivity to adopted stellar model, distance/extinction variations), all of which confirm this ``young'' UFD scenario.

sources.summary: And XIII is presented as a third faint M31 dwarf, with an RGB population, peaked metallicity distribution, elliptical overdensity, measured center, and uncertain but derived structural parameters.

quotes.quote: And XIII The third faint dwarf galaxy that appears in the survey is presented in Figure M31dwarf_fig05 . Once again, the CMD shows a RGB populated with 30 stars within 3' of the central position of the dwarf. The metallicity distribution is also well peaked, with a median value of =-1.4 . Since this peak has no clear limits, especially of the low metallicity side, we select And XIII stars within 0.5 dex of the median value. The distribution of these stars on the sky (bottom right panel of Figure M31dwarf_fig05 ) reveals a roughly elliptical overdensity.

The mean position of this sample of stars is ( , )=(0^ h 51^ m 51^ s ,+33 00'16'') and is taken as the center of the dwarf. The number of stars in And XIII is only a little higher than in And XII, and so the parameters of the dwarf are also difficult to derive precisely. As above, we use the distance of M31 for the dwarf to account for the difficulty of determining the tip of the sparsely populated RGB.

sources.summary: The paper finds Andromeda XIII lies in a structured field, identifies four likely stars near v 200, and reports an unresolved velocity dispersion with large uncertainties.

quotes.quote: The kinematic properties of Andromeda XIII were also presented in collins10 , And XIII sits at a large projected distance from Andromeda (  120 kpc) in the southern M31 halo, so we expect contamination from the Milky Way and Andromeda halo to be low. It is surprising then, that we see significant structure within our DEIMOS field. This is also seen within the 3 fields observed by T12, who attribute this over-density of stars located at v_r 120 to an association with the TriAnd over-density within the Galactic halo. We too see a number of stars between 140 and 100 . From their positions within the CMD of And XIII, they appear more consistent with MW foreground K-dwarfs than M31 RGB stars.

gmw_00001607.name: IRAS01072+4954
gmw_00001607.overview: IRAS01072+4954 is a starburst/Seyfert composite galaxy [arXiv:1203.6063, arXiv:1305.3277] that exhibits an elongated bar-like structure [arXiv:1305.3277]. The galaxy hosts an intermediate-mass black hole [arXiv:1305.3277] surrounded by hot dust [arXiv:1203.6063], and it displays Seyfert 1-like X-ray emission [arXiv:1305.3277] with negligible intrinsic absorption [arXiv:astro-ph/0407585].

sources.summary: The paper studies IRAS 01072+4954 as a starburst/Seyfert composite, finding little nuclear obscuration, hot dust suggestive of a torus, and evidence against a true Seyfert 2 interpretation.

quotes.quote: One of them, the starburst/Seyfert composite galaxy IRAS 01072+4954 ( z=0.0236 ), has a typical Type-1 X-ray emission, while its optical spectrum resembles an H ii galaxy and lacks the expected broad lines. We performed near-infrared integral-field observations of this object with the aim to determine the nature of its nuclear emission and to find indications for the existence or absence of a broad-line region. Several reasons have been proposed to explain this peculiar emission. We studied the validity of these hypotheses, including the possibility for this galaxy to be a True-Seyfert 2. We found little obscuration toward the nucleus A_V = 2.5 mag , and a nuclear star-formation rate _ SFR < 11.6 yr^ 1 kpc^ 2 , which is below the average in Seyferts.

Unresolved hot-dust emission with T 1150 K seems to indicate the presence of a torus with its axis close to the line of sight. We found that IRAS 01072+4954 hosts a low-mass black hole with an estimated mass of 10^5 . Its bolometric luminosity is 2.5 10^ 42 , which yields a high accretion rate with an Eddington ratio _ Edd 0.2 .

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 01072+4954 as a starburst/Seyfert composite with Seyfert-like X-rays, models its central kiloparsec and surface-brightness profile, finds a bar-like structure, and presents IMBH evidence.

quotes.quote: The starburst/Seyfert composite galaxy IRAS 01072+4954 ( z=0.0236 ) is an enigmatic source that combines a Seyfert 1-like X-ray emission with a starburst optical spectrum that lacks broad line emission. We performed high angular resolution observations of the central kiloparsec of this galaxy in the near-infrared. Combining our data with 2MASS images of the whole galaxy, we obtain and model the surface brightness profile. We find indications for the presence of an elongated bar-like structure in both data sets.
We present evidence for the presence of an IMBH at the center of IRAS 01072+4954.

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS01072+4954 as one of four Chandra-observed composite galaxies, with X-ray luminosity lower than RASS and no statistically required intrinsic absorption.

quotes.quote: We have performed a study with Chandra of the four ``Composites'' that still lack a high resolution X-ray observation, with two main goals: 1) to investigate any extended X-ray component possibly associated with the InfraRed Starburst; 2) to determine the nature of the nuclear emission.
The luminosity is smaller by a factor 10 for IRAS04392-0123, and a factor 2 for IRAS01072+4954 IRAS01319-1604, as indicated by R in Table tab .

We find instead that intrinsic N _ H is always small or negligible: IRAS01072+4954 and IRAS01319-1604 do not require statistically an intrinsic N _ H component; IRAS04392-0123 has N _ H ^ intrinsic 1.17 10^ 21 cm^ 2 ; IRAS20069+5929 has N _ H ^ intrinsic = 2.69 [ 2.21-3.22 ] 10^ 21 cm^ 2 .

gmw_00001608.name: IRAS01319-1604
gmw_00001608.overview: IRAS01319-1604 is a composite galaxy that exhibits both starburst and active galactic nucleus properties [arXiv:astro-ph/0407585]. High-resolution X-ray observations show that its luminosity dropped by a factor of two compared to ROSAT All-Sky Survey data, with no statistically required intrinsic absorption [arXiv:astro-ph/0407585].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS01319-1604 as one of the Chandra composite galaxies, reporting a factor-two luminosity drop from RASS and no statistically required intrinsic absorption.

quotes.quote: We have performed a study with Chandra of the four ``Composites'' that still lack a high resolution X-ray observation, with two main goals: 1) to investigate any extended X-ray component possibly associated with the InfraRed Starburst; 2) to determine the nature of the nuclear emission.
The luminosity is smaller by a factor 10 for IRAS04392-0123, and a factor 2 for IRAS01072+4954 IRAS01319-1604, as indicated by R in Table tab .

We find instead that intrinsic N _ H is always small or negligible: IRAS01072+4954 and IRAS01319-1604 do not require statistically an intrinsic N _ H component; IRAS04392-0123 has N _ H ^ intrinsic 1.17 10^ 21 cm^ 2 ; IRAS20069+5929 has N _ H ^ intrinsic = 2.69 [ 2.21-3.22 ] 10^ 21 cm^ 2 .

gmw_00001609.name: IRAS04392-0123
gmw_00001609.overview: IRAS04392-0123 is a composite galaxy that hosts both starburst and active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:astro-ph/0407585]. Chandra observations of the galaxy reveal modest intrinsic X-ray absorption and an X-ray luminosity ten times lower than previously indicated by the ROSAT All-Sky Survey [arXiv:astro-ph/0407585].

sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS04392-0123 as a Chandra-observed composite galaxy with luminosity ten times below RASS and modest measured intrinsic X-ray absorption.

quotes.quote: We have performed a study with Chandra of the four ``Composites'' that still lack a high resolution X-ray observation, with two main goals: 1) to investigate any extended X-ray component possibly associated with the InfraRed Starburst; 2) to determine the nature of the nuclear emission.
The luminosity is smaller by a factor 10 for IRAS04392-0123, and a factor 2 for IRAS01072+4954 IRAS01319-1604, as indicated by R in Table tab .

We find instead that intrinsic N _ H is always small or negligible: IRAS01072+4954 and IRAS01319-1604 do not require statistically an intrinsic N _ H component; IRAS04392-0123 has N _ H ^ intrinsic 1.17 10^ 21 cm^ 2 ; IRAS20069+5929 has N _ H ^ intrinsic = 2.69 [ 2.21-3.22 ] 10^ 21 cm^ 2 .

gmw_00001610.name: IRAS20069+5929
gmw_00001610.overview: IRAS20069+5929 is a galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0407585]. It contains a bright, variable X-ray point source detected by Chandra [arXiv:astro-ph/0407585].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS20069+5929 as the brightest Chandra source in the composite sample, consistent with a point source and variable within the observation.

quotes.quote: We find consistency only for IRAS20069+5929.

We show in Figure lc the non constant light curve of source IRAS20069+5929, which has the largest statistics of the 4 Chandra observations. We highlight this variability detection since it indicates the presence of a point source even if the average luminosity of this source has not changed since RASS.
We plot the histograms in Figure psf for the brightest source IRAS20069+5929 and verify that it is consistent with a point source.

gmw_00001611.name: PKS 0548-322
gmw_00001611.overview: PKS 0548-322 is a high-frequency-peaked BL Lacertae object [arXiv:astro-ph/0610416, arXiv:1006.5289] hosted by a giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0610416, arXiv:1005.4475]. This host galaxy is the dominant member of a rich cluster [arXiv:1005.4475, arXiv:1407.7615] and is in close interaction with a companion galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0103343].

sources.summary: The paper observes PKS 0548 322/BZB J0550 3216 as a high-energy-peaked BL Lac hosted by a giant elliptical, finding bright X-ray flux and a curved synchrotron spectrum peaking near 2 keV.

quotes.quote: We observed the high energy peaked BL Lac object (BZB J0550 3216) with to study the temporal and spectral properties of its synchrotron emission simultaneously in the Optical, Ultraviolet and X-ray energy bands. We carried out a spectral analysis of 5 XRT and UVOT observations of over the period April - June 2005. The X-ray flux of this BL Lac source was found to be approximately constant at a level of F_ (2-10 keV ) 4 10^ 11 , a factor of 2 brighter than when observed by in 1999 and close to the maximum intensity reported in the Slew Survey. The very good statistics obtained in the 0.3-10 keV X-ray spectrum allowed us to detect highly significant deviations from a simple power law spectral distribution. A log-parabolic model describes well the X-ray data and gives a best fit curvature parameter of 0.18 and a peak energy in the Spectral Energy Distribution of about 2 keV.

( z =0.069, ), also named BZB J0550 3216 in the recent Multifrequency Catalogue of Blazars ( ), is a remarkable BL Lac object characterized by a relatively strong and fast variability in the X-ray energy band ( ). It is hosted in a giant elliptical galaxy (Falomo et al. 1995, ) which is the dominant member of a rich cluster of galaxies. The synchrotron power peaks in the X-ray band and for this reason it is classified as an HBL source ( ).

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 0548 322 as a nearby BL Lac in a dominant giant elliptical host, notes strong X-ray variability, and finds its XMM light curve consistent with broad-band white noise.

quotes.quote: PKS 0548 322 PKS 0548 322 is a nearby BL Lac object and is hosted in a giant elliptical galaxy (Falomo Pesce Treves 1995; Wurtz et al. \ 1996) which is the dominant member of a rich cluster of galaxies.
PKS 0548 322 is a relatively strong and rapidly variable source in the X-ray energy bands (Blustin et al. \ 2004).

This blazar PKS 0548 322 was continuously monitored by XMM-Newton for 45.5 ks on October 11, 2002 and the rather flat LC we extracted from this data is given in the first panel in Fig. \ 1. The SF we produced from this observation is plotted as the middle panel in the first row of Fig. \ 1. This SF plot shows a continuous rising trend as the temporal lag increases. Therefore any variability timescale that might have been present at that time is larger than the length of the data set. The power spectrum analysis of our single data set for PKS 0548 322 revealed the variability pattern to be in a good agreement with a model dominated by a broad-band white noise process, i.e., P(f) f^ 0 .

sources.summary: The review presents PKS 0548-441 as a BL Lac in a rich cluster, where spectroscopy shows the host is the dominant cluster member at the source redshift.

quotes.quote: A interesting case of rich environment is found for the radio source PKS 0548-441. This target was among the first BLLs to be pointed out as likely connected with a cluster of galaxies at z 0.04 disney74a . The claim was, however, soon disproved as the redshift of the source (z = 0.069) was derived fosbury76 . This nearby object was deeply investigated by who obtained both high quality images and spectroscopy of galaxies in the field .

It turned out that the source is indeed located in a rich cluster of galaxies (see Fig. fig:p0548 ) of which the BLL host galaxy is clearly the dominant member with an absolute magnitude M _R = --23.5. The physical association of the cluster was proved by spectroscopy of many galaxies in the field that have the same redshift as the BLL source. This is a rare case of BLL object associated with a relatively rich (Abell class 2) cluster of galaxies while most of the BLLs reside in poorer environments.

sources.summary: The paper presents a long XMM-Newton observation of the BL-Lac object PKS 0548-322 at z=0.069, noting its cluster environment, strong brightness, and an RGS absorption feature best interpreted as intervening OVI.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present observations of the BL-Lac object PKS 0548-322 at z=0.069 Fosbury76 . Prior to that Disney74 had detected the presence of a group of galaxies at z=0.04 around this object, suggesting that PKS 0548-322 might be a cluster member. Much more recent work by Falomo95 found that there is indeed a cluster (A S549) around this BL-Lac object, but at its average redshift of z=0.069 . X-ray emission from this cluster is very complicated to detect and measure given the brightness of PKS 0548-322.

The XMM-Newton RGS1 spectrum of PKS 0548-322 ( z=0.069 ) shows a single absorption feature, formally significant at the 3.7 level. Monte Carlo simulations show that this significance is probably lower, but in any case higher than 2 . Intervening OVI K absorption at z=0.058 is the most likely interpretation of the absorption line.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0548--322 as having a broken-power-law X-ray spectrum, excess absorption plausibly tied to its interacting BL Lac environment, and PDS detection to 30 keV.

quotes.quote: For the third observation the single power--law model fits the data very well; instead, the first observation is significantly better fitted by a broken power--law (F--test >99.8 ), with a break around 4 keV. In both cases, the data require a column density in excess of the galactic one, with high significance (F--tests >99.9 and >99 respectively, see Table 2 and 3). A column density higher than galactic seems also suggested by the fit with the ``curved" model: the fit with a galactic N_ H provides low energies spectral indices slightly flatter than expected from a peak around 3--4 keV ( _1=0.4 at 0.5 keV). More likely values are obtained with N_ H around 3.6--4.5 10 ^ 20 cm ^ 2 ( _1=0.7-0.8 ). Interestingly, this extra absorption could be associated with the BL Lac environment.

In fact, PKS 0548--322 belongs to a rich cluster of galaxies, and has two companions, one of which is seen in close interaction with the host galaxy of the BL Lac object (from NTT images, see Falomo et al. 1995).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PKS 0548-322 as one of five well-sampled TeV HBLs, finding narrower peak-energy and luminosity variation than Mrk 421 but similar spectral-parameter trends.

quotes.quote: To search for trends, one needs at least 10 observations with E_p , S_p , b well estimated, a requirement satisfied by only 5 of our sources, namely PKS 0548-322, 1H 1426+418, MRK 501, 1ES 1959+650 and PKS 2155-304. For these sources we perform spectral analyses to evaluate independently E_p , S_p , b , to which we apply the redshift corrections discussed above.

Figure 1 shows the results for PKS 0548-322; it is worth noting that in this source both E_p^* and L_p^* vary in a narrower range compared to Mrk 421. PKS 0548-322 follows the same trend of Mrk 421 on both the E_p^*-L_p^* and E_p^*-b planes, with correlation coefficients r_ log =0.61 and r_ log =-0.60 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reports the first VHE gamma-ray detection of PKS 0548 322, a nearby high-frequency-peaked BL Lac hosted by a giant elliptical galaxy in a rich cluster.

quotes.quote: PKS 0548 322 ( z=0.069 ) is a ``high-frequency-peaked'' BL Lac object and a candidate very high energy (VHE, E >100 GeV) ray emitter, due to its high X-ray and radio flux. PKS 0548 322 is detected for the first time in the VHE band with H.E.S.S.

PKS 0548 322 is a nearby ( z=0.069 , Fosb76 ) and bright BL Lac object hosted by a giant elliptical galaxy of absolute visual magnitude M_V=-23.4 Falo95,Wurt96 which is the dominant member of a rich cluster of galaxies. Observations performed by H.E.S.S. \ from 2004 up to 2008 have established PKS 0548 322 as a VHE ray source amongst the closest blazars.

sources.summary: The paper includes PKS 0548-322 as an HBL confirmed by HESS, with Swift UV/X-ray observations, Fermi/LAT non-detection, and no significant HESS or Swift variability.

quotes.quote: PKS 0548-322. A HBL object was observed between October 2004 and January 2008 with the HESS, and confirmed to be a TeV source (Superina et al. 2008, Aharonian et al. 2010). The integral flux above 200 GeV is 1.3 of the Crab flux and the observed spectrum is characterized by a power-law with a photon index 2.86 . Contemporaneous UV and X-ray observations in November 2006 were made by Swift, but it was not be detected by Fermi /LAT (Tavecchio et al. 2010b). No significant variability was detected by HESS and Swift.
