quotes.quote: Notes on F583-1: Comparison and colours Using the procedures described in BHB95 we have determined the radial luminosity and colour profiles of F583-1. These are presented in Fig. A4. The leftmost panels show the radial profiles in B , R and I .

Comparison with other galaxies from BHB95 shows that this galaxy is a typical LSB galaxy in all respects. The colours (shown in top-right panel of Fig. A4) are also quite normal for LSB galaxies. We find that in F583-1 the average (area weighted) value for B-R is 0.95, which is slightly redder than the typical value of the sample of BHB95, but still well within the range covered by LSB galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes F583-1 as well resolved and strongly favoring a constant-density core over an NFW cusp, with beam smearing no longer viable as an explanation.

quotes.quote: F583-1 A well resolved and well-observed curve that shows the NFW over/under/over-fit discrepancy. For all assumptions about stellar mass, ^2_ ISO ^2_ NFW . This galaxy strongly prefers a halo with a constant density core over one with a cusp, a conclusion which has not changed from mcg_nodm98 . Only a substantial change in the shape of the rotation curve would alter this conclusions, which would require a large systematic error. Beam smearing can no longer be invoked as the cause of such a systematic error now that this object has been resolved to sub-kpc scales.

gmw_00001637.name: NGC 488
gmw_00001637.overview: NGC 488 is a nearby, moderately inclined early-type Sb spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0509708, arXiv:9910288]. It features a regular, tightly wound two-armed spiral structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0211538] and exhibits a high rotation velocity [arXiv:astro-ph/0509708, arXiv:astro-ph/9702128].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 488 as a nearby Sb spiral with a high-velocity disk, tightly wound two-armed structure, and stability behavior largely unaffected by rotation-curve choice.

quotes.quote: NGC 488 is a nearby Sb galaxy with an inclination of about 40 (Gerssen et al. 1997). With Hubble constant of 75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , the distance to NGC 488 is about 30 Mpc. Sandage Bedke (1994) describe this galaxy as a prototype of the multi-armed spiral galaxies. Hubble Space Telescope observations (Carollo et al. 1997, Sil'chenko 1999) reveal, however, a tightly wound two-armed spiral pattern shown on the left panel of Figure 5.

NGC 488 is well studied kinematically. Peterson (1980) measured the rotation curve of this galaxy using emission line spectra and Gerssen et al. (1997) have measured the disk rotation using stellar absorption-line data. NGC 488 has a remarkably high rotation velocity with a maximum value of about 360 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 488 to measure disk velocity-ellipsoid components, finding a high vertical-to-radial dispersion ratio and relatively limited spiral-structure heating.

quotes.quote: On applying the method to observations of the Sb galaxy NGC 488, we find evidence for a higher ratio of vertical to radial dispersion in NGC 488 than in the solar neighbourhood of the Milky Way (the only other place where this quantity has ever been measured). The difference is qualitatively consistent with the notion that spiral structure has been relatively less important in the dynamical evolution of the disk of NGC 488 than molecular clouds.

We choose the large early type spiral NGC 488 for this analysis because of its regular optical appearance and its intermediate inclination. Table 1 lists some properties of this galaxy. Note the very high rotational velocity (Peterson 1980). B and I band images of NGC 488 were obtained with the 48 inch telescope at Mt. \ Hopkins observatory in September 1992. Bulge-disk decompositions were performed on these images to assess the extent to which the bulge contaminated the velocity dispersions of the disk.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 488 as an early-type Sb galaxy whose tightly wound two-armed spiral is governed by the most unstable two-armed disk mode.

quotes.quote: NGC 488. Upper left panel of Figure 1 shows the theoretical spiral pattern superimposed onto the HST B-band image of this galaxy after subtracting the bulge contribution to the surface brightness distribution. The early-type Sb galaxy NGC 488 has a regular tightly wound two-armed spiral structure which can be traced close to the central region. The theoretical modal analysis and nonlinear simulations show that the dynamics of the disk in NGC 488 is governed by the most unstable two-armed spiral. The theoretical spiral pattern is, however, more open compared to the observed spiral, especially in the central regions of the disk.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC488 as the only external direct velocity-ellipsoid comparison, noting its inclination, giant Sb morphology, scale length, rotation speed, and axis ratio.

quotes.quote: The only other galaxy in which a direct measurement of the velocity ellipsoid has been reported, is NGC488 (Gerssen et al. 1997). NGC488 is a moderately inclined galaxy, which enables these authors to solve for the dispersions from a comparison of measurements along the major and minor axes. NGC488 is a giant Sb galaxy with a photometric scale length of about 6 kpc (in the B band) and an amplitude of the rotation curve of about 330 km s ^ 1 . The axis ratio _ z / _ R is 0.70 0.19; this value, which is probably larger than that for the Galaxy, suggests that the spiral irregularities mechanism should be relatively less important, in agreement with the optical morphology.

gmw_00001638.name: HIJASS J1021+68
gmw_00001638.overview: HIJASS J1021+68 is a complex of gas clouds located west of the galaxy IC 2574 [arXiv:1903.03767, arXiv:2002.03813]. It has been identified as a candidate optically dark or tidal dwarf galaxy, though it lacks a stellar counterpart and may instead be stripped gas [arXiv:1903.03767, arXiv:2002.03813].

sources.summary: HIJASS J1021+68 is treated as an optically dark/tidal-dwarf candidate west of IC 2574, embedded in H I clouds that may have originated in IC 2574.

quotes.quote: Moreover, the existence of an ``optically dark'' galaxy -- HIJASS J1021+68 -- located west of IC 2574 has been reported in the recent blind \ HIJASS survey \ Jodrell All-Sky Survey, Boyce2001 . This hints at the existence of more low density clouds in the space between the M81 system and IC 2574, and a possible \ connection of the two entities through discrete clouds.

The derived \ mass of HIJASS J1021+68 is (3.5 0.7) 7 , in agreement with the quoted HIJASS mass of 3 7 . For the complex of small \ clouds lying east of the object, which seem to form a ``filament'' towards IC 2574, we measured a total \ mass of (1.3 0.5) 7 . The northern cloud of HIJASS J1021+68, which seems to connect to the dark galaxy, has an \ mass of (1.6 0.6) 7 , and the cloud south to the galaxy has a mass of (0.1 0.2) 7 .

In fact, Boyce2001 hinted that HIJASS J1021+68 is a possible tidal dwarf galaxy in which star formation has not begun. The present observations (bottom panel of fig:nhimap_drao ) revealed additional clouds in the neighbourhood of the HIJASS J1021+68, and an \ envelope of IC 2574 that seems to point towards the tidal dwarf. These new features suggest that HIJASS J1021+68, as well as the neighbouring clouds, may in fact be a complex of \ gas that was initially in IC2574.

sources.summary: The paper resolves HIJASS J1021+68 into gas clumps, finds no stellar counterpart, and argues it is probably stripped or infalling H I rather than a dark galaxy.

quotes.quote: Located at approximately 54 kpc west of IC 2574, HIJASS J1021+68 is a complex of gas clouds previously identified as a dark galaxy in the HIJASS catalogue Boyce2001 . The complex is not much studied in the literature, and no stellar counterpart has been reported. fig:ic_hijass_nhi shows an \ map of the region containing IC 2574 and the gas complex. HIJASS J1021+68 appears to be a complex of two major clouds (denoted CC and EC in fig:m81 ) and two minor clouds (SC and WC), with the central cloud (CC) being the largest component.

On the nature of HIJASS J1021+68, two possibilities arise: the object is either just a complex of gas clouds, or it is a dark galaxy in which star formation has not begun yet. The first hypothesis is obvious, as the system shows no stellar counterpart, and appears to be fragmented in different clouds. However, a few observational facts argue in favour of the dark galaxy nature of the object: the apparent gradient in the velocity field and the high velocity dispersion (up to 20-25 ) in the atomic gas, and the similarity of the object with the low-mass dwarf irregular galaxy GR8 Carignan1990 . In fact, the \ kinematics of HIJASS J1021+68 present the same characteristics as those of GR8, whose gravitational support is essentially provided by rotation only in the inner regions, while it is dominated by random motions in the outer parts.

gmw_00001639.name: IC 171
gmw_00001639.overview: IC 171 is an elliptical galaxy that exhibits an observed isophotal twist, suggesting its structure is not exactly axisymmetric [arXiv:1206.5768]. If the galaxy is triaxial, its gas may be rotating around its long axis [arXiv:1206.5768].

sources.summary: The paper finds IC 171 is probably non-axisymmetric, with gas rotation better explained by long-axis rotation and only marginal evidence for a distinct dark halo.

quotes.quote: IC 171. The observed isophotal twist (Fig. fig:photometry ) suggests that IC 171 is not exactly axisymmetric, similarly to NGC 708. Moreover, v_ rot do not match the dynamical v_ circ for any within the observed range. If IC 171 is triaxial, the gas might also be rotating around the long axis of the galaxy.

A = 13^ brings v_ rot in agreement with v_ circ . If the gas rotates around the long axis of the galaxy, then the line of nodes of the gaseous disk is at = 103^ . This value is consistent with the orientation of the galaxy isophotes at the corresponding deprojected radii ( 3 kpc, Fig. fig:photometry ).
For IC 171 the evidence for > 0 is about 2 and the dark halo outweighs the mass following the light beyond 2.9 , which is significantly on the far side of r_ max .

gmw_00001640.name: NGC 679
gmw_00001640.overview: NGC 679 is an elliptical galaxy that hosts a nearly face-on dust ring in its nucleus [arXiv:1206.5768]. This nuclear dust ring has a radius of approximately 1.5 kiloparsecs, and dynamical modeling of the galaxy requires a dark matter halo [arXiv:1206.5768].

sources.summary: The paper reports HST imaging of NGC 679, a nearly face-on nuclear dust ring, and dynamical modeling requiring a significant dark halo.

quotes.quote: As part of the HST Snapshot Proposal 10884 (P.I. G. A. Wegner), the galaxies NGC 679, NGC 687, NGC 708, NGC 759, and UGC 1308 were observed with Wide Field Planetary Camera 2 (WFPC2) on board the HST between 2007 July 14 and 2008 September 27.
NGC 679. The nucleus of the galaxy hosts a nearly face-on ring of dust. It has a radius of 4 5 (1.5 kpc).

The kinematics of NGC 679, NGC 687, and NGC 759 cannot be fit without > 0 . The statistical significance is at least 3 and the dark halos dominate beyond 1.7 , 1.4 , and 1.0 , respectively (Fig. fig:rotation ).

gmw_00001641.name: NGC 687
gmw_00001641.overview: NGC 687 is an early-type galaxy [arXiv:2202.08593] that lacks detectable emission lines [arXiv:1206.5768] and nuclear dust features [arXiv:1206.5768]. Its radio emission is diffuse and lacks a central compact core [arXiv:2202.08593], while its kinematics require a dark matter halo to fit the data [arXiv:1206.5768].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 0687 as a diffuse, coreless LOFAR radio source with steep spectrum, grouped among extended giant early-type galaxies with diffuse large-scale emission.

quotes.quote: In two objects (NGC 0687 and NGC 2672) the radio emission, extending over 40 ( 15 kpc) and 105 ( 30 kpc), respectively, is diffuse and lacks of any well defined radio core. Seven of them show a very steep spectral slope ( _ 150 ^ 1400 > 1.2 , namely NGC 0080, NGC 0507, NGC 0687, NGC 0910, NGC 2672, NGC 2832, and NGC 3842), only three of them being detected (NGC 0507, NGC 2672, and NGC 3842) by the surveys at 1.4 GHz. All of them are characterized by rather weak cores, surrounded large-scale emission, often showing a diffuse structure.
NGC 0687 (diffuse). This radio source is diffuse, and it lacks a central compact source. It is not detected at 1.4 GHz, resulting in a steep spectral index ( _ 150 ^ 1400 > 1.23 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 687 as lacking detectable emission lines and nuclear dust features while its kinematics require a significant dark halo.

quotes.quote: In the following we discuss the Abell 262 galaxies individually, except for NGC 679 and NGC 687 which show no detectable emission line.
Only NGC 687 shows no dust features in its nucleus.

The kinematics of NGC 679, NGC 687, and NGC 759 cannot be fit without > 0 . The statistical significance is at least 3 and the dark halos dominate beyond 1.7 , 1.4 , and 1.0 , respectively (Fig. fig:rotation ).

gmw_00001642.name: NGC 703
gmw_00001642.overview: NGC 703 is an elliptical galaxy characterized by stable isophotal position angles across all observed radii [arXiv:1206.5768]. Its photometric and kinematic data are consistent with stellar and gaseous models, showing little to no requirement for a separate dark matter halo component [arXiv:1206.5768].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 703 has stable isophotal position angles, photometric and kinematic consistency with the models, and little need for a separate dark halo.

quotes.quote: NGC 703. The isophotes show little variation in position angle over all the observed radii ( 30^ 50^ , Fig. fig:photometry ). We found and within the scatter of the data after minimizing the ^2 difference between dynamical circular velocity and gas rotation velocity (Fig. fig:rotation ). The photometric and kinematic data are consistent with our assumptions about both the stellar and gaseous components.

For the remaining objects (NGC 703, NGC 708, NGC 712, and UGC 1308) a second mass-component does not improve the fit significantly. In fact, the best-fitting models of NGC 708, NGC 712, and UGC 1308 are obtained without any dark matter in a halo with a different mass distribution than the stars , while the best-fit halo-mass fraction inside in NGC 703 is = 0.001 , its upper limit 0.06 .

gmw_00001643.name: NGC 712
gmw_00001643.overview: NGC 712 is an elliptical galaxy with a mass distribution that closely follows its light [arXiv:1206.5768]. Its emission-line spectrum shows low measured gas rotation velocities, which are consistent with a gaseous disk [arXiv:1206.5768].

sources.summary: The paper reports uncertain gas deprojection for NGC 712, low measured gas rotation, and a mass distribution that follows the light closely.

quotes.quote: NGC 712. The emission-line spectrum was taken close to the galaxy minor-axis, making deprojection of v_ gas highly uncertain. The measured rotation velocities are low (Fig. fig:kinematics ) and, therefore, consistent with a gaseous disk with the same = and = as adopted in the dynamical model of the galaxy.

For the remaining objects (NGC 703, NGC 708, NGC 712, and UGC 1308) a second mass-component does not improve the fit significantly. In fact, the best-fitting models of NGC 708, NGC 712, and UGC 1308 are obtained without any dark matter in a halo with a different mass distribution than the stars , while the best-fit halo-mass fraction inside in NGC 703 is = 0.001 , its upper limit 0.06 .
By contrast, our measurements reach r_ max 1.5 in NGC 712 yet the mass distribution follows the light almost exactly.

gmw_00001644.name: NGC 759
gmw_00001644.overview: NGC 759 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0801.0591] that hosts a compact, star-forming central disk of cold gas and dust [arXiv:0801.0591]. This nuclear dusty disk is nearly face-on [arXiv:1206.5768] and may represent a late-stage merger or accretion event [arXiv:0801.0591].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 759 has a face-on nuclear dusty disk, regular gas kinematics, model-consistent shape, and highly significant halo-mass evidence.

quotes.quote: NGC 759. The galaxy has a nuclear dusty disk which is close to being face-on. It has a radius of 5 0 (1.7 kpc) and it is characterized by tightly wound multiple spiral arms.

NGC 759. The position angle of the isophotes of NGC 759 ranges between about 10^ and 50^ beyond radii of 0.1 kpc (Fig. fig:photometry ). The gas kinematics was measured along three different axes and has a regular and symmetric rotation curve and velocity dispersion profile (Fig. fig:kinematics ). We found and (Fig. fig:rotation ) confirming the orientation and intrinsic shape obtained from the stellar dynamics.
For example, the presence of halo mass in NGC 759 is highly significant, even though r_ max < .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 759 as a late-merger/accretion elliptical with a compact star-forming central cold gaseous disk, unresolved submillimeter dust emission, and no detected H I.

quotes.quote: NGC 083 and NGC 759 are elliptical galaxies with surface brightness profiles that are fit by the ^ 1/4 law e.g. you05, wik97 . Using deep CCD V and R images, you05 confirmed in detail that NGC 83 is classically fit by the ^ 1/4 law from about radii 6'' to 80'' and that within about radii 6'' it has a regular-shaped cold gaseous dust disk. NGC 759 is also an elliptical galaxy in which wik97 earlier showed that there is a compact, star-forming, central cold gaseous disk Consistent with dust emitting components that are likely co-incident with the compact gaseous disk detections in NGC 83 you05 and NGC 759 wik97 , both these galaxies are not resolved in the submm images presented in Figures fig:ngc83 and fig:ngc759 .

The central disks in NGC 083 and NGC 759 could represent a late stage in the merging of two disk galaxies or accretion of gas-rich material. The gas could have lost momentum in the merging process (or since the accretion) and fallen to the center of the galaxy.

gmw_00001645.name: UGC 1308
gmw_00001645.overview: UGC 1308 is a round, weakly rotating elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1206.5768]. Its nucleus features an asymmetric, inclined dust disk that is stronger on the western side [arXiv:1206.5768].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 1308 as a round, weakly rotating galaxy with an asymmetric inclined nuclear dust disk and no significant need for a separate dark halo.

quotes.quote: UGC 1308. The galaxy nucleus appears to have an inclined dust disk. The projected elliptical shape has a major axis of 3 2 (1.1 kpc) across in the North-South direction and minor axis 2 6 (0.9 kpc). It is asymmetric in that it is stronger on the West side and is not present on the East side.

UGC 1308. The orientation of the isophotes scatters significantly within 2 , but it is constant at larger radii. For the dynamical model we adopted =110^ , which is representative of the orientation of the major axis of the isophotes at 2 kpc (Fig. fig:photometry ).

For example, UGC 1308 is round and shows almost no rotation, whereas NGC 712 is a moderately flattened, strong rotator and the Coma galaxy GMP 1990 is a highly-flattened ( _ max 0.6 ) fast rotating system as well.

gmw_00001646.name: NGC 4424
gmw_00001646.overview: NGC 4424 is a peculiar spiral galaxy in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:0803.3638, arXiv:1609.03896] that is classified as an H II galaxy [arXiv:1304.1279]. It is a merger remnant undergoing ram pressure stripping [arXiv:1810.09234], which has produced a long neutral hydrogen tail [arXiv:1609.03896, arXiv:1810.09234] and quenched star formation in its outer disc [arXiv:1810.09234].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4424 as a Virgo truncated/compact low-rotator with a stellar-kinematics distance far beyond the HI estimate, linking its low gas velocity to environmental disturbance.

quotes.quote: Following the use of the star formation classes introduced by Koopmann Kenney (2004), we can group our sample galaxies as follow Normal galaxies: NGC 4651. Truncated/Normal galaxies: NGC 4351, NGC 4569, NGC 4580, and NGC 4694. Truncated/Compact galaxies: NGC 4064, NGC 4424, and NGC 4606. Anemic or Truncated/Anemic galaxies: NGC 4293, NGC 4429, and NGC 4450.

NGC 4064 and NGC 4424 are particularly interesting because both galaxies have been regarded as possible foreground galaxies. Moreover, both galaxies, together with NGC 4694, belong to the class of Rubin's ``low rotator" galaxies, although for the case of NGC 4064 Rubin's H velocities are much smaller than our ionized gas velocity measurements (Cort es 2006). Solanes's distance estimations indicate a distance of 9.8 0.4 Mpc for NGC 4064, and 4.1 0.2 Mpc for NGC 4424 1 errors in stark contrast with our estimations of 18.0 _ 0.8 ^ 1.3 Mpc, and 15.2 1.9 Mpc respectively. The discrepancies are of a factor 1.8 for NGC 4064 and 3.7 for NGC 4424, which are statistically significant. In the case of the other ``low rotator", NGC 4694, we determine a distance of 13.4 _ 1.0 ^ 1.3 Mpc.

Clearly, NGC 4064 and NGC 4424 belong to the Virgo cluster. NGC 4694 might be affected by the dark matter bias in our models, so its distance should be considered a lower limit. All these galaxies present truncated H disks (Koopmann Kenney 2004), and their gas kinematics are peculiar (see Cort es 2006), indicating that they are heavily disturbed by the environment. They all show evidence for strong gravitational interactions or mergers, which may account for their low gas velocities.

sources.summary: The paper evaluates NGC 4424 as a possible stripped parent of the Virgo HI cloud complex, noting its disturbed morphology, velocity, location, HI deficiency, and incompatible tail direction.

quotes.quote: The SBa galaxy NGC 4424 (also known as UGC 7561 and VCC 9079) is located at =12^ h 27^ m 11.6^ s , =+09^ 25^ 14^ (J2000). This places NGC 4424 at a projected distance of 236 kpc from the cloud center, and 900 kpc from M87. This peculiar galaxy is at a velocity ( cz_ =437 ), near that of the main cloud. The angular size of the stellar component, 3^ .6 1^ .8 , translates to a linear size of 17 9 kpc at the Virgo distance. A detailed study by Cortes (2006) characterizes it as having disturbed morphology; they also suggest that CO observations are indicative of non--circular gas motions and discuss convincingly the vulnerability of the galaxy to ram pressure stripping (see also Kenney 1996).

Those figures indicate that NGC 4424 has lost between 80 and 90 of its HI gas. Chung (2007) noted that NGC 4424 exhibits a one--sided tail extending to the southeast, which is also detected by ALFALFA, and that the HI disk is truncated at a radius smaller than that of the stellar component of the galaxy. The direction of the tail shows the southeast to northwest direction of motion for NGC 4424; this motion is not compatible with the position of the cloud complex.

sources.summary: The paper maps a 60 kpc HI tail stripped from NGC 4424, arguing the peculiar post-merger spiral is undergoing ram-pressure stripping in Virgo, possibly aided by earlier tidal interaction.

quotes.quote: Out to an unprecedented extent, we detect an tail of 60 kpc being stripped off NGC 4424, a peculiar spiral galaxy. The properties of the galaxy, together with the shape of the tail, suggest that NGC 4424 is a post-merger galaxy undergoing a ram pressure stripping as it falls towards the centre of the Virgo Cluster.

Case of NGC 4424 Several authors argued that the tail seen in NGC 4424 is a mixed effect of RP and tidal interaction Kenney1996, Cortes2006, Chung2007, Chung2009 . The arguments for tidal interaction are mainly based on the optical morphology of the galaxy, classified as a chaotic early spiral in the Uppsala General Catalogue UGC, Nilson1973 , a peculiar Sa by Binggeli1985 , and an uncertain barred Sa by DeVaucouleurs1991 . The H morphology of the galaxy has been described as truncated/compact by Koopmann2004a and optical observations reveal that it has a heavily disturbed stellar disk, and shows a strong H emission confined to its central kpc Kenney1996, Cortes2006 . However, the present observations reveal an tail of NGC 4424 three times longer than previously thought.

This type of process might explain the single tail of NGC 4424, and the galaxy could have collided with any massive galaxy in the Virgo cluster. Chung2007 compared the RP to the restoring force in tail'ed Virgo galaxies using the simulations of Vollmer2001 and found that for NGC 4424, RP due to the observed hot X-ray emitting gas could exceed the restoring force, meaning that RP is likely at the origin of the tail.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4424 as a Virgo merger remnant undergoing ram pressure stripping, with quenched outer-disc star formation and an ionised-gas tail driven by a central starburst outflow.

quotes.quote: We have observed the late-type peculiar galaxy NGC 4424 during the Virgo Environmental Survey Tracing Galaxy Evolution (VESTIGE), a blind narrow-band H + [ NII ] imaging survey of the Virgo cluster carried out with MegaCam at the Canada French Hawaii Telescope (CFHT). The presence of a 110 kpc long (in projected distance) HI tail in the southern direction indicates that this galaxy is undergoing a ram pressure stripping event. The deep narrow-band image revealed the presence of a low surface brightness ( (H ) 4 10 ^ 18 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 ) ionised gas tail 10 kpc long extending from the centre of the galaxy to the north-west direction, thus in the direction opposite to the HI tail. Chandra and XMM X-rays data do not show any compact source in the nucleus nor the presence of an extended tail of hot gas, while IFU spectroscopy (MUSE) indicates that the gas is photo-ionised in the inner regions and shock-ionised in the outer parts. Medium- (MUSE) and high-resolution (Fabry-Perot) IFU spectroscopy confirms that the ionised gas is kinematically decoupled from the stellar component and indicates the presence of two kinematically distinct structures in the stellar disc.

All this observational evidence suggests that NGC 4424 is the remnant of an unequal-mass merger occurred 500 Myr ago, when the galaxy was already a member of the Virgo cluster, now undergoing a ram pressure stripping event which has removed the gas and quenched the activity of star formation in the outer disc. The tail of ionised gas probably results from the outflow produced by a central starburst fed by the collapse of gas induced by the merging episode. This outflow is sufficiently powerful to overcome the ram pressure induced by the intracluster medium on the disc of the galaxy crossing the cluster.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4424 as a disturbed HI-deficient Virgo galaxy whose shells, weak rotation, central gas bar, and stripped gas indicate an intermediate-mass merger plus ram pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: NGC 4424 NGC 4424 is a peculiar Sa galaxy located 3.1 (870 kpc projected distance) from M87. It shows a more disturbed morphology than NGC 4064, characterized by unusual heart-shaped inner R isophotes and shell like features (Fig. n4424rgb ), suggesting a merger event (Kenney 1996). It also has H emission confined to the central kpc and one of the highest H surface brightnesses within the Virgo cluster (Koopmann Kenney 2004). H velocities derived by Rubin (1999) are extremely low ( 40 ). Its HI deficiency is also high ( 1.0) (Helou etal 1984; Kenney Young 1989).

With its heavily disturbed stellar morphology, small V/ , disturbed dust lanes, central gas bar, and odd core gas kinematics, NGC 4424 is very likely an intermediate-mass merger. The lack of outer disk gas is likely caused by ram pressure stripping, which would be particularly effective on gas driven outwards by the merger, and which might plausibly occur at NGC 4424's present location in the cluster, at 0.5 virial radii.

sources.summary: ALFALFA finds NGC 4424 near a transient HI cloud complex, arguing its HI deficiency and environment-driven stripping likely connect it to the clouds.

quotes.quote: The nearest optical galaxy with a velocity near that of the complex is NGC 4424, located some 40' W of the complex center. An optical image of NGC 4424 is shown in figure complex , at its approximate sky location with respect to the cloud complex. NGC 4424 has heliocentric velocity of 441 .

The HI mass of NGC 4424 is 1.7 10^8 . Given its HI mass and optical size, NGC 4424 is very HI deficient ( Def 1 , corresponding to having lost most of its cold gas ). The HI deficiency and other properties of NGC 4424 are is strongly suggestive of environment--driven mechanisms at work, and likelihood of association with the cloud complex.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4424 as an unbarred spiral with centrally confined non-zero minor-axis gas velocities and strong non-circular or off-plane motions.

quotes.quote: In NGC 4424 and NGC 4941, we measured non-zero gas velocities only in the central regions along the minor axis and gas velocities drop to zero at larger radii.

The non-zero gas velocities are confined in the central regions along the minor axis of NGC 4424 and NGC 4941, where the gas velocity curve shows a steep gradient. The gas velocity increases to a maximum of 10 \ in the inner few arcsec, and further out it decreases to zero. Our measurements along the minor axis of NGC 4424 are in agreement within errors with those by Kenney et al. (1996) which are characterized by a lower spatial sampling.

In spite of the morphological classification in RC3, NGC 4424 is an unbarred galaxy. Kenney et al. (1996) first pointed out the presence of strong non-circular gas motions and/or rotation in a plane different from that of the outer stellar disk and attributed to a recent merger.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4424 as an H II galaxy with disturbed stellar structure, central star formation, a southern H I tail, and radio polarization morphology indicating a central outflow.

quotes.quote: NGC 4424 NGC 4424 is classified as an H ii galaxy. It has a strongly disturbed stellar disk, with banana-shaped isophotes and shells (Cort es et al. 2006). There is strong star formation in a bar-like string of H ii complexes in the central 1 kpc, and no star formation beyond (Kenney et al. 1996). Its stellar rotation velocities ( 30 km s ^ 1 ) are extremely modest. A prominent H i tail extends to the south (Chung et al. 2007, 2009).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4424 as SN 2012cg's nearby host and models its complex two-component light distribution; diffuse host X-rays and galaxy light limit progenitor constraints.

quotes.quote: The Lick Observatory Supernova Search (LOSS; filippenko01 ) discovered the nearby SN 2012cg in NGC 4424 ( z = 0.001458 + 0.000007 ; kent08 ) on 2012 May 17.2 UT (MJD = 56064.22) and quickly announced it to the community cenko12ATEL .

Instead, we used GALFIT peng02 to model the large-scale, 2-D light distribution of the galaxy. A nearby star served as a PSF model for the Sersic fit. Neighboring objects were also modeled and simultaneously fit, with initial guesses for the Sersic parameters obtained from SExtractor. The complex structure of the host galaxy warranted two Sersic components, especially given the large residuals from a single-component fit. Indeed, both components provided extended fits to the galaxy light distribution.
Unfortunately, unresolved diffuse X-ray emission from the host galaxy prevents us from obtaining a deeper limit.

sources.summary: The paper uses HST ACS/WFC halo imaging to derive a first TRGB distance for NGC 4424, tying the result to the LMC zero-point and comparing with Cepheid distances.

quotes.quote: We present here deep Hubble Space Telescope ( ) ACS/WFC imaging of the resolved stellar populations in the metal-poor halos of the host galaxies , , and . These three Virgo constellation galaxies are prime targets for calibrating the extragalactic distance scale given their relative proximity in the local Universe and their low line-of-sight reddenings. Anchoring the TRGB zero-point to the geometric distance to the Large Magellanic Cloud via detached eclipsing binaries, we measure extinction-corrected distance moduli of _ stat _ sys mag, _ stat _ sys mag, and _ stat _ sys mag for , , and , respectively, or _ stat _ sys Mpc, _ stat _ sys Mpc, and _ stat _ sys Mpc.

For these three galaxies, the distances are the first based on the TRGB, and for and , they are the highest precision distances published to date, each measured to 3 . Finally, we report good agreement between our TRGB distances and the available Cepheid distances for and , demonstrating consistency between the distance scales currently derived from stars of Population I and II. stars: Population II, cosmology: distance scale, galaxies: individual: NGC 4424, galaxies: individual: NGC 4526, galaxies: individual: NGC 4536

sources.summary: NGC 4424 is one of the sample's most unusual H-morphology galaxies, with compact circumnuclear HII emission, high central H surface brightness, noncircular kinematics, shell-like features, and evidence for a recent minor merger.

quotes.quote: These include two Virgo galaxies with the most unusual H morphologies of our sample: NGC 4424 and NGC 4064, which have compact H emission from several circumnuclear HII complexes, as shown in Figure stt . The H surface brightnesses of these two galaxies within 0.1r _ 24 0.8 kpc are among the highest in the Virgo and isolated samples (see also Figure haclasscomp ), and most of the H emission originates in HII complexes with luminosities of L _ H =5 10 ^ 38 erg s ^ 1 . H major axis spectra show low line-of-sight velocities inconsistent with circular motions in the plane suggested by the outer optical isophotes (Rubin et al. 1999). NGC 4424 has unusual heart-shaped inner R isophotes and shell-like features (Kenney et al. 1996) similar to those seen in simulations of mergers (e.g., Hernquist Quinn 1988; Hernquist 1992, 1993), suggesting that NGC 4424 is the product of a recent minor merging event (Kenney et al. 1996).

sources.summary: NGC4424 is one of two tailed Virgo galaxies with high leading-half molecular-gas excess, driven by an offset clump and likely affected by recent merger or tidal interaction plus ram pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: The two galaxies in Fig. fig:lt_ratio b with _ H_2 > 2 are NGC4424 and NGC4694. In both cases the large value is driven by a single, large clump slightly offset from the galaxy centre on the leading half. There is evidence that both NGC4424 and NGC4694 have experienced a recent mergers and/or tidal interactions in addition to ongoing RPS e.g. cortes2006,lisenfeld2016 , thus the outlier values likely include a contribution from these gravitational interactions in addition to potential gas compression from RPS.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4424 was considered because of proximity and velocity, but its ram-pressure tail is roughly parallel to the BC6 cloud complex rather than aimed toward it.

quotes.quote: Both Kent+2009 and Minchin+2019 focused on NGC 4445 and NGC 4424 as these are only 30 \ to the SW of BC6 and have similar radial velocities to the gas complex 346 \ and 437 , respectively; SDSSDR13,Haynes+2018 . NGC 4424 is in the process of being ram pressure stripped Chung+2009,Sorgho+2017 , but its \ tail points to the SE, roughly parallel to the \ complex containing BC6, rather than towards it. In the case of NGC 4445 the M_ HI -B-band diameter scaling relation of Jones+2018 indicates that its expected original \ content (before falling into the Virgo cluster) would have been M_ HI / M_ = 9.19 0.21 and it still contains M_ HI / M_ = 7.7 ; Haynes+2018 . The observed mass of the \ complex therefore represents almost its entire original \ content. Furthermore, in Sorgho+2017 , figure A1, NGC 4445 also appears to have a ram pressure tail (though much smaller than that of NGC 4424) which is pointing almost directly away from BC6 and the \ cloud complex. Thus, neither of these appear to be a viable candidate for the progenitor system.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4424 as a foreground object with very low inner rotation, likely a merger remnant, and possibly not strongly HI-deficient if reclassified S0-a.

quotes.quote: NGC 4424 The very low inner rotation of this galaxy suggests a merger remnant RWK99 . It almost certainly lies in the foreground of the Virgo cluster. Moreover, as discussed in Sect. trulydef , NGC 4424 may be of type S0/a, in which case it would not be 3 deficient in neutral Hydrogen.
